Escape route blocked by flood; HM's choices collapse

timeline.period_label: April 1852: HM's Last Stand and Death
timeline.primary_location: Thicket Ditch — HM's Final Defensive Position
timeline.narrative_beat: HM and murids make final stand; HM killed and beheaded — the thistle destroyed

production_notes.macro_visual_strategy: The film operates on two irreconcilable visual registers: MOUNTAIN (raw, vertical, textured — Caucasus ridges, low saklya ceilings, firelight, mud, stone, cold air) versus PALACE (horizontal, gilded, upholstered — Winter Palace loft, Tiflis dinner table, chandelier light, white linen). Every cut between these worlds is a moral argument. Hadji Murad moves through both and belongs to neither. The thistle of the prologue — singular, crimson, crushed but still upright — is the visual thesis that both worlds destroy without understanding.
key_visual_motifs: The crimson thistle (prologue and epilogue mirror)
Firelight vs. chandelier light (mountain warmth vs. imperial cold luxury)
Vertical stone drops — the Mansokha abyss, the Vedeno prison pit, the ravine outposts
Snow and blood in the same frame (Avdeev's wounding, HM's final stand)
The open door or gate as threshold between worlds (Shahgirinsk Gate, Tiflis anteroom, Nukha window with minaret)
geographic_anchors: Caucasus Greater Mountain Range (Chechen/Avar highland aoul world — approx. 1500-2500m elevation)
Tiflis / Tbilisi (Vorontsov's Caucasus imperial capital — transition zone between worlds)
St. Petersburg / Winter Palace (absolute imperial centre — the source of all orders)
Nukha / Sheki, Azerbaijan (HM's gilded-cage liminal zone — neither mountain nor court)
production_notes.color_strategy: The mountain world begins in saturated earth tones — ochre, terracotta, firelight amber — and progressively desaturates as Hadji Murad moves deeper into Russian control. The imperial world (Tiflis, Petersburg) is always golden but cold: gilded surfaces that produce no warmth. The final sequence at the thicket ditch returns the palette to raw earth, with the single red of HM's blood echoing the opening thistle — suggesting not defeat but completion of an unbroken moral arc. Nicholas I's Petersburg sequences use a slightly blue-shifted, bleached palette to underscore their moral sterility despite their material splendour.
production_notes.compression_rationale: The 89 raw locations extracted from Tolstoi's novel reflect a panoramic, multi-POV literary structure unsuitable for a 120-minute feature. The consolidated 19 canonical locations serve the film's three-act spine: Act I (HM's flight and surrender — mountain and frontier fort), Act II (negotiation, surveillance, and parallel worlds — Tiflis, Petersburg, Vedeno, Petrovsk, Nukha), Act III (escape attempt and death — road, floodplain, thicket). Dropped locations are either (a) duplicate descriptors of the same physical space, (b) backstory references with no shootable scene, or (c) transitional travel descriptions that serve the novel's pace but not cinema's economy. The compression preserves every major narrative beat while reducing location count by 79%, making the production physically feasible as a single-camera historical feature.

recurring_locations: Caucasus Wilderness — Forest, Ravine & Escape Routes
Mansokha Path — The Legendary Escape (Mountain Abyss)
Winter Palace — St. Petersburg (Nicholas I's Court)
Road Beyond Nukha — The Final Ride
Thicket Ditch — HM's Final Defensive Position

lines.text: Behind us. Twenty riders. Coming fast.
lines.subtext: Threat counted, range estimated, kill order already forming in his head.
lines.action_before: Reins in beside HM, head half-turned to the trail.

lines.text: Agha. My rifle is out.
lines.subtext: I will fire before you have to. Give the word and I take the first three.
lines.action_before: Draws rifle from its cover in one practiced motion.

lines.text: And — they will not pass me.
lines.subtext: If they reach you it is over my body; he is binding his life to HM's escape.
lines.action_before: Settles his hand on the dagger, eyes fixed on the lead rider.

lines.text: As Allah wills, Agha. We ride.
lines.subtext: He believes the next minutes may be his last and is making peace, not piety.
lines.action_before: A short bow of the head toward HM.

lines.text: Stop. Who calls?
lines.subtext: Buying one breath to count rifles, to weigh the dark, to decide if these men die tonight.
lines.action_before: Reins in; hand drops to the pistol at his belt.

lines.text: Eldar. Cover the cover.
lines.subtext: Trust placed without flourish; the murid is the only wall left between him and Shamil's men.
lines.action_before: Half-turns in the saddle, voice low.

lines.text: What do you want? Do you wish to take me? Take me, then.
lines.subtext: Daring them surfaces the cowards; he knows they came to perform, not to die.
lines.action_before: Unbuttons the rifle cover, draws it across the pommel.

lines.text: Sado, go back. You have given bread tonight. Do not give blood also.
lines.subtext: Protecting the host who sheltered him; refusing to drag a friend into Shamil's account-book.
lines.action_before: Speaks without turning, eyes on the riders across the ravine.

lines.text: Allah knows the road. Ride.
lines.subtext: The matter is closed in his head; no more counsel, no more parley — only distance and the river ahead.
lines.action_before: Draws his palm slowly down his beard, then spurs the horse.

lines.text: Listen. The lane bends past the well — go there, not the main road. My house rides with you tonight.
lines.subtext: I am giving you my own footsteps; if they catch you, they catch me first.
lines.action_before: Lays a hand briefly on the horse's flank.

lines.text: Eldar — keep close to his stirrup. A guest is sent by God. He does not ride alone while I draw breath.
lines.subtext: If it comes to blood, you die before he does. That is the order.
lines.action_before: Half-running, eyes fixed on the moving shadows ahead.

lines.text: Those men in the road — they know my name. They will not fire twice. Inshallah, ride.
lines.subtext: I will stand in their path so the second bullet finds me, not him.
lines.action_before: Slows, turning his shoulder toward the blocked path.

lines.text: Go. The water below — follow the water. I have said what needed saying.
lines.subtext: I have already chosen to stay behind. Do not look back for me.
lines.action_before: Stops walking; hand pressed flat to his chest.

lines.text: The horses are fed, Agha. Watered. Saddles checked twice.
lines.subtext: I have already prepared for the ride you have not yet ordered — wherever you go, I am going.
lines.action_before: Stands at the doorway, hand resting on dagger.

lines.text: And — the Russian. He laughs much. I do not laugh with him.
lines.subtext: I do not trust Loris-Melikov. I am watching him for you, even when you smile at him.

lines.text: Khan Mahoma talks. I listen at the door.
lines.subtext: While you tell stories in here, I am guarding the room. That is my place.
lines.action_before: A small bow of the head.

lines.text: Agha — when we ride for the mountains, I ride first. Inshallah.
lines.subtext: If there is a bullet on the road home, it finds me before it finds you. This is decided.
lines.action_before: Resettles hand on the dagger.

lines.text: Loris-Melikov. Sit. Where the forelegs go, the hind legs must follow. So it was with Hamzad's house.
lines.subtext: Naming the listener first as protocol; the proverb is not history, it is a warning about what Shamil will do to my own household if I sit too long among carpets.
lines.action_before: Settles deeper on the divan, hands quiet on his knees.

lines.text: Aseldar struck the Khansha from behind. A man who kills women from behind teaches his master the same hand. Shamil learned it well.
lines.subtext: I am telling you what kind of man holds my son. Move your sardar, or Yusuf goes over a precipice like the Khan's youngest.

lines.text: My brother Osman fired first. I finished it with the dagger. Two boys against thirty swords. It is so.
lines.subtext: Do not mistake my courtesy here for tameness. I have done in a mosque what your regiments have not done in the field.
lines.action_before: A pause; eyes flat on Loris-Melikov.

lines.text: Eldar is young. Made of iron. He has not yet had to bury his own.
lines.subtext: I have. And while I tell stories on this divan my mother and Yusuf are still in Vedeno. Carry that to the sardar.

lines.text: Allah knows the rest. Shall I go on, or has the sardar heard enough for one evening?
lines.subtext: I am closing this door. The next words must come from your side — troops, or I am wasting breath I owe my dead.
lines.action_before: Draws his palms slowly down his beard.

lines.text: Khansha. Be at peace in my house. The Khans rode the wrong road — God closed it behind them.
lines.subtext: She is already sentenced; the hospitality formula seals the verdict.
lines.action_before: Seats himself on the dais of the murdered Khans.

lines.text: Reproach is for women who still have sons. The law of the Prophet does not bargain with a dead house.
lines.subtext: He is naming her as the next to fall while pretending to instruct her.
lines.action_before: Does not look at her; studies the rim of the cup.

lines.text: Aseldar. The hind legs must follow the fore.
lines.subtext: Kill order, delivered as proverb so his own hand stays clean.
lines.action_before: A single small nod toward the murid behind her.

lines.text: The Ghazavat asks one hand, one sword. Whoever stands apart from it stands against God — and I do God's work.
lines.subtext: Warning to every Khan-loyalist still in the room: submit by sundown or share her rafter.
lines.action_before: Rises. Voice flat, weighted, final.

lines.text: Eh — the prince's tea is hot, his bread is white. A man could forget where his saddle is.
lines.subtext: I am watching every door in this house; I have not forgotten anything.
lines.action_before: grins, stretches like a cat by the samovar

lines.text: Frivolous? Allah knows. A frivolous tongue carries the news the serious tongue is too proud to speak.
lines.subtext: Hadji Murad calls me light, but he sends me, not Eldar, when the road is sharp.
lines.action_before: laughs once, short, then quiet

lines.text: Eldar is iron, yes — good. Iron does not bend. But a messenger, brother, must bend twice before breakfast.
lines.subtext: Do not replace me with the boy. The road needs my crooked Russian, not his straight back.
lines.action_before: clicks tongue, taps own chest twice

lines.text: Give me the word, Hadji Murad, and I ride. Bad horse, bad weather — but we are alive, no?
lines.subtext: Send me. Without an errand I am nothing — a tongue without a master.
lines.action_before: rises, already half-turned toward the door

lines.text: Khan Mahoma — a jolly fellow. I have been in to have a talk with thy henchmen.
lines.subtext: I am taking the measure of your men so I know what to write down for the Prince.
lines.action_before: Sits, sets notebook aside, meets Hadji Murad's eye.

lines.text: The young one, Eldar — I liked him. Quiet. He watches the door even while he stands still.
lines.subtext: I notice what a soldier notices; do not mistake my pen for blindness.

lines.text: Permit me — 'where the forelegs have gone, the hind legs must follow.' Say it again, brother. I want it on the page exactly so.
lines.subtext: I will not soften your law into Russian comfort. Let Tiflis read it raw.
lines.action_before: Switches to Tatar, dips the pen.

lines.text: And Hamzad — alive when your brother fired? You finished him on the head. Is that the word — finished?
lines.subtext: I am buying a breath to choose a word that will not betray you when a general reads it.
lines.action_before: Pauses, repeats the clause back.

lines.text: Continue, please. Not for the Prince alone — for the record itself.
lines.subtext: Whatever they decide about you, your truth will outlast the man who files it.
lines.action_before: Lowers his voice, almost to himself.

lines.text: Pour me another, mon cher — the chikhir's the only honest creditor I've got tonight.
lines.subtext: He is dodging the ledger of debts and the burnt aoul his column left behind that morning.
lines.action_before: Pushes his glass forward without meeting Petrov's eye.

lines.text: Did you see them ride out today? Banners, the whole forest moving — c'est la guerre, and by God it's beautiful.
lines.subtext: He is reassuring himself that leaving the Guards for this campaign was not a ruinous mistake.
lines.action_before: Stands too quickly, smile a touch too bright.

lines.text: Marya Dmitrievna — forgive me — a splendid dinner. The major is fortunate. Truly fortunate.
lines.subtext: He is pressing down a desire he will not name, and protecting Petrov by overdoing the courtesy.
lines.action_before: Bows from the waist, hand pressed flat to his tunic.

lines.text: It's beginning to be a long evening. I'll turn in — one needs a clear head for the morning, mon cher.
lines.subtext: He is fleeing the table before the wine loosens what he keeps locked: the boy on the horse, the wailing woman.
lines.action_before: Sets the glass down with deliberate care.

lines.text: Sado. Put down the spade. I will dig with you.
lines.subtext: The debt is mine. I brought the soldiers to your gate by coming here.
lines.action_before: Steps over the broken doorpost; sees the boy.

lines.text: The boy looked at me as if I were already a song. Now he is the silent one, and I am still talking.
lines.subtext: I should have died in his place; my survival is the obscenity here.
lines.action_before: Touches the barka covering the child's face.

lines.text: Tell the mother nothing from me. Words from my mouth would be another wound.
lines.subtext: I know my name on her tongue would taste like the bayonet.
lines.action_before: Glances toward the wailing woman, then away.

lines.text: The bees also. Even the bees. Allah knows what oath I am keeping now.
lines.subtext: My bargain with the Russians has cost more than I priced; the ledger has turned against me.
lines.action_before: Draws his palms slowly down his beard.

lines.text: When the grave is closed, I ride at dawn. My family still waits in Vedeno.
lines.subtext: I cannot stop; if I stop to grieve I will see what I have become.
lines.action_before: Takes the spade from Sado's hands.

lines.text: What does this mean? Who has killed him?
lines.subtext: I am asking because if I name what I see, I will have to feel it.
lines.action_before: Cannot tear his eyes from the head.

lines.text: Cover it, Kamenev. For God's sake — cover it. The moonlight is indecent on a face like that.
lines.subtext: Cover the part of the campaign I have been pretending not to see.
lines.action_before: Takes a half step back, jaw tightening.

lines.text: Only a week ago he gave me his hand. Two fingers. The whip hanging from them. Saubul, he said.
lines.subtext: He was more whole than any of us, and we have hacked him into a sack.
lines.action_before: Quiet, almost to himself; tastes the Tartar word.

lines.text: Well. C'est la guerre, isn't it, mon cher. Tell the major I'll be in directly — I want a moment with the air.
lines.subtext: If I go inside now, I will weep, or drink, or both, and I cannot afford either.
lines.action_before: A small, brittle, self-deprecating laugh.

lines.text: The horse is good. The road is open. A man rides while he can.
lines.subtext: I am leaving tonight; whoever stands in my way will fall.
lines.action_before: tightens the girth, eyes the ridge

lines.text: Yusuf is in Vedeno. The sardar gives me carpets. Allah knows what a father is worth in carpets.
lines.subtext: The Russians have failed me; my son's life is the only currency now.
lines.action_before: draws palms slowly down his beard

lines.text: Tell them Hadji Murad rode out at dawn. Not as a guest. Not as a prisoner.
lines.subtext: If I die, let the manner of it be mine; I refuse the cage.
lines.action_before: lays his hand on the murid's shoulder

lines.text: My mother told me: do not be Khan, do not be killed. Forgive me, mother.
lines.subtext: I have chosen the second; the family must live even if I do not.
lines.action_before: falls silent, looks east

lines.text: It is well. Allah is great.
lines.subtext: The matter is closed; I am ready.
lines.action_before: draws the dagger, steadies his breath

lines.text: Why should I be frightened, Peter Nikolaevich? I have washed worse than what is in your sack.
lines.subtext: Bracing herself; refusing to give him the flinch he wants from a woman.
lines.action_before: Hand on hip, freckles already going hot.

lines.text: Cut-throats. Drunk cut-throats, the lot of you. Take that thing off my step.
lines.subtext: The line has been crossed; she will not pretend otherwise, even in front of an officer.
lines.action_before: Sees the face in the moonlight, goes still.

lines.text: He looked at me like I was a person, batyushka. Not many of you do that.
lines.subtext: She has chosen his side, quietly, and against her own household.
lines.action_before: Quieter, almost to Butler, eyes still on the head.

lines.text: God see them. God see all of you. I am going inside.
lines.subtext: Out of arguments; refusing to keep arguing; grief held flat so it does not break her.
lines.action_before: Turns her back on the Cossack and the sack.

lines.text: Marya Dmitrievna — please. It might happen to anyone out here. That's war, isn't it.
lines.subtext: He is begging her not to make him see what he just saw — and pretending the phrase still means something to him.
lines.action_before: Steps down into the porch, hand half-raised.

lines.text: He gave the major a sword, you know. Only last week. Two fingers and the whip — like that.
lines.subtext: He is grieving the only man who ever made him feel unfraudulent, and cannot say the word grief.
lines.action_before: Small, awkward laugh; looks at his boots.

lines.text: They're drunk in there, Marya Dmitrievna. They don't — they don't mean the grinning. Truly.
lines.subtext: He knows perfectly well they do mean it, and that he belongs to them, not to her.
lines.action_before: Glances back at the door, lowers his voice.

lines.text: Saubul, he taught me. That was the word. Saubul. And I — well. Here we are.
lines.subtext: Tasting the dead man's word like a debt he cannot pay; the soldier's idyll has finally come due.
lines.action_before: Looks past her at the dark steps, jaw tightening.

lines.text: Eldar. Saddle the black. We ride at the third hour — quietly, by the river road.
lines.subtext: The decision is already made; the escape begins tonight, no discussion invited.
lines.action_before: Draws palms slowly down his beard, eyes on the floor.

lines.text: Cossack — a fine carbine. Your father's? Tell me, how many men ride out with us at dawn?
lines.subtext: He is measuring the guard, the weapon, the reach of the man who would stop him.
lines.action_before: Touches the stock of the Cossack's rifle, almost gently.

lines.text: The Sardar promised soldiers. Months pass. In Vedeno my mother counts the days on her fingers.
lines.subtext: Russian hospitality has become his cage; without action his family is already dead.
lines.action_before: Sets down the untouched cup of tea, turns it once on the tray.

lines.text: Yusuf. The boy was small when I last lifted him. Allah knows what hands hold him now.
lines.subtext: Naming the son once is the whole grief; he will not name him again tonight.
lines.action_before: Long stillness, then a half-glance toward the dark window.

lines.text: Hadji Murad has said it. Better to fall on the road than to die clean in a Russian chair. It is well.
lines.subtext: He has chosen the manner of his own death; the door is closed.
lines.action_before: Rises, takes up his shashka, lays his hand flat on the hilt.

lines.text: You're all cut-throats. Cut-throats, and drunk on top of it.
lines.subtext: I have stopped pretending this is war; I am calling it what it is, even at the cost of my place here.
lines.action_before: Stands up from the step, will not look at Butler.

lines.text: A dead man's head belongs in the earth, batyushka, not bumping about in a sack from fort to fort.
lines.subtext: I am still trying to cover for you by calling you batyushka, but do not mistake the softness for permission.
lines.action_before: Freckles flushing red, hand flat against her hip.

lines.text: He looked at me like I was a person. Your major in there has not done that since Easter.
lines.subtext: I have begun to doubt our side of this, and I do not yet know where to put the doubt.
lines.action_before: Voice goes flat, almost toneless.

lines.text: Well, then — God see them, the lot of you. I have soup on the stove and no stomach for any of it.
lines.subtext: I have run out of arguments and I refuse to keep arguing; I will go back to the work that keeps me a person.
lines.action_before: Turns toward the back door, skirt gathered tight.

lines.text: The road bends toward the mountains. Allah knows the rest.
lines.subtext: He has decided. The escort is already dead in his mind; he is closing the door on the Russian chapter.
lines.action_before: Touches up the white Kabarda; eyes the ridgeline.

lines.text: Yusuf. A black horse waits for you. Your father is coming.
lines.subtext: The grief is the brevity. He may not reach the boy alive, and he knows it.
lines.action_before: Right hand finds the pistol grip beneath the burka.

lines.text: Sardar, you kept your carpets. I keep my word.
lines.subtext: Bitter accounting with Vorontsov in absentia; the betrayal lies on the Russian side, not his.
lines.action_before: Hears the roan gelding closing behind him.

lines.text: Mother — forgive. I could not be Khan. I will not be a guest.
lines.subtext: Answering her old song; he chooses the third path she did not name — death on his own ground.
lines.action_before: Slows the Kabarda a half-stride; breath steadies.

lines.text: It is well. Hadji Murad rides home.
lines.subtext: Final closure. 'Home' is not Vedeno and not Tiflis — it is the act itself, the gun, the mountain.
lines.action_before: Draws palm slowly down his beard; thumbs the hammer.

lines.text: Mishkin runs like a dog with news in his teeth. The mare is spent, brother — spent.
lines.subtext: We have lost the race for silence; the alarm will be raised within the hour.
lines.action_before: Reins in beside Hadji Murad, eyes back on the road

lines.text: Pouches full. Powder dry. Enough for a short quarrel — not a long one.
lines.subtext: We cannot fight our way through; whatever you choose, choose fast.
lines.action_before: Slaps the cartridge-pouches taken from the dead

lines.text: Right road climbs to our people. Left road lies. A clever man takes the lying road first.
lines.subtext: I see why you turn away from the mountains — and I will not name it aloud.
lines.action_before: Tilts chin toward the rice-fields, away from the ridges

lines.text: Two smokes, maybe less, and that tower will spit horsemen at us like seeds.
lines.subtext: I am afraid, and I am dressing the fear as a guide's calculation.
lines.action_before: Glances back at the minarets of Benerdzhik

lines.text: I came with you on the road, Hadji Agha. The road does not pretend. Lead — I follow.
lines.subtext: I will not swear forever, but I will not turn back now; my craft is your craft today.
lines.action_before: Tightens the bridle, sets the mare beside his

lines.text: Hadji Agha. Four down. One rode for the tower.
lines.subtext: The alarm is already up; we have minutes, not hours — give the word.
lines.action_before: wipes the blade once on the dead Cossack's sleeve

lines.text: Leave the grey horse. He carries their smell. He will run home to them.
lines.subtext: Do not let sentiment slow us; I have already chosen which animals live.
lines.action_before: shoulders Khanefi back from Nazarov's reins

lines.text: Rice-fields, then the river. Not the right road. They will look right.
lines.subtext: I mapped this ground while you spoke with the Russian; trust my eye.
lines.action_before: tilts his good eye toward the low fields

lines.text: Allah is great. It is enough, Hadji Agha.
lines.subtext: Stop weighing it. Decide. I am ready to die here, on horseback, beside you.
lines.action_before: reins his horse one pace closer to Hadji Murad's flank

lines.text: Khanefi. Leave the horse. A man who runs with a dead Cossack's mount runs slow.
lines.subtext: Discipline reasserted under fire; he is already calculating pursuit, not loot.
lines.action_before: Reins in his grey, glances back at the road

lines.text: Bata. The river, not the mountains. They will look for us where our hearts go.
lines.subtext: He knows his own hunger for the hills will be the hunters' first guess.
lines.action_before: Points left toward the rice-fields with a flat hand

lines.text: Gamzalo. How many cartridges?
lines.subtext: Ledger of what remains between him and his son in Vedeno.
lines.action_before: Wipes blood from his sleeve without looking at it

lines.text: It is well. We ride until the horses fall, then we ride on foot. Yusuf is waiting.
lines.subtext: Decision closed; the boy's name is the only reason he is still on this road.
lines.action_before: Draws his palms slowly down his beard

lines.text: Allah knows the rest.
lines.subtext: He has measured the odds and found them poor; the door on the calculation shuts here.
lines.action_before: Turns his horse off the high road into the wet field

lines.text: Bismillah. Mishkin is gone, agha. The horse was faster than my arm.
lines.subtext: I failed you and I name the failure before you must.
lines.action_before: reins his horse hard beside HM

lines.text: Two hours, no more. Then the tower will spit men like seeds.
lines.subtext: Decide now, brother — every breath here is a breath stolen from your wife.
lines.action_before: spits, glances back at the tower

lines.text: Leave the bay. Blood on the saddle, the dogs will smell him a verst off.
lines.subtext: I obey your order without question, but I will not let pride drown us.
lines.action_before: slaps Nazarov's loose horse away

lines.text: (low, half-sung) The earth is not yet dry, my brother — and the avenger does not sleep.
lines.subtext: I am steeling myself; if we die in the rice-fields, let the song outlive us.
lines.action_before: wipes blade on his sleeve, eyes the river

lines.text: Agha. Right is the mountains. Left is —
lines.subtext: I will follow you into the wrong road because your mother's milk is in me; finish the sentence yourself.
lines.action_before: halts, looks left toward the fields

lines.text: Eldar. The road to the right is theirs. We go left. The Alazan will hide our tracks.
lines.subtext: He has already chosen; the murid is told, not consulted.
lines.action_before: Reins his horse, glances once toward Benerdzhik.

lines.text: The field is water. The horses sink. We rest in the shrubs until the moon goes behind the hill.
lines.subtext: Calculating the cost of every wasted hour against Yusuf's life.
lines.action_before: Listens to a hoof tear free of the mud with a wet pop.

lines.text: Eat. Let them graze. A tired horse is a dead rider.
lines.subtext: Fear for the family spoken sideways, as care for the animal.
lines.action_before: Spreads bread and cheese on the burka between them.

lines.text: The nightingales sing again. Last night I heard the song of Hamzad. Bring me water — I will pray.
lines.subtext: He knows he may die before sunrise; the prayer is preparation, not piety.
lines.action_before: Sets down the bread, draws his palms slowly down his beard.

lines.text: Allah knows the road. Hadji Murad has given his word. It is well.
lines.subtext: The door is closed; no further talk of escape, of fear, or of family is welcome.
lines.action_before: Folds the burka for ablution, faces the dark hill.

lines.text: Be at peace in my tent, son of the Khansha. Eat. The road from Khunzakh is long, and a guest of mine fears nothing under this roof.
lines.subtext: Containment. The boy is already sentenced; the hospitality formula seals the verdict.
lines.action_before: Gestures the youth to the cushion nearest him.

lines.text: Your mother bargains with the infidel and calls it statecraft. God's law knows another name for it.
lines.subtext: Frames the coming murder as scripture, not ambition; offloads guilt onto her treason.
lines.action_before: Sets down the bowl, eyes fixed on Aseldar across the fire.

lines.text: The Ghazavat has one road. The Khans of Khunzakh will walk it, or they will not walk.
lines.subtext: Ultimatum disguised as prophecy; the dynasty ends tonight either way.
lines.action_before: Lifts two fingers, the murid's signal.

lines.text: Aseldar. The guest is tired. See that he rests.
lines.subtext: The kill order. Courtesy as the blade's sheath.
lines.action_before: Smiles once, slow, and turns his face to the tent wall.

lines.text: Brother — bad ditch. Shallow. The wolf does not sleep where the hunter sees his ear.
lines.subtext: I'm terrified; the position is a death-trap and I want Hadji Murad to hear it.
lines.action_before: Wipes mud from rifle, eyes the embankment.

lines.text: Karganov. I know his shout. Greedy man — wants the whole carcass, not just the tail.
lines.subtext: He won't accept surrender; this ends in blood, not coin.
lines.action_before: Spits toward the militia line.

lines.text: Three smokes maybe. Maybe two. The mare is hobbled — bad. A man on foot is half a man here.
lines.subtext: We cannot run; I am calculating my own death and finding no way out.
lines.action_before: Glances at the hobbled horses through the branches.

lines.text: If the moon had held, Hadji Aga — Russian bread tonight. Now we eat what the road gives.
lines.subtext: I trusted the plan; the plan failed; I will not blame the man beside me.
lines.action_before: Loads the rifle slowly, hands steady.

lines.text: I go first to the bank. Always first. Tell my horse — she walks home alone, she walks proud.
lines.subtext: I am about to die and I am asking you to remember me without saying the word.
lines.action_before: Tightens his belt, does not look back.

lines.text: Karganov. Militia. Both sides of the clump.
lines.subtext: I have counted them. We are surrounded; no point softening the fact.
lines.action_before: Returns from the edge, eye on the ditch.

lines.text: Hadji Agha — the ditch will hold till the cartridges run.
lines.subtext: I renew my oath. I will die here with you, not surrender.
lines.action_before: Sets his rifle across the earthwork.

lines.text: It is enough. Say the word.
lines.subtext: Stop weighing it. Decide. I am already ready to die.
lines.action_before: Meets Hadji Murad's eyes, one pace closer than needed.

lines.text: If God wills, we fall outside, on our feet. Not in their hands.
lines.subtext: Better the rifle than their paper; better the bog than a Russian room.
lines.action_before: Mutters a half-prayer, thumbs a cartridge.

lines.text: Hadji Murad. Listen — come out. The Russians offer the road back. Refuse, and I cannot hold them.
lines.subtext: Come out so I do not have to remember what we were. Make this easy for the man I am pretending to be.
lines.action_before: Touches dagger hilt, will not face the ditch.

lines.text: We are many. You are six. By Allah, count your cartridges before you count your honor.
lines.subtext: I need Allah as witness because my own mouth will not hold the lie alone.
lines.action_before: Spits once into the wet grass.

lines.text: You ran to them first. Do not name brotherhood at me from a hole in the ground.
lines.subtext: If I say it loud enough, the men behind me will believe I was the one wronged.
lines.action_before: Half-step forward, then checks himself.

lines.text: Bring the militia up the left bank. Close the ditch. Move — move, before the light is full.
lines.subtext: If I keep talking, I will have to look at what I am doing.
lines.action_before: Turns his back to the clump, voice clipped.

lines.text: Karganov. You are many. We are five. Come closer and count again.
lines.subtext: Acknowledges the trap by name; refuses to plead; measures the officer's nerve before the first shot.
lines.action_before: Sets rifle on the embankment, does not rise.

lines.text: Bata — the black mare first. A horse without a rider tells the militia what waits in the ditch.
lines.subtext: Buys seconds and signals the men: we fight like Hamzad, we do not surrender.
lines.action_before: Nods once toward the clearing edge.

lines.text: Khanefi, Gamzalo, Hadji Aga — dig deeper. A handful of earth is worth a brother today.
lines.subtext: Affection delivered as command; he is already counting which of them will not see the evening.
lines.action_before: Cuts a branch with his dagger, lays it on the bank.

lines.text: Yusuf is in Vedeno. If I fall here, the imam will know my answer was no.
lines.subtext: The deepest wound named in one short sentence; brevity is the grief; his death becomes the message.
lines.action_before: Pauses, eyes on the muddy water at his feet.

lines.text: Allah knows. It is well.
lines.subtext: The door closes. No further discussion. He has chosen the manner of his death and is at peace with it.
lines.action_before: Draws both palms slowly down his beard.

lines.text: Bismillah. Powder dry. Forty rounds, agha — and the dagger.
lines.subtext: I have counted what we have left to die with; do not ask me again.
lines.action_before: Slides his pouch toward Hadji Murad.

lines.text: Militia. Both sides. The bay will not run today.
lines.subtext: We are surrounded; the horses are finished, and so are we.
lines.action_before: Wipes mud from the rifle-lock.

lines.text: (low, half-sung) The earth dried on Hamzad's grave, brothers...
lines.subtext: I am making my peace. This is the song I will die inside.
lines.action_before: Sets his shoulder against the embankment.

lines.text: Your mother's milk. I came to give it back. It is done.
lines.subtext: The blood-debt is paid here, in this ditch, with my body.
lines.action_before: Touches Hadji Murad's sleeve, once.

lines.text: Agha. The ridge — Hadji Aga. Akhmet Khan with him.
lines.subtext: I have already chosen which throat opens first; tell me to move.
lines.action_before: Half-rises from the ditch, settles dagger.

lines.text: And — let me go out. Draw them. You ride.
lines.subtext: Spend me. I am the cheaper life here.
lines.action_before: Looks once at HM, then at the open ground.

lines.text: No. We stay. Where you stay, Agha.
lines.subtext: Do not send me back without you. I will not go.
lines.action_before: Plants knee against the earth wall.

lines.text: Inshallah. The dagger is enough.
lines.subtext: I am making peace now; the next minute may be the last.
lines.action_before: Lays palm flat on hilt, breath slowing.

lines.text: Hadji Agha. Two hundred. Hadji Aga leads them. Akhmet Khan rides at his shoulder.
lines.subtext: Old kunak turned dog, and your enemy's son beside him — this is the death they chose for you, not a soldier's death.
lines.action_before: spits, sights down the rifle, does not turn his head

lines.text: My horse is down. Good. One less rein to hold.
lines.subtext: I have already accepted I will not ride out of this clump.
lines.action_before: glances once at the bleeding mare in the bushes

lines.text: Khanefi — load. Faster. Allah, Allah.
lines.subtext: I am praying myself ready; when the prayer ends I expect the swords.
lines.action_before: thumbs a fresh cartridge, mutters under the breath

lines.text: Let them come into the shrubs. Closer. It is enough.
lines.subtext: Stop waiting, Hadji Agha — give the word, I am ready to die now.
lines.action_before: shifts one pace nearer to Hadji Murad at the ditch

lines.text: Wherever you fall, Hadji Agha. One step before. As I swore.
lines.subtext: The imam is nothing now, the Russian paper is nothing — only you, only the oath.
lines.action_before: lays a hand briefly on Hadji Murad's shoulder, then back to the rifle

lines.text: Hadji Murad. Come out. The road back is open one hour — no longer.
lines.subtext: Step into the open so I do not have to climb in after you and see your face.
lines.action_before: Reins in twenty paces from the ditch, does not dismount.

lines.text: Listen — you fired first. By Allah, every man here heard it. The blood from this hour is on your hand, not mine.
lines.subtext: I need a witness God cannot cross-examine, because my own men remember what we were.
lines.action_before: Half-turns to Akhmet Khan without looking at the ditch.

lines.text: Swords out, my men! Through the shrubs — do not stop at the first shot. Akhmet, the right. Close the ring, close it!
lines.subtext: If I stop talking I will have to think whose throat is at the end of this order.
lines.action_before: Thumb finds the dagger hilt; he does not draw, only touches.

lines.text: Faster — faster, you dogs! He is one man. One. Anyone who hangs back, I see it.
lines.subtext: If I leave him alive past noon, the shame outlives my sons.
lines.action_before: Spurs forward two lengths, then checks the horse — refusing to be the first into range.

lines.text: Hadji Aga. Once you ate from my dish. Today you bring two hundred guns to my ditch.
lines.subtext: Naming the betrayal without curse; reminding the man that adat remembers what he has chosen to forget.
lines.action_before: Lowers the rifle a finger's width, looks across.

lines.text: Khanefi — the long gun. Load the short one after. Eldar, sit. A dagger is for the last step, not the first.
lines.subtext: Buying Eldar's life by holding him still; he is already counting which of his men will not see the river.
lines.action_before: Does not turn his head; voice no louder than the reloading.

lines.text: Akhmet Khan rides his father's saddle. The father I knew. The son I will know shortly.
lines.subtext: He has measured the distance to that horse and decided the rider dies before he himself does.
lines.action_before: Marks one shrub, then the next, with his eyes.

lines.text: Gamzalo. The black mare bleeds in the grass. When the sun clears that branch, we go to the river.
lines.subtext: The horse is finished; so is waiting; the order is given without being given.
lines.action_before: Glances once at the dying horse, then up at the sun.

lines.text: Allah knows the road. Yusuf is in Vedeno. Hadji Murad is here. It is well.
lines.subtext: Closing the door on his son; accepting that he will not reach him; naming his own death without the word.
lines.action_before: Draws his palms slowly down his beard.

lines.text: Eh — Hadji Aga brings two hundred guests. Khanefi, load faster, brother — the wedding starts.
lines.subtext: We are outnumbered and likely to die; I will not say it.
lines.action_before: Snaps the breech shut, grins sideways at Khanefi.

lines.text: Il lyakha il Allakh! That one ran like a goat — Allah knows where my bullet went. To his cousin, maybe.
lines.subtext: I missed and it shames me; better they hear singing than silence.
lines.action_before: Lowers smoking rifle, spits into the ditch.

lines.text: Akhmet Khan in the bushes, agha. The son. He wants your head more than the Russian coin.
lines.subtext: This is the man who will not let us out alive.
lines.action_before: Goes still, peers through the leaves, voice flattens.

lines.text: Hadji Aga, eh — once he ate from your bowl. Now he shouts surrender. Dog learns Russian quickly.
lines.subtext: I am sick at heart that a kunak became this.
lines.action_before: Clicks tongue, reloads without looking down.

lines.text: I joke, Hadji Murad — but tell Eldar to sit. If he stands once more I carry him home in two pieces, inshallah.
lines.subtext: I am afraid for the boy and I love him; protect him for me.
lines.action_before: Brief glance at trembling Eldar, then back to the sights.

lines.text: Bismillah. Loaded, agha.
lines.subtext: My hands will not fail you; that is all I have to give.
lines.action_before: Slaps powder into the pan, passes the gun up.

lines.text: Gamzalo's bay bleeds in the bushes. He will not run again.
lines.subtext: One of us is already gone; the rest follow soon.
lines.action_before: Glances once at the thrashing horse, then away.

lines.text: Hadji Aga shouts loud. Brave men do not shout.
lines.subtext: The traitor knows he is a traitor; that is why his throat is open.
lines.action_before: Spits into the dirt at the edge of the ditch.

lines.text: (low, half-sung) The earth dried on Hamzad's grave — and the avenger did not sleep.
lines.subtext: If I am to die here I will die as your mother's son, not my father's.
lines.action_before: Wipes blood from his sleeve, draws breath.

lines.text: Your powder. It is done.
lines.subtext: Ask me nothing more; I am where I was always meant to end.
lines.action_before: Sets the last flask in HM's reach, kneels back to the wall.

lines.text: Stand back. The cheek is nothing — a flap of meat. I still have the dagger hand.
lines.subtext: I am bleeding out and I know it; if I name the wound, I become the wound.
lines.action_before: Presses the severed cheek against the bone.

lines.text: By Allah, no Khan's son dies on his knees. Open the door wider — let them see me come.
lines.subtext: The oath is the only thing holding my legs under me.
lines.action_before: Spits blood, steadies the blade.

lines.text: Hamzad wanted the line of Avaria. He will have it — at the length of my arm.
lines.subtext: I cannot save the house, but I can make the price of it unforgettable.
lines.action_before: Glances once at the doorway where his brothers fell.

lines.text: Coward? I am no coward. Tell my mother that — afterwards. Tell her exactly that.
lines.subtext: Her word still cuts deeper than any sabre in this room.
lines.action_before: Wipes the eye clear of blood with his sleeve.

lines.text: Hadji Murad — brother — if you live, remember which of us walked out first.
lines.subtext: I love you, and I am about to leave you alone with the rest of our lives.
lines.action_before: A half-step toward the threshold, dagger raised.

lines.text: Horses! Stay back, you fools — back, back!
lines.subtext: If the horses die, no one rides out; he is already planning a way that does not exist.
lines.action_before: Drives the horses uphill, away from the gully.

lines.text: Ha! Closer, brother — closer, so I do not waste lead.
lines.subtext: Loud cheer is the size of his fear; he sees the ring tightening.
lines.action_before: Fires standing, no rest for the barrel.

lines.text: Tssh — neck. Just the neck. Bad road today.
lines.subtext: He is hit and dying and refuses to name it; the road is him.
lines.action_before: Sits down hard, spits red into the dirt.

lines.text: Hadji Agha — powder. Pass. I still have one hand.
lines.subtext: Loyalty as craft, not vow; he works to the last because that is who he is.
lines.action_before: Reaches with his good arm toward Khanefi.

lines.text: The mare — let her loose. She knows the way without me.
lines.subtext: He is releasing himself; the horse is the soul he will not name.
lines.action_before: Looks once toward the tethered horses, then away.

lines.text: Agha. Let us go at them. With the swords.
lines.subtext: I cannot watch you bleed behind a wall — let me die in front of you, not beside you.
lines.action_before: Rises to his knees, dagger half-drawn.

lines.text: And — three more. Behind the shrub. Closer now.
lines.subtext: I am still your eyes. Use me before I am gone.
lines.action_before: Wipes powder-grit from his lashes, sights down the barrel.

lines.text: The swords, Agha. Now. Inshallah.
lines.subtext: I have made my peace. Do not make me outlive you.
lines.action_before: Resettles his hand on the dagger, eyes fixed on Hadji Murad.

lines.text: We go together. I do not go back.
lines.subtext: Bind my fate to yours — refuse me the shame of surviving.
lines.action_before: Leans out past the earth bank, body already half-given to the bullet.

lines.text: Khanefi. The powder. Keep it dry.
lines.subtext: I am still your naib; do not look at my wound.
lines.action_before: plugs cotton wool into shoulder, hands rifle back

lines.text: Eldar. Rest now. You ran ahead of us all.
lines.subtext: The boy is dead and I cannot bury him.
lines.action_before: draws his leg slowly from beneath the body

lines.text: Khan Mahoma — sing louder. Let them hear who they are killing.
lines.subtext: Make my name reach Vedeno before my body does.
lines.action_before: wipes blood from the lock of his gun

lines.text: Yusuf. A black horse. Like your grandfather rode.
lines.subtext: Forgive me. I could not come for you.
lines.action_before: second bullet strikes his side; he lowers himself into the ditch

lines.text: Allah is great. Hadji Murad has said it.
lines.subtext: The door is closed; I will die standing in my own name.
lines.action_before: draws both palms slowly down his beard

lines.text: Eh — sing louder, Khanefi! The reeds are full of Russian ears tonight, let them learn an Avar tune.
lines.subtext: I am terrified; if I stop joking I will start counting our dead.
lines.action_before: rams a bullet home, glances at Eldar's body

lines.text: Hadji Agha — your shoulder leaks. Plug it tighter. A bridegroom does not bleed on his own wedding, no?
lines.subtext: Do not die yet, master — I have nothing left to be if you go.
lines.action_before: tears a strip from his own beshmet, throws it across

lines.text: Allah knows where Bata's tongue went — into his neck, maybe. Quieter man now. Better.
lines.subtext: He sat down to die and I cannot look at him.
lines.action_before: spits, fires once at a moving shrub

lines.text: Two horses, four guns, one song. Cossack dogs — come closer, I cannot insult you from so far!
lines.subtext: Let them aim at me first; the master needs another minute.
lines.action_before: stands half-up from the ditch, waves his papakh

lines.text: If we ride out of this ditch, brother, I buy you white bread. If we do not — Allah owes me the loaf.
lines.subtext: I know we are finished and I am saying goodbye in the only language I have.
lines.action_before: drops back beside Hadji Murad, reloads slowly

lines.text: Bismillah. Pass it, agha — dry powder, the rag is fat.
lines.subtext: I will keep your hands full of loaded steel until mine stop moving.
lines.action_before: Rams a wrapped bullet home, holds the gun out.

lines.text: Eldar is with God. His pouch is still heavy — I take it.
lines.subtext: Grief later. The dead serve the living one cartridge at a time.
lines.action_before: Bends over the boy, fingers in the bloodied coat.

lines.text: (singing, low) The milk that fed me has not yet dried, my brother.
lines.subtext: I am telling you I will die here before I leave your side.
lines.action_before: Tears a cartridge with his teeth, voice cracks into tenor.

lines.text: Two more guns. Then the dagger. It is done.
lines.subtext: Do not ask me to run. The road back is the road of my father's shame.
lines.action_before: Lays the last loaded rifle by Hadji Murad's hand.

lines.text: Read it again, mon cher. Slowly. I should like to be quite certain the dispatch says 'killed' and not merely 'taken.'
lines.subtext: He already heard. He needs the second reading to compose his face before anyone sees it move.
lines.action_before: Sets the teacup down without sound.

lines.text: A pity. A very great pity. He was, it would seem, the one Tatar Petersburg might have learned something from — had Petersburg been disposed to learn.
lines.subtext: Mourning a lost trophy, not a man; and quietly indicting the Tsar he will never name.
lines.action_before: Folds the telegram once, along its existing crease.

lines.text: We shall write that he fell bravely, surrounded, refusing surrender. That much, at least, is true — and it costs the Empire nothing to say so.
lines.subtext: Begins drafting the official lie before the body is cold; civility as the polite face of erasure.
lines.action_before: Lifts the pen, tests the nib against his thumb.

lines.text: Maria Vasilyevna will ask after him at dinner. Tell her — tell her I shall explain it myself. She was fond of him, was she not?
lines.subtext: He cannot bear to hear the sentence spoken aloud by another voice; the affection was real, and that is the wound.
lines.action_before: A pause; the hand that held the telegram closes slowly.

lines.text: Speak. Shamil hears.
lines.subtext: I already smell what you carry; do not waste my breath warming it.
lines.action_before: Sets down the Quran, does not look up.

lines.text: So. The wolf is meat. Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un. It is written: he who flatters the giaur dies upon the giaur's knife.
lines.subtext: Good. The threat to my Imamate has erased itself, and Allah signs the receipt.
lines.action_before: Strokes his beard once, slowly.

lines.text: His head — where?
lines.subtext: I need the trophy in Russian hands; only then is the lesson legible to every naib who counts horses before prayers.
lines.action_before: Eyes fix on the courier.

lines.text: His mother remains. His wife remains. His sons remain. Treat them as guests of the Imam. So.
lines.subtext: They live as my warning to the next ambitious naib; mercy here is a longer blade.
lines.action_before: Turns a single page of the Book.

lines.text: Enough. Go. Tell the murids: the path narrows tonight. Allah has decided.
lines.subtext: I am closing this matter before any tongue in this court dares to call him brave.
lines.action_before: Lifts one palm; the beard-stroke ceases.

lines.text: My father — he is coming. The stones will know his horse before the dogs above know their own names.
lines.subtext: He is begging the dark to keep the promise his father has not yet kept.
lines.action_before: Presses ear to the pit wall, listening upward.

lines.text: Inshallah tonight. Inshallah. I am not afraid. Not afraid.
lines.subtext: He is asking the listener — anyone, even God — to stay a little longer.
lines.action_before: Wraps both arms around his own ribs.

lines.text: Tell Shamil's dogs the Naib's son spat on their bread. Tell them louder than I can.
lines.subtext: Testing whether his voice still has any reach beyond this hole.
lines.action_before: Kicks the empty bowl into the corner.

lines.text: Mother — if she asks — say I stood. Say it even if —
lines.subtext: He has just understood, for one second, that he may not see her again.
lines.action_before: Turns his face to the wall so the guard cannot see.

lines.text: Hadji Agha. The ravine. Now. Or never.
lines.subtext: I have already counted them. We die here unless we move this breath.
lines.action_before: spits powder, eyes the slope above the ditch

lines.text: Cut my hands loose. One rifle. It is enough.
lines.subtext: Do not let me die kneeling. Let me die owing nothing.
lines.action_before: tests the rope at his wrists with a slow pull

lines.text: Allah. Witness this. They took him indoors of the field.
lines.subtext: Shame on me. I was one step behind, not before.
lines.action_before: sees the head rolled aside, lowers his good eye

lines.text: Hadji Agha. I come. One step. As I swore.
lines.subtext: The oath does not end with your breath. It ends with mine.
lines.action_before: leans toward the bound corpse, jaw set, lips moving in prayer

lines.text: Hadji Murad. On your face. By Allah, the thistle falls the same as the rose.
lines.subtext: Naming him is the wound; the proverb is the bandage he ties over it for the men to see.
lines.action_before: Touches dagger hilt; will not look at the face.

lines.text: Listen — hold the others. Bind them. Khanefi, Khan Mahoma, Gamzalo. Living. The Russians count heads, not corpses.
lines.subtext: If he keeps issuing orders he will not have to feel the next blow he is about to strike.
lines.action_before: Steps over the body, eyes on his militiamen.

lines.text: Move the foot. Not the shoe — the head. Clean. Clean.
lines.subtext: He cannot soil the shoes because he cannot soil the story he will tell tonight.
lines.action_before: Plants boot on the corpse's back, lifts the dagger.

lines.text: He ran to them first. Remember that. He ran first.
lines.subtext: Telling the men is telling himself; the repetition is the only prayer he has left.
lines.action_before: Rolls the severed head away with the side of his foot.

lines.text: By Allah, the Tsar will hear our names tonight.
lines.subtext: The witness he calls cannot contradict him; his sons will eat, and that must be enough.
lines.action_before: Half-second pause; will not say the forbidden word.

lines.text: Yusuf...
lines.subtext: The deepest wound named once; the brevity is the grief and the farewell he could never speak aloud.
lines.action_before: Bleeding head lifts; eyes find no horizon.

lines.text: Mother. The song.
lines.subtext: Both halves of her warning have come true; he acknowledges the failure without pleading for it to be otherwise.
lines.action_before: Hand searches the bark of the tree.

lines.text: A horse. A black one.
lines.subtext: The promised gift to his son becomes the only inheritance left; love delivered in concrete things, never in words.
lines.action_before: Lips move against the dirt.

lines.text: Hadji Murad has stood.
lines.subtext: Third-person oath-register: the name still belongs to him, the body does not; honour outlasts the flesh the militiamen will take.
lines.action_before: Body shudders; pushes once from the trunk.

lines.text: Allah knows.
lines.subtext: The closing door. No reckoning asked of the imam, the Tsar, or the dagger above him; the matter ends with this sentence.
lines.action_before: Palms try to draw down the beard; fall.

lines.text: Eh — tie tighter, brother. My wrists, they are shy today.
lines.subtext: I will not let them see I am breaking; the joke is the last weapon I have.
lines.action_before: Spits blood, grins at the militiaman binding him.

lines.text: Look — they roll his head like a melon. Careful, dog, do not scuff your boots.
lines.subtext: If I name the horror loud, maybe my heart will not name it inside.
lines.action_before: Jerks chin toward Hadji Aga cleaning his shoe.

lines.text: Quiet, Khanefi. Listen. The nightingales again. Allah is not impressed.
lines.subtext: The world goes on without him; I cannot bear how small we are.
lines.action_before: Stiffens as bird-song rises over powder-smoke.

lines.text: I rode beside him, you understand? Beside. Write that down somewhere, Russian.
lines.subtext: Make me useful one last time — let my name travel where his head will travel.
lines.action_before: Lifts bound hands toward Karganov, voice cracking once.

lines.text: Eh — but we are alive, no? For now. Allah knows the rest.
lines.subtext: I do know the rest. I am simply not the one who will say it.
lines.action_before: Looks away from the corpse, mouth twisting into a smile.

lines.text: Bismillah. Agha — rise. The bay still stands.
lines.subtext: I will not let you die alone; if you mount, I die at your stirrup.
lines.action_before: Wipes blood from his palm onto his sleeve.

lines.text: Brothers — close. Shoulder to shoulder. It is done.
lines.subtext: We are already dead; only the manner remains ours.
lines.action_before: Cocks the pistol, sets his back to Gamzalo's.

lines.text: (singing, low) The earth dried on Hamzad's grave — the avenger did not sleep.
lines.subtext: I cannot weep for him; the song carries what my mouth cannot.
lines.action_before: Sees Hadji Murad fall by the tree.

lines.text: Bind me, dog. My hands are nothing now.
lines.subtext: You took the head; the body you bind is already empty.
lines.action_before: Drops the spent pistol, lifts both wrists.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. MAKHMET AOUL - DUSK
adapted_scenes.action: A lone rider emerges from the tree line above the aoul. HADJI MURAD — tall, broad-shouldered, sheepskin papakha pulled low — holds his horse to a walk on the descent. Below: mud-walled saklyas packed tight as honeycomb, kizyak smoke rising in thin blue columns against the darkening sky. Cattle low. A muezzin's call dies on the wind. Hadji Murad does not pause. He rides straight toward the gate, eyes tracking the rooftops, one hand loose at his side, the other steady on the rein. A MURID follows three lengths behind, saying nothing.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad descends into unknown territory without hesitation
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Tension — who is this man and why does he ride so calmly into danger?
emotional_beats.character_feels: Controlled vigilance; the stillness of a man who has made a decision he cannot unmake

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Willed exposure — a man stepping deliberately into the open
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Blue kizyak smoke against amber-grey dusk sky; the rider a dark silhouette descending; packed saklyas below like grey stone cells

inner_monologue_converted.original: Tolstoi's authorial comment on Hadji Murad's reputation and military fame
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: HM's posture and the Murid's three-length distance — deference without closeness — show rank without exposition

inner_monologue_converted.original: Thistle resilience as authorial metaphor for HM's character
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Dissolved into HM's unbroken descending line against a hostile landscape — the image carries the weight without voiceover

adapted_scenes.transition: The muezzin's dying call bleeds into the creak of a gate opening in darkness

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT./INT. MAHKET — SADO'S SAKLYA — NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: Two riders move through a narrow side street, hoods pulled low. The lead rider — HADJI MURAD — scans every doorway. He stops at a saklya cut into the hillside. On the roof, a shape under a sheepskin. HM taps the shape with his whip handle. SADO rises stiffly, blinks his lashless eyes, works his feet into wooden-heeled slippers. He descends the roof ladder without haste. At the bottom he reaches for HM's stirrup — Eldar is already there, hand on the bridle, moving the old man aside with a look. HM dismounts, limping slightly, enters under the penthouse. Sado follows, calling to a boy passing in the doorway to fetch his father from the mosque.

dialog_draft.line: Selaam aleikum.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am here. You know what it means that I am here.
dialog_draft.action_before: He uncovers his face — just enough for Sado to see.

dialog_draft.line: Aleikum selaam.
dialog_draft.subtext: I see you. I will not pretend I do not.
dialog_draft.action_before: He holds HM's gaze a moment, toothless mouth pulling into a smile, then looks away — checking the street.

dialog_draft.line: Sit. The bread is warm. My house is your house tonight.
dialog_draft.subtext: Whatever comes through that door after you, it comes through me first.
dialog_draft.action_before: He holds the inner door open with his body, not his hand — both hands are busy bolting the outer gate.

emotional_beats.beat: HM arrives disguised — a naib who once commanded dozens, now creeping through a side street with one murid
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The scale of his fall, the danger of this moment
emotional_beats.character_feels: Controlled alertness masking humiliation

emotional_beats.beat: Sado recognizes HM and does not hesitate — offers hospitality without question
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Relief and dread — the bond is real, and so is the cost
emotional_beats.character_feels: Sado: duty activated; HM: the first exhale since the flight began

emotional_beats.beat: The outer gate is bolted. The decision is made and cannot be unmade.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The trap closing — on both men, from different sides
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: temporary safety; Sado: the weight of a promise already given

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Refuge granted under an unspoken, irreversible covenant
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Dark narrow street, lamplight from a single rooftop chimney; sheepskin, wooden slippers, cracked clay walls; Eldar as silent bulk at the gate

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad trying to attract as little attention as possible, peering with quick black eyes
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Tight turn into side street instead of main road; HM's eyes moving before his body moves

inner_monologue_converted.original: Sado's hospitality as instinctive recognition of kunak bond
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Sado bolts the outer gate with both hands while inviting HM in — action and welcome simultaneous

adapted_scenes.transition: The bolted gate — close on iron against wood — carries into the next scene as the sound of a Russian patrol horse echoes in a distant valley

Subplot 'kunak_covenant' was marked as 'cut' but appears in scene

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. SADO'S SAKLYA — NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: Hadji Murad steps through the low doorway. The room: whitewashed clay walls, two polished copper basins catching lamplight, weapons racked in order. An old man hangs Hadji Murad's rifle and sword beside the house weapons — deliberate, reverent. Sado's wife sets cushions along the front wall without looking at the men. Hadji Murad and Sado lower themselves to the floor together, palms upward. They recite the prayer in near-silence, then draw their hands down their beards in unison. Eldar enters behind them, takes his place on the felt without a word.

dialog_draft.line: Ne habar?
dialog_draft.subtext: I already know the answer. I need to hear how much you will tell me.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sado settles on his heels beside Hadji Murad, eyes closed until the prayer ends.

dialog_draft.line: Habar yok.
dialog_draft.subtext: There is nothing you need to carry. Not yet.
dialog_draft.action_before: Hadji Murad adjusts his pistol at his back before he sits — the only visible sign of vigilance.

dialog_draft.line: The hares keep debating how to drive away the eagles. The Russians burned the hay in Mitchit. May their faces be torn.
dialog_draft.subtext: My village is exposed. You have brought the eagles closer by coming here.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sado turns his face toward the lamp flame, not toward his guest.

dialog_draft.line: Who tends your animals tonight?
dialog_draft.subtext: I understand the danger you are in. I am not asking you to say it aloud.
dialog_draft.action_before: Hadji Murad nods once — a slow acknowledgment — then glances toward Eldar.

dialog_draft.line: My son. He knows.
dialog_draft.subtext: The house is sealed. We will not be betrayed from inside.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sado rises without haste and places a flat bread at the center of the cloth.

dialog_draft.line: Inshallah.
dialog_draft.subtext: Whatever comes, we have placed ourselves in God's hands and in yours.
dialog_draft.action_before: Eldar fixes his gaze on the doorway, not on the food.

emotional_beats.beat: Weapons hung beside the host's weapons — guest accepted as equal under this roof
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Relief undercut by the fragility of the gesture
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad: controlled gratitude; Sado: the weight of what he has just committed to

emotional_beats.beat: Joint prayer — two men performing the same ritual, bodies synchronized
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Solidarity; a moment of stillness before danger resumes
emotional_beats.character_feels: Both: genuine faith and genuine fear braided together

emotional_beats.beat: The Russian burning of Mitchit named — violence enters the room without entering the room
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The walls of the saklya suddenly feel thinner
emotional_beats.character_feels: Sado: suppressed rage; Hadji Murad: guilt for having drawn that world closer

emotional_beats.beat: Eldar's single word — Inshallah — closes the scene
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The precariousness of everything; three men choosing trust over reason
emotional_beats.character_feels: Eldar: absolute loyalty; Sado: the bond is sealed and irrevocable

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Sacred hospitality as a quiet act of defiance against overwhelming force
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Clay walls, copper basins, ordered weapons, one oil lamp — warm amber against stone-cold dark; bread at center, three men at margins

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad, understanding that the old man did not wish to say what he knew, slightly nodded his head and asked no more questions.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: HM asks about the animals — indirect acknowledgment. A single nod. No follow-up. Silence does the work.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Sado's implication that danger is present but unspoken
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Sado turns toward the lamp flame rather than his guest when naming the Russian burning — discomfort physicalized.

on_the_nose_risk: Sado's 'The hares keep debating' — stays allegorical, not declarative; acceptable
adapted_scenes.transition: The lamp flame holds on Eldar's watchful face as voices outside the saklya — wind, a distant dog — press against the silence.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. SADO'S SAKLYA - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: A low-ceilinged room, a single oil lamp, smoke-stained walls. Hadji Murad sits cross-legged on felt beside Eldar. Sado squats before him, palms upward — he prays first, then speaks. His face is a map of constrained fear: Shamil's order arrived yesterday, the village cannot openly defy it. When Sado finishes, Hadji Murad nods once — deliberate, no urgency — and names his requirement: a guide to the Russian lines. Sado calls his son, who vanishes and returns with Bata: sun-blackened, compact, coat frayed at every seam. Bata takes in Hadji Murad without flinching. A beat. He grins.

dialog_draft.line: My house is yours. The open road — that I cannot promise.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will die before they take you here, but I cannot protect you outside these walls.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sado strokes his face after prayer. Meets HM's eyes for the first time.

dialog_draft.line: A letter must go to the Russian commander. My murid carries it. He needs a guide.
dialog_draft.subtext: I have already weighed all risks. I need execution, not counsel.
dialog_draft.action_before: HM sets his right hand flat on his knee. Closes the subject of fear with the gesture.

dialog_draft.line: Two smokes to the ford. The mare knows it better than I do — but I know it too.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am the best man for this and we both know it. Now name the coin.
dialog_draft.action_before: Bata steps inside, glances at Eldar, then squarely at HM. A short nod — murid to legend.

dialog_draft.line: Go tonight. Deliver it to the sardar's hand. No one else.
dialog_draft.subtext: Failure here ends everything. I am trusting a stranger with my only move.
dialog_draft.action_before: HM draws a folded letter from his coat and holds it out — unhurried, as if offering bread.

dialog_draft.line: Tonight, then. Inshallah.
dialog_draft.subtext: God willing — and if not God, then still tonight.
dialog_draft.action_before: Bata takes the letter without looking at it. Tucks it inside his coat against his chest.

emotional_beats.beat: Sado names the threat — Shamil's order is live, the village is afraid
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The net closing around HM before he has taken a single step toward the Russians
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM absorbs the information as confirmation, not surprise — he already knew

emotional_beats.beat: HM pivots immediately to action — send the letter, get the guide
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Admiration tinged with dread: this man moves like a chess player who sees ten moves ahead
emotional_beats.character_feels: Controlled urgency — every hour of delay narrows his window

emotional_beats.beat: Bata accepts without ceremony, tucks the letter away
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Fragile hope — the machinery of defection has started moving
emotional_beats.character_feels: Bata: professional pride. HM: relief compressed to nothing visible.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Options narrowing — HM sets his single gambit in motion through a stranger
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Oil lamp between HM and Sado; Eldar shadow behind; Bata's frayed coat, letter disappearing inside it; stone floor, low smoke.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad heard him absently, looking at the door and listening to the sounds outside
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: HM's gaze tracks the door throughout Sado's speech — body turned toward the room, attention on the exit

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad listened with attention and nodded approvingly
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Single slow nod when Sado finishes — no expression beyond it; the nod closes the fear-discussion and opens the action-discussion

on_the_nose_risk: Bata's 'Inshallah' risks piety cliche — played dry and quick, it reads as professional fatalism, not prayer
adapted_scenes.transition: The saklya door closes on Bata's silhouette; the lamp gutters — cut to darkness, then mountain trail pre-dawn

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. SADO'S SAKLYA - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: Firelight. Bata stands before Hadji Murad — burnt-black skin, ragged Circassian coat, frayed sleeves. He listens without moving. Hadji Murad holds up three fingers: the price. Bata nods once, puts both hands on his chest, and steps out. The door closes. Sado's wife and daughter enter carrying food — low table, tea, cheese, pancakes in butter. The daughter keeps her eyes down but her posture stiffens as she moves past Hadji Murad. He sees the silver coins at her collar and lets his hand fall from his cartridge pouch. He says nothing more.

dialog_draft.line: Canst thou take him there — and bring him back?
dialog_draft.subtext: I am not paying for a one-way trip. I am paying for a man who returns.
dialog_draft.action_before: Hadji Murad holds up three fingers, waits. His eyes do not leave Bata's face.

dialog_draft.line: I can. There is not another Chechen who would pass as I can.
dialog_draft.subtext: My worth is the route. Without me, you go nowhere.
dialog_draft.action_before: Bata grins — one sharp, short grin — then straightens.

dialog_draft.line: A rope should be long. A speech — short.
dialog_draft.subtext: You are already talking too much. Prove it with your feet.
dialog_draft.action_before: Hadji Murad looks away from Bata toward the fire. Dismissal without movement.

dialog_draft.line: Then I'll hold my tongue.
dialog_draft.subtext: I accept. And I am not offended.
dialog_draft.action_before: Bata nods once, places his hands over his heart.

dialog_draft.line: By the bend of the Argun — the two haystacks at the forest glade. Ask for Khan Mahoma. He knows what to do.
dialog_draft.subtext: The plan is already in motion. You are the last piece.
dialog_draft.action_before: Hadji Murad speaks without raising his voice, as if reciting a direction already memorized long ago.

dialog_draft.line: Eat first. The road does not leave before morning.
dialog_draft.subtext: This house still offers peace. Whatever comes, that remains.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sado steps aside as the women set the table. He gestures toward the food without looking at Bata or Hadji Murad.

emotional_beats.beat: Bata hired — mission dispatched
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The web tightens; HM is moving faster than anyone around him realizes.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad: controlled urgency. Bata: pride.

emotional_beats.beat: Sado's daughter enters; HM's hand drops from cartridge pouch
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Unexpected stillness — a flicker of normalcy inside danger.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad registers beauty and vulnerability simultaneously; stops himself from speaking of the next errand.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A trap being sprung in the dark — each man playing his role with precision while the household goes on around them.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Firelight orange on ragged wool. Silver coins at the daughter's collar catching the light. Three fingers in the air. Hands over a heart. The low table between armed men.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad took hold of one of the cartridge pouches but let his hand drop and became silent on seeing two women enter.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: His hand physically stops mid-gesture; he does not speak again. The restraint is visible, not narrated.

adapted_scenes.transition: The low table is set. Firelight holds. Sound of wind through the saklya door carries into the dark hillside outside — Khan Mahoma waiting somewhere beyond the treeline.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. SADO'S SAKLYA — NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: The women have withdrawn. Silence settles. Sado pours cold water from the metal ewer over Hadji Murad's hands; HM turns up his sleeves with precise, unhurried movements, dries on a rough towel. Eldar exhales audibly — the first sound from him since the women entered. At the low table, HM pulls a rolled note from beneath a cartridge-bullet and sets it on the boards between himself and Sado. He eats sparingly — a sliver of bread, a scrape of cheese — then presses honey onto a crust with his small knife. Sado watches him eat, saying nothing yet. Eldar reaches for more bread, catches HM's withdrawal from the table, and pulls his own hand back. Sado takes the note and tucks it into his own cartridge-pocket.

dialog_draft.line: To be handed to my son.
dialog_draft.subtext: My son is hostage. This note is the only thread I hold.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sets the rolled note on the table between them without looking up.

dialog_draft.line: Where must the answer be sent?
dialog_draft.subtext: I am already committed — I only need the mechanics.
dialog_draft.action_before: Does not touch the note yet. Fingers the ewer handle.

dialog_draft.line: To thee. And thou must forward it to me.
dialog_draft.subtext: You are now my courier. That is kunak duty, not request.
dialog_draft.action_before: Meets Sado's eyes for the first time since eating began.

dialog_draft.line: It shall be done.
dialog_draft.subtext: My life is now also pledged to your son's survival.
dialog_draft.action_before: Takes the note, folds it once, presses it into his own cartridge-pocket.

dialog_draft.line: Our honey is good. This year above all others — plentiful and good.
dialog_draft.subtext: You are a guest in this house. For this moment, pretend the war is outside.
dialog_draft.action_before: Watches HM spread honey on the crust. A quiet flush of pleasure crosses his face.

dialog_draft.line: I thank thee.
dialog_draft.subtext: The honey. The house. The note. All of it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lays down the knife, pushes back from the table.

emotional_beats.beat: HM produces the note — wordless declaration of his purpose
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The weight of what he has left behind; a father's desperation held perfectly still
emotional_beats.character_feels: Control maintained over grief; the note is action where there is no other action

emotional_beats.beat: Sado pockets the note without ceremony
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Relief — this man will not flinch
emotional_beats.character_feels: Sado: the bond sealed; HM: a fraction of the weight transferred

emotional_beats.beat: Honey exchange — host pride, guest restraint
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Poignant normalcy inside danger; the beauty of the ritual
emotional_beats.character_feels: Sado: dignity of hospitality intact; HM: warmth received, contained

emotional_beats.beat: Eldar mirrors HM's withdrawal from the table
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The absolute devotion of the young murid — wordless, total
emotional_beats.character_feels: Eldar: hunger suppressed without complaint; loyalty as reflex

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Sacred obligation exchanged in the language of bread and honey — the kunak bond made binding
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Low oil lamp; amber honey catching light; Sado's calloused hands on the ewer; cold water on HM's white forearms; the note — small, rolled — between two men

inner_monologue_converted.original: Eldar sat motionless as a statue — relief when women left
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Eldar's audible exhale once the door closes; his reaching for bread then stopping when HM withdraws

inner_monologue_converted.original: Though he had eaten nothing for more than twenty-four hours, HM ate only a little
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: HM eats sparingly — bread, cheese, a scrape of honey — then lays the knife down and steps back; hunger made invisible by discipline

inner_monologue_converted.original: Sado knew he was risking his life
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: The pocket-motion of tucking the note away — deliberate, final — carries the full weight of the risk without naming it

adapted_scenes.transition: Sado's hand pressing flat over the cartridge-pocket — over the note — holds for a beat; then sound of wind against the roof tiles carries into the dark mountain exterior

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. FOREST AMBUSH POST - VOZVIZHENSK - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: Three Russian soldiers move single-file through dark forest, boots crushing dead leaves. They carry rolled cloaks across their shoulders, sheepskin coats, muskets shouldered. They halt at a blackened plane tree — the standing ambush post. Stars visible through bare branches above. Panov squats against the trunk, produces a short pipe, taps it against his knee. The other two soldiers settle into position, scanning the tree line. Silence except for wind and distant dogs.

dialog_draft.line: Good job it's dry. Last week I had water in my boots for three days.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is managing fear with talk about small discomforts — the body, not the danger.
dialog_draft.action_before: Panov settles against the plane tree trunk, draws out his pipe.

dialog_draft.line: They say he crossed over near Mahket. Hadji Murad himself. You believe it?
dialog_draft.subtext: He half-believes it. The name carries weight even here.
dialog_draft.action_before: He strikes flint, cups the flame against the wind. Eyes stay on the tree line.

emotional_beats.beat: World pivot — the camera crosses from Chechen village to Russian outpost. Two worlds exist simultaneously, neither knowing the other's exact position.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Dread — the proximity of the two worlds, the collision coming.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Routine alertness — Panov knows the risk but has made peace with it through repetition.

emotional_beats.beat: HM's name spoken by a Russian soldier — the fugitive becomes rumor, becomes legend, on both sides of the line.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The scale of HM's crossing registers: even his enemies discuss it in the dark.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Low-grade unease, masked by soldier's flat affect.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The empire's indifferent machinery — soldiers in the dark, doing their duty, not knowing what moves through the forest nearby.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Blackened plane tree, dead leaves underfoot, stars through bare branches, the glow of a pipe bowl in darkness; wool and leather textures; cold blue-grey palette

inner_monologue_converted.original: The soldiers' routine ambush duty described narratively by Tolstoi
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Physical staging: dead leaves, starlight, pipe ritual — routine made visible

adapted_scenes.transition: Pipe ember fades to black — cut to interior warmth of Sado's saklya
