adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. FOREST OUTPOST - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: Four soldiers settle against plane trees in darkness, rifles grounded. Stars visible through bare branches above. PANOV, senior among them, rifles through his coat pockets, jaw tight. He crouches, pats the earth around his boots, finds nothing. AVDEEV, broad-cheeked, pulls a knife from his belt without a word. He hollows a small depression in the soil with practiced ease, sets the pipe stem into it, packs tobacco flush. He strikes a sulphur match — brief flare. The ember catches. Avdeev draws once, exhales, rolls to make room. Panov wipes the stem on his sleeve and lies prone. Smoke curls up into cold air. The four men barely visible against the dark tree trunks.

dialog_draft.line: Good job it's dry.
dialog_draft.subtext: Noting the small mercy that makes a hard night bearable.
dialog_draft.action_before: Panov unslings his rifle, leans it against the tree with a clang.

dialog_draft.line: What are you looking for?
dialog_draft.subtext: Already watching Panov, already half a step ahead.
dialog_draft.action_before: Avdeev sees Panov patting his pockets, turns onto his side.

dialog_draft.line: Bowl of my pipe. Where the devil has it got to.
dialog_draft.subtext: Small loss, real irritation — a man's comforts are few.
dialog_draft.action_before: Panov checks his coat a second time, shakes his head.

dialog_draft.line: Stem's there though.
dialog_draft.subtext: The problem is already solved in his mind.
dialog_draft.action_before: Avdeev glances at Panov's hand, where the stem protrudes.

dialog_draft.line: What a smart lad you are, Avdeev.
dialog_draft.subtext: Genuine warmth, rare between these men — Avdeev is liked.
dialog_draft.action_before: Panov rises, takes the improvised pipe from the earth, wipes the stem.

dialog_draft.line: That'll be as the company decides.
dialog_draft.subtext: Deflecting — unwilling to take sides on the officer question, loyal by default.
dialog_draft.action_before: Avdeev rolls onto his back, exhales smoke upward toward the stars.

emotional_beats.beat: Avdeev improvises a ground-pipe for Panov without being asked
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Warmth — competence and quiet generosity in a bleak place
emotional_beats.character_feels: Avdeev feels useful, present; this is where he belongs

emotional_beats.beat: Soldiers share smoke and drift into talk of the commander's gambling debts
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Unease beneath the ease — empire's rot seeps even here
emotional_beats.character_feels: Panov keeps the peace; Avdeev echoes him; the third soldier chews on grievance

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Brief human warmth against a cold empire's indifference — these men sustain each other where the institution fails them
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Starlight through bare branches; match flare catches Avdeev's broad face; ember glow the only warmth; soldiers prone like shadows against dark soil

inner_monologue_converted.original: Panov did not consider it necessary to forego the pleasure of smoking
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He wipes the stem, lies prone — no explanation needed; the act says it

inner_monologue_converted.original: smoking in ambush was forbidden, but this ambush hardly deserved the name
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Visual: the casual posture of men who feel no real threat; rifles propped, not held

adapted_scenes.transition: Ember dies in the earth; darkness resettles over the four men; the stars do not move

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. RUSSIAN FORT — OUTPOST TREE — NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: Three soldiers sit beneath a plane tree, backs against bark, a small fire dying between them. PANOV draws the last life from a clay pipe. A SOLDIER (Nikitin) speaks low, insistent — the company chest, the missing kopeks, the commander who borrows and pays back slow. Panov taps ash onto the dirt, says nothing for a moment. Avdeev stares at the stars through the canopy. A chorus of jackals erupts beyond the treeline — the Ukrainian soldier, half-asleep against his rolled cloak, mutters something. The jackals stop. Wind moves the branches. Avdeev rolls onto his back, folds his arms behind his head.

dialog_draft.line: It's not the first time. He takes it, he gives it back.
dialog_draft.subtext: Don't start a fight we can't finish — the hierarchy always wins.
dialog_draft.action_before: Panov taps the pipe clean against his boot heel, doesn't look up.

dialog_draft.line: The community is a strong man.
dialog_draft.subtext: I agree with you, Panov, to end this before it turns ugly.
dialog_draft.action_before: Avdeev quotes it flat, almost to himself, eyes on the branches.

dialog_draft.line: Panov... do you ever feel dull?
dialog_draft.subtext: I am disappearing out here and I need someone to notice.
dialog_draft.action_before: Long silence after the jackals quit. Avdeev doesn't move, still staring up.

dialog_draft.line: Dull, why?
dialog_draft.subtext: Don't ask me to carry this. I'm barely carrying my own.
dialog_draft.action_before: Panov glances sideways at him, reluctant.

dialog_draft.line: I do. Sometimes so dull I don't know what I might not do to myself. That time I drank all the money — that was from it. It took hold of me till I thought: I'll just get blind drunk.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am confessing to a darkness I have no name for.
dialog_draft.action_before: Avdeev exhales slowly. No drama — just a man reporting a weather condition.

dialog_draft.line: Sometimes drinking makes it worse.
dialog_draft.subtext: I know exactly what you mean and I will not say so aloud.
dialog_draft.action_before: Panov refills the pipe with slow fingers, not looking at Avdeev.

emotional_beats.beat: Money dispute — Panov closes it down with a proverb-level finality
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The worn patience of men who've argued this before
emotional_beats.character_feels: Panov: quiet authority; Nikitin: unresolved grievance

emotional_beats.beat: Jackals laugh in the dark — the Ukrainian's half-joke breaks tension
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Brief, dark relief — the wilderness is indifferent to human grievance
emotional_beats.character_feels: Avdeev: momentarily amused, then the dullness returns harder

emotional_beats.beat: Avdeev confesses his dullness — Panov absorbs it without comfort
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The loneliness of men who cannot help each other
emotional_beats.character_feels: Avdeev: slightly lighter for saying it; Panov: weighted by it

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Quiet dissolution — men enduring a slow erasure they can name but not cure
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Pine tar smell, dying embers, clay pipe, loose cloak on dirt; jackals as offscreen presence; starlight filtered through canopy; two faces lit orange, one in shadow

inner_monologue_converted.original: Avdeev felt so dull he didn't know what he might not be ready to do to himself
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Avdeev's confession delivered flat, without self-pity — a weather report; Panov's slow pipe-loading as avoidance of eye contact

inner_monologue_converted.original: Tolstoi's explanation of the company commissariat system
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Cut entirely — Nikitin's grievance implies the system; no exposition needed

on_the_nose_risk: Avdeev line 'don't know what I might not do to myself' — retained because Tolstoi's flat peasant register is itself the subtext engine; emotionally naked but tonally correct
adapted_scenes.transition: Panov's pipe-smoke rises into stars; dissolves to the same stars seen from a different hillside — HM's world, or the fort ramparts at dawn

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. FOREST OUTPOST ROAD - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: Three Russian soldiers crouch at a dark outpost among fallen leaves. Avdeev sits closest to Panov, speaking low — the confession of a man picking at an old wound. When footsteps crunch through the underbrush, Panov is on his feet first, gun raised. Two silhouettes emerge from the tree line: Bata, shorter, hands open at his sides; behind him a taller murid, still. Bata steps into the thin moonlight and speaks before Panov can demand it.

dialog_draft.line: It's chiefly because of that — went instead of my brother — that I feel so dull. He's living like a king. And I'm here.
dialog_draft.subtext: He chose sacrifice and now can't forgive himself for it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Avdeev stares at the ground, pulling a twig apart between his fingers.

dialog_draft.line: Well, brother. That's how it is.
dialog_draft.subtext: There's no comfort to give. He knows it. Avdeev knows it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Panov exhales, taps out his pipe against his boot.

dialog_draft.line: Me — friendly Chechen. Gun, yok. Sword, yok. Want prince.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am unarmed. I am a messenger. Do not shoot.
dialog_draft.action_before: Bata stops dead in the road, arms spread wide, palms open toward the soldiers.

dialog_draft.line: On your knees. Both of you.
dialog_draft.subtext: I don't trust this, but I'm listening.
dialog_draft.action_before: Panov advances two steps, rifle leveled at Bata's chest.

dialog_draft.line: Letter. For prince. Hadji Murat sends.
dialog_draft.subtext: This is bigger than you. Don't make a mistake.
dialog_draft.action_before: Bata slowly draws a folded paper from inside his coat with two fingers, holds it out.

emotional_beats.beat: Avdeev's confession of regret — he sacrificed himself for his brother and now doubts it
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Quiet sadness; the waste of ordinary men in an imperial machine
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hollow; trapped; unable to name his loss without sounding weak

emotional_beats.beat: Footsteps in the dark — the watch snaps alive
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Sudden tension cutting through the melancholy
emotional_beats.character_feels: Panov: focused, dangerous; Avdeev: grateful for the interruption

emotional_beats.beat: Bata steps into the light, letter extended — the first bridge between HM and the Russians
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Suspension; this is a hinge moment
emotional_beats.character_feels: Bata: controlled pride, professional calm; Panov: wariness holding violence in check

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The empire's smallest soldiers — dulled by sacrifice and routine — become the accidental first link in Hadji Murat's gamble.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Moonlight through bare trees. Three soldiers' breath misting. Bata's open palms catching the only white light. The folded letter between two worlds.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Avdeev's internal rumination on why he replaced his brother and his growing resentment
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Spoken confession to Panov — the twig pulled apart in his hands, the trailing silence after

inner_monologue_converted.original: Soldiers' heightened alertness at hearing footsteps
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Panov kicks Nikitin awake; bodies rising in the dark without a word spoken

on_the_nose_risk: Avdeev's line 'I feel so dull' approaches direct emotion-naming — anchored by the twig action and the resigned delivery to keep it oblique
adapted_scenes.transition: Bata's extended letter holds in the frame — then Panov's hand reaches across and takes it; the paper passes between empires

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. VOZDVIZHENSK FORT — OUTPOST — NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: Panov steps onto the road, musket leveled. Two shadows halt — Bata, shorter, raises both empty hands wide; the taller man (Bata's unnamed companion) mirrors the gesture. Avdeev and Bondarenko march the pair off toward the fort lights. Two hours later they return. Avdeev drops his rifle butt on the earth and crouches by the fire, grinning. Panov and Nikitin watch him. In the distance the one lit window of the commander's house glows behind trees.

dialog_draft.line: Prince want. Big business.
dialog_draft.subtext: I carry enough authority to demand access — don't treat me as a prisoner.
dialog_draft.action_before: Both arms still raised, he nods toward the fort.

dialog_draft.line: You know, mates — those shaved-headed boys are fine. One of them's married. I asked him, 'Molly bar?' He says 'bar.' Two of them. What a talk.
dialog_draft.subtext: The enemy is a man like me — that fact is too large to sit with quietly.
dialog_draft.action_before: Settles against the tree, not quite looking at his comrades.

dialog_draft.line: Nice. Meet him alone and he'd let your guts out soon enough.
dialog_draft.subtext: Don't get soft. Softness gets you killed.
dialog_draft.action_before: Doesn't look up from his pipe.

dialog_draft.line: Maybe so. Still — fine fellows.
dialog_draft.subtext: I know. I don't care.
dialog_draft.action_before: Looks up at the fading stars, not arguing.

emotional_beats.beat: Bata walks unarmed into Russian guns and names his business without flinching
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Tension cut by a kind of stunned admiration
emotional_beats.character_feels: Bata: controlled professional confidence; soldiers: wary curiosity

emotional_beats.beat: Avdeev returns and humanizes the 'enemy' in a handful of words
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The war's absurdity made briefly visible
emotional_beats.character_feels: Avdeev: genuine wonder; Nikitin: reflexive defensiveness against that wonder

emotional_beats.beat: Single lit window in the commander's house — the fort sleeps but power does not
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The bureaucratic machinery waiting above all this
emotional_beats.character_feels: The soldiers: indifferent; audience: foreshadowing

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The small human warmth that survives at the border of two killing systems
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Firelight on soldiers' faces; Bata's open hands catching the light; distant single window gold against dark pine. Dawn not yet visible, stars thinning.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Panov's practical assessment of the scouts as requiring escort
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Direct spoken dispatch order to Avdeev — terse, no commentary

inner_monologue_converted.original: Tolstoi's narrator noting the soldiers' chilliness and silence
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Panov unrolling his cloak; silence after; visual beat only

inner_monologue_converted.original: Avdeev's internal warmth toward the Chechens
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Avdeev's stumbling, delighted retelling — the emotion is in the repetition, not stated

on_the_nose_risk: Avdeev's line 'fine fellows' borders on overt — kept because it is his character tic and Nikitin immediately deflates it
adapted_scenes.transition: The single lit window in the commander's house holds — then MATCH CUT to interior: Simon Vorontsov at his writing table, candle burning low.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. VORONTSOV QUARTERS - DRAWING ROOM - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: Four candles burn on the card table. Rich curtains, carpeted floor — an impossible drawing room inside a Caucasian fort. Vorontsov deals. Princess Marya Vasilevna leans toward Poltoratsky, crinoline pressing his leg. Poltoratsky plays an ace into a losing hand. Across the table the ADJUTANT's jaw tightens. No one looks at the war outside.

dialog_draft.line: That is the second ace you have sacrificed tonight.
dialog_draft.subtext: You are embarrassing this regiment in its own drawing room.
dialog_draft.action_before: Adjutant sets down his cards precisely, aligns their edge with the table.

dialog_draft.line: Mais voyez-vous — the cards conspire against me, mon cher.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am not here for the cards.
dialog_draft.action_before: Poltoratsky glances at Marya Vasilevna before replying to the Adjutant.

dialog_draft.line: Do forgive him. I distracted him — didn't I tell you so?
dialog_draft.subtext: I know exactly what I am doing.
dialog_draft.action_before: She touches Poltoratsky's sleeve briefly, ostensibly pointing at his cards.

emotional_beats.beat: Poltoratsky wastes another ace, oblivious to the game
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Amused recognition — a man undone by proximity
emotional_beats.character_feels: Poltoratsky: suspended between desire and performance; Adjutant: contempt held in check

emotional_beats.beat: Marya Vasilevna openly claims responsibility with a smile
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The empire's luxury floating above the war — beautiful and hollow
emotional_beats.character_feels: Vorontsov: mildly aware, choosing not to see; Marya: savoring the power

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Empire-decadence: a Petersburg salon transplanted to a war zone, desire replacing vigilance
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Candlelight on crystal and gold braid; darkness beyond curtained windows; Adjutant's rigid posture against Poltoratsky's slack ease

inner_monologue_converted.original: everything that reduced Poltoratsky to obliviousness of everything except the consciousness of her nearness
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Poltoratsky discards a winning ace while watching Marya Vasilevna's hand move across the table

adapted_scenes.transition: Candle flame gutters as someone opens a door; cold air from the Caucasus night enters the warm room

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. VORONTSOV RESIDENCE - DRAWING ROOM - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: The card game winds down. POLTORATSKY refills glasses, stealing glances at MARYA VASILEVNA across the green baize. MICHAEL VORONTSOV is summoned out by a servant. He returns composed, calls for champagne, reveals nothing. As farewells are exchanged, Vorontsov leans to Poltoratsky: tomorrow's forest expedition. MARYA VASILEVNA grips Poltoratsky's hand — firm, deliberate — and holds it a beat longer than courtesy demands.

dialog_draft.line: Is it your company ordered to the forest tomorrow?
dialog_draft.subtext: I know what the officer on duty brought. You will see it for yourself soon enough.
dialog_draft.action_before: Poltoratsky reaches for his coat; Vorontsov intercepts him at the doorway, voice low.

dialog_draft.line: Yes. Mine. Why?
dialog_draft.subtext: He is barely listening — his eyes track Marya Vasilevna across the room.
dialog_draft.action_before: Turns to face Vorontsov, visibly distracted.

dialog_draft.line: Then we shall meet tomorrow.
dialog_draft.subtext: The prize I have been offered will land in your sector first.
dialog_draft.action_before: A slight smile. He does not elaborate.

emotional_beats.beat: Vorontsov receives secret news and immediately recontains it behind hospitality
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Unease — something is coming that the room does not yet know
emotional_beats.character_feels: Vorontsov: quiet satisfaction; the machinery is moving

emotional_beats.beat: Poltoratsky misses the political weight of the exchange entirely, focused on Marya Vasilevna
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Dramatic irony — he is about to be handed a consequential assignment he treats as backdrop
emotional_beats.character_feels: Poltoratsky: pleasantly oblivious, buoyed by the handshake

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Vorontsov's controlled foreknowledge vs. Poltoratsky's social blindness — the empire's machinery hums while its officers play cards
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Green baize, champagne flutes, candlelight; Vorontsov's stillness against the room's chatter; Marya Vasilevna's extended handshake in foreground

inner_monologue_converted.original: Poltoratsky preoccupied only by the thought that he would in a minute be pressing Marya Vasilevna's hand
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He turns away from Vorontsov mid-sentence to track Marya Vasilevna; the missed political weight shown by his expression, not stated

inner_monologue_converted.original: Vorontsov shrugging — 'I mustn't say'
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He orders champagne immediately after returning — the gesture of a man resealing a room's mood

adapted_scenes.transition: Poltoratsky steps into cold night air, Marya Vasilevna's handshake still warm in his palm — cut to the dark tree-line of the forest at dawn

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. VORONTSOV QUARTERS — NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: The last guests are gone. Candles burn low. Marya Vasilevna stands before Simon Vorontsov in the center of the room, arms crossed, waiting. He does not sit — remains near the door as if half-considering escape. She holds his gaze until he looks away first. He moves to the mantle and straightens a candlestick that doesn't need straightening.

dialog_draft.line: Eh bien. Vous allez me dire ce que c'est.
dialog_draft.subtext: I already know. I want to see if you'll lie to me.
dialog_draft.action_before: She stops him with two words before he can reach his chair.

dialog_draft.line: Mais, ma chère—
dialog_draft.subtext: Stalling. He has no plausible denial ready.
dialog_draft.action_before: He lifts his hand as if to deflect, then drops it.

dialog_draft.line: C'était Hadji Murat, n'est-ce pas?
dialog_draft.subtext: Say yes. Say it was him. Let me have been right about something tonight.
dialog_draft.action_before: She steps closer. Her voice does not rise.

dialog_draft.line: Not Hadji Murat himself. His emissary. He will come tomorrow — to meet me at the wood-cutting site.
dialog_draft.subtext: The correction is real but the pride is unmistakable: I will deliver him to my father.
dialog_draft.action_before: He turns from the mantle to face her, reading her disappointment as curiosity.

dialog_draft.line: Tomorrow.
dialog_draft.subtext: Not a question. She is calculating what that means for Simon — and for the man coming in.
dialog_draft.action_before: She looks toward the dark window, not at him.

emotional_beats.beat: Marya Vasilevna breaks the polite fiction of the evening immediately.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Respect for her — she sees through the social performance.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Simon feels cornered but not displeased; he wants her to know he matters.

emotional_beats.beat: Simon corrects the myth of HM's personal appearance — but the correction reveals how much is at stake for his career.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Unease — the man being traded is already being weighed for his usefulness.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Simon feels the weight of his father's name pressing on tomorrow's meeting.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Ambition dressed as intimacy — a private moment that is really about power.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Low candlelight, long shadows. Marya Vasilevna in evening dress still, Simon still in uniform jacket. Untouched wine glass on table.

inner_monologue_converted.original: In the monotonous life of the fortress the young Vorontsovs were glad of this occurrence.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Marya Vasilevna's posture — alert, energized, leaning in — shows the relief of something finally happening.

adapted_scenes.transition: The candle Simon pinches out — darkness snaps in. Cut to: a different darkness. Sado's saklya.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. SADO'S SAKLYA — BEFORE DAWN
adapted_scenes.action: The saklya is silent. A fire has burned to coals — dim orange glow on stone walls. Hadji Murat lies on red cushions, fully dressed, papakha still on his head, one hand under his cheek. He does not look like a man sleeping — he looks like a man who finally stopped running. Eldar is a few feet away against the wall, flat on his back, arms at his sides, his tall frame motionless. A cartridge-belt crosses his chest, rising and falling with slow breath. Outside: nothing. The mountain silence is total.

emotional_beats.beat: First time we see Hadji Murat without eyes open — three sleepless nights of flight behind him.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: A held breath released — and a new dread. This peace is borrowed.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Not felt by anyone. They are finally, briefly, unconscious.

emotional_beats.beat: Eldar's posture — soldier even in sleep, cartridges visible.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Loyalty made physical. The murid who will die for him is already arranged like a body.
emotional_beats.character_feels: N/A — visual establishment only.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The eye of the storm — stillness before the crossing.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Dying coals. Two bodies at rest. HM's papakha on his head even in sleep. Eldar's cartridge-pouches catching the ember light. No sound.

inner_monologue_converted.original: After the three sleepless nights he had passed flying from the murids Shamil had sent to capture him, Hadji Murad fell asleep.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: HM's body fully dressed, papakha on — a man who could not afford to undress. The three nights made visible in his posture.

adapted_scenes.transition: The coals dim further. A pre-dawn grey at the smoke-hole. Hold. Then: exterior — the fort at first light, a bugle distant.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. SADO'S GUEST CHAMBER (SAKLYA) - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: Embers crumble in the grate. A wick flutters in a wall niche. The earthen floor creaks. Hadji Murad's hand finds his pistol before his eyes open. Sado steps in, soft as cloth, and lowers himself onto his heels in front of him. He does not light a lamp. Eldar sleeps in the corner, pistol and dagger still in his belt, his upper lip moving as a child's might. Sado's gaze passes over the boy, returns to Hadji Murad. Sado's right hand rests flat on his own chest. Outside, faint and not yet near: voices gathering at the mosque, the dry clatter of wooden shoes on packed dirt. Hadji Murad listens. He draws his palms slowly down his beard.

dialog_draft.line: What is it?
dialog_draft.subtext: He has already counted the doors and the men.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sits up without a sound, fingers leaving the pistol grip.

dialog_draft.line: Listen. A woman from her roof saw you. The aoul knows. The Elders have gone to the mosque. They mean to keep you here.
dialog_draft.subtext: I have failed to hide you, but I have not failed you yet.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lays his palm flat on his chest, eyes lowered.

dialog_draft.line: It is well. I must be off.
dialog_draft.subtext: The matter is decided; do not try to keep me.
dialog_draft.action_before: Draws both palms slowly down his beard.

dialog_draft.line: My house is your house. The horses are saddled.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will die before they take you from this room.
dialog_draft.action_before: Rises in one motion, already half-turned to the door.

dialog_draft.line: Eldar.
dialog_draft.subtext: We are leaving now. No questions.
dialog_draft.action_before: Reaches for his burka without taking his eyes from the door.

dialog_draft.line: And — ready.
dialog_draft.subtext: I was already awake; I am yours.
dialog_draft.action_before: Is on his feet before the name finishes, hand setting his cap straight, the other hand resettling on his dagger.

emotional_beats.beat: The hidden guest learns the village has betrayed his hiding place.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Tightening dread; a clock has started.
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: cold clarity. Sado: shame folded into duty. Eldar: the calm of a man who has been waiting to act.

emotional_beats.beat: Sado offers the kunak vow without speaking it aloud.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Reverence; a small culture made visible.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Sado: a verdict. HM: gratitude swallowed by urgency.

emotional_beats.beat: HM crosses the threshold from guest to fugitive in two words.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The point of no return.
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: a door closing inside him.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Honor under siege — a vow kept in whispers while the village arms itself.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Niche-lamp glow. Black-and-orange palette. Pistol on belt, papakha on peg, burka on hook. Mosque-hum bleeding through mud walls. No moon yet.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad rose immediately, as if he had not been asleep at all.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Hand on the pistol before the eyes open; the body has been keeping watch.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Sado's grave duty as kunak host.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Palm laid flat on his own chest before he speaks.

adapted_scenes.transition: Mosque-hum thickens; wooden shoes clatter closer outside the saklya wall — the sound carries into the next scene as the door swings open onto starlight.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. SADO'S COURTYARD / VILLAGE LANE - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: Starlight without moon. Saklya roofs cut black silhouettes; the mosque and its minarets sit at the top of the village like a held breath. The black-eyed boy holds two horses, their breath steaming. Hadji Murad places his foot in the narrow stirrup and swings up onto the high saddle in one continuous motion, gun already across his thigh. Eldar mounts behind him, slower, settling. Sado walks beside the horse in a sheepskin too large for him, arms swinging, crossing from one side of the lane to the other as if to draw any bullet meant for the rider. A door opens further down the lane; a head pokes out, retreats. A man breaks uphill toward the mosque, clattering. From the mosque, more voices now — many. Hadji Murad lifts his whip, touches the boy's shoulder lightly. The boy steps aside.

dialog_draft.line: Sado — may God reward you.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will not see you again, and we both know it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Settles into the saddle, right foot finding the second stirrup; addresses Sado without turning his head.

dialog_draft.line: Inshallah. Ride.
dialog_draft.subtext: Do not waste this moment on me.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lays his hand briefly on the horse's neck, then steps clear.

dialog_draft.line: And — Agha. Ahead.
dialog_draft.subtext: I have already chosen which man I would kill first.
dialog_draft.action_before: Repositions his hand on the dagger; eyes fixed up the lane toward the running man.

dialog_draft.line: Allah knows.
dialog_draft.subtext: The matter is closed; the ride is the only argument now.
dialog_draft.action_before: Touches the whip lightly to his horse's flank.

emotional_beats.beat: The fugitive enters the open air of a village that has already turned.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Exposed; every door is a possible enemy.
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: cold focus. Eldar: a body tightening for fight. Sado: a man giving away what he loves to keep his word.

emotional_beats.beat: Sado walks the side-step pattern of a kunak who knows what walks beside an honored guest.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Tenderness inside danger.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Sado: a vow extending past his door into the open lane.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad crosses an irrevocable threshold — from hidden man to hunted man, and on toward the Russians.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The story has tipped; there is no road back.
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: a door closing on the mountains.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Crossing the threshold — honor made into motion.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Starlit roofs, no moon. Mosque-minarets above. Steam from horses. Sado in oversized sheepskin. Whip, stirrup, papakha. Mosque-hum + wooden-shoe clatter.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Sado followed almost running, swinging his arms and crossing the street.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Side-stepping bodyguard pattern beside the horse — his loyalty made into footwork.

inner_monologue_converted.original: The horse, as if it knew what it had to do, started at a brisk pace.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Whip-touch on flank; horse moves before the rider has finished speaking — the animal has read the night.

adapted_scenes.transition: Hoofbeats accelerate down the side-street; the mosque-voices fall behind into wind — the rhythm of horses carries into the next scene as the riders break out of the aoul.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. AOUL — NARROW SIDE-STREET — NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: A boy steps back from a bridle. HADJI MURAD swings into the saddle; the horse starts at a brisk pace down the lane as if it knows the road. ELDAR rides a length behind, rifle across his thighs. SADO follows on foot in his sheepskin, almost running, swinging his arms, crossing the lane to scan ahead. At the streets' meeting, shadows peel from a wall — five MEN block the way, muskets rising. A voice barks: Stop. HM does not stop. He draws the pistol from his belt and spurs straight at them. The men split. Two shots crack at his back. A bullet chips clay. Sado halts mid-stride, arms half-raised. He watches the riders vanish. He does not call out. He lays his palm flat on his own chest.

dialog_draft.line: Stop. Who's that. Stop!
dialog_draft.subtext: We have to be seen to try — Shamil is watching.
dialog_draft.action_before: A musket-hammer cocks in shadow; boots scuff stone.

dialog_draft.line: Sado.
dialog_draft.subtext: I take your roof with me; I cannot give it back.
dialog_draft.action_before: HM reins in for one beat at the lane's mouth, half-turns in the saddle, finds Sado's eyes.

dialog_draft.line: Go. The road is yours.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will die before I name where you went.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sado lays his palm flat on his chest and bows his head a fraction.

emotional_beats.beat: The kunak bond is severed by motion, not by word — HM rides, Sado stays.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Loss without farewell; the cost of hospitality made physical.
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: gratitude compressed into a single name. Sado: a vow kept by standing still.

emotional_beats.beat: First shots — the village fires on its own guest to save its own face.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Dread; the world has narrowed to the road.
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: cold recognition that retreat is now closed.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Severance — the guest leaves, the host remains, and neither will see the other again.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Moonless lane, lanterns behind shutters, blue-grey night. Sheepskin papakhas. Pistol-flash on clay walls. Sado's hand on his own chest as final image.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Sado follows running, swinging his arms, crossing from side to side — narrator marks his agitation.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Sado physically scanning the lane, then halted mid-stride, palm to chest — body carries the vow.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Source-narrator: 'as if it knew what it had to do' — the horse senses the night.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Horse starts before HM's heels touch — animal calm against human dread.

adapted_scenes.transition: The hoofbeats fade into the gurgle of the river below — sound bridges into the next scene at the ravine.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. RAVINE ABOVE THE STREAM — NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: HM reins in the panting horse on a rise. Below, water gurgles over stone. Behind him in the aoul, cocks crow back and forth across roofs. Then, beneath the cocks, a tramp of hooves and the voices of many men. ELDAR halts beside him, rifle half out of its cover. HM does not look back. He nudges the horse on at an even pace until twenty MOUNTED MEN close the gap and fan out behind him in the dark. HM stops. He drops the bridle from his right hand. With his left he unbuttons the rifle-cover, draws the rifle with his right. Eldar mirrors the movement, not a heartbeat behind. HM turns his horse a half-step to face the riders, lifts the rifle to his hip, level. The lead riders pull up. No one fires. The river runs. An owl cries once, far off.

dialog_draft.line: What do you want?
dialog_draft.subtext: I have already decided what happens next; speak quickly.
dialog_draft.action_before: HM lifts his rifle clear of its cover, settles the stock against his hip without sighting.

dialog_draft.line: Do you wish to take me?... Take me, then.
dialog_draft.subtext: The first man forward dies; the second decides whether more die.
dialog_draft.action_before: HM lets the bridle fall slack across the horse's neck, both hands now on the rifle.

dialog_draft.line: Agha.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am here. Whichever one steps first, he is mine.
dialog_draft.action_before: Eldar shifts his horse a half-length forward and to HM's right, rifle settled.

dialog_draft.line: It is well.
dialog_draft.subtext: The matter is decided. The talking ends here.
dialog_draft.action_before: The lead rider opens his mouth to call across — HM cuts him off, drawing his palm slowly down his beard.

emotional_beats.beat: Standoff — twenty against two, and the two are still.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Held breath; the geometry of honour made visible.
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: the calm of a man who has measured the cost. Eldar: the wordless joy of being chosen for this.

emotional_beats.beat: HM fires once into the air; the riders pull back.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Relief that resolves into dread — the road only goes deeper into the forest now.
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: a door has closed behind him.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Decision — refusal is performed in stillness, not in flight.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Steep ravine wall, water silver-black below. Twenty silhouettes arrayed in the dark, breath of horses visible. HM and Eldar isolated on the rise. Owl-cry over silence.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Narrator: the riders 'had decided to detain Hadji Murad or at least to make a show of detaining him in order to justify themselves in Shamil's eyes'.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Riders fan out but never close the last twenty yards — the show is the act.

inner_monologue_converted.original: HM's interior calculation of distance and threat as he listens.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He drops the bridle and uncovers the rifle in one accustomed movement before turning.

adapted_scenes.transition: HM fires once into the dark; the muzzle-flash bleeds into firelight on a Russian sentry's face — cut into the next scene.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. FOREST GLADE — NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: A bonfire flickers between dark pines. Four murids crouch around it — KHANEFI, ELDAR, and two others. Horses stand hobbled at the tree line. A whistle from the dark. Khanefi rises. Hadji Murad rides in from the shadows, dismounts without a word. He looks at the fire, then at Khanefi. Khanefi nods once. The men scatter the coals with boot heels, stamp out the branches. Eldar takes both horses by the bridle and ties them apart between two trees. Gamzalo lifts a rifle from a burka on the ground, checks it, and moves to the forest edge without being told — the direction HM came from. Eldar shoulders his own rifle and takes the opposite flank. The glade goes dark. HM stands alone at the center, reloading his own rifle. Above the pines, the Pleiades burn cold.

dialog_draft.line: Put out the fire.
dialog_draft.subtext: We are hunted. The fire is a target.
dialog_draft.action_before: Dismounts. Stands still. Eyes on the flame.

dialog_draft.line: Has Bata been here?
dialog_draft.subtext: Are the first steps of the plan already in motion?
dialog_draft.action_before: Moves to the burka, unslings his rifle.

dialog_draft.line: Long ago. With Khan Mahoma. That way.
dialog_draft.subtext: The mission moves faster than you. It is already beyond your reach.
dialog_draft.action_before: Points without turning — the opposite direction from where HM rode in.

dialog_draft.line: We must take care. I was followed.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am already an enemy of the world I left.
dialog_draft.action_before: Loads the rifle methodically, not rushing.

emotional_beats.beat: HM returns to his men — the fire is doused on his single word
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The weight of the decision: something irreversible has begun
emotional_beats.character_feels: Command — but the command of a man alone between two dangers

emotional_beats.beat: Gamzalo and Eldar take their positions without being ordered
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Loyalty rendered in silence — these men will die for him
emotional_beats.character_feels: Grim trust: the only currency that has not yet been spent

emotional_beats.beat: HM reads the Pleiades — night is almost gone
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Time pressing. The point of no return passed while he counted stars.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Isolation: the sky is the only thing that does not ask anything of him

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Irrevocable threshold — the last moment of the old life before surrender
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Dying coals, boot heels crushing embers; starlit glade; two horses tied apart; Gamzalo and Eldar as dark silhouettes at opposite edges of frame

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad calculated by the Pleiades that it must be long past midnight
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: HM lifts his eyes to the Pleiades — a single held beat. He begins to walk.

adapted_scenes.transition: The last ember dies; darkness holds the glade. Cut to: first grey light on a Russian palisade wall

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. FOREST CAMP - BEFORE DAWN
adapted_scenes.action: The fire is ash. Stars fade above black pines. Hadji Murad takes a ewer from Khanefi's pack without a word, steps away from the sleeping murids, removes his shoes, pours water over his hands. He lays his burka on the earth, faces south, folds his fingers to his ears. His lips move in silence. He sits down cross-legged, bows his head, and is still. His breathing slows. Then — a burst of jackals: howling, cackling, a sound between weeping and laughter. He lifts his head. Pale light bleeds along the eastern tree-trunks. He glances toward the path Khan Mahoma took — empty. He bows his head again. Then boots on dead leaves. Khan Mahoma pushes through the brush, Bata at his shoulder, both still moving fast. Hadji Murad raises his head. He does not stand.

dialog_draft.line: Eh — the prince himself. We sat, we ate. White bread.
dialog_draft.subtext: The mission went better than expected; he is proud and wants HM to feel reassured
dialog_draft.action_before: Khan Mahoma drops beside Hadji Murad, still breathing hard from the walk

dialog_draft.line: What did he say.
dialog_draft.subtext: Strip the pleasure from it — give me the substance
dialog_draft.action_before: Hadji Murad does not turn his body; his eyes move to Khan Mahoma's face

dialog_draft.line: He is pleased. He will receive you. Tomorrow, if you come.
dialog_draft.subtext: The door is open — all HM's calculation has worked
dialog_draft.action_before: Khan Mahoma straightens, the smile holding

dialog_draft.line: It is well.
dialog_draft.subtext: He expected this — fate has confirmed what he already knew
dialog_draft.action_before: A long pause. Hadji Murad looks east at the brightening sky, then closes his eyes briefly

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad prays alone in the pre-dawn dark — the murids sleep, he is awake and apart
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Solitude; the weight of the decision he has already made
emotional_beats.character_feels: Certainty; fate is with him

emotional_beats.beat: Jackals erupt — he wakes from half-sleep, the dream of triumph dissolving into animal noise
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Unease; the howling undercuts the confidence
emotional_beats.character_feels: A flicker of doubt instantly suppressed

emotional_beats.beat: Khan Mahoma returns with good news — the path to the Russians is open
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Relief cut with irony — the audience knows what HM does not
emotional_beats.character_feels: Vindication

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Solitary faith — and the first hairline crack in it
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Cold blue pre-dawn light; steam from the ewer; burka flat on needle-covered earth; jackal sounds off-screen; eastern sky turning grey-rose behind black pines

inner_monologue_converted.original: He felt in advance firmly convinced of success, and fate smiled on him
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He does not stand when Khan Mahoma returns — no relief in his body, only confirmation of what he already knew

inner_monologue_converted.original: He dreamt how he and his brave followers rushed at Shamil with songs
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: The jackal howl replaces the dream — we hear chaos where he imagined triumph; he simply lifts his head and looks east

adapted_scenes.transition: The eastern light grows. Hadji Murad stands, shakes earth from his burka. The camp stirs. Sound of horses.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. FOREST CLEARING / ROAD THROUGH FOREST - EARLY MORNING
adapted_scenes.action: Dawn. Mist drifts through felled timber. Russian soldiers work axes at the tree line — the thud of blades rhythmic, mechanical. Poltoratsky and his officers sit on upturned drums in the clearing, empty bottles at their feet, eating from paper wrappings. A drummer works at a cork. From the forest road a small mounted party emerges through the fog: Hadji Murad at the head, erect, weapons gleaming from the night's cleaning. Khan Mahoma and Bata flank him. The murids ride behind. The Russians stop eating. Poltoratsky rises slowly. Hadji Murad halts twenty paces out and waits — he does not dismount.

dialog_draft.line: Well. He actually came.
dialog_draft.subtext: He expected a trap or a no-show — not this.
dialog_draft.action_before: Poltoratsky sets his tin cup down on the drum without looking at it, eyes fixed on the mounted figure.

dialog_draft.line: Eh — I told you. He said he would come. He came.
dialog_draft.subtext: Pride in having brokered this. Also mild reproach at Russian skepticism.
dialog_draft.action_before: Khan Mahoma nudges his horse a half-step forward, grinning at Poltoratsky's officers.

dialog_draft.line: Tell him the prince expects him. We ride at once.
dialog_draft.subtext: He has no script for this moment — falls back on orders.
dialog_draft.action_before: Poltoratsky straightens his jacket, then gestures toward the road.

dialog_draft.line: It is well.
dialog_draft.subtext: He has already read every Russian face in the clearing. He sees what they are.
dialog_draft.action_before: Hadji Murad holds Poltoratsky's gaze for a long beat before answering — not defiant, simply measuring.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad rides out of the mist, fully composed, into Russian hands
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Awe at his composure — and a low dread at what he is giving up
emotional_beats.character_feels: Control. He chose this. He will not show what it costs.

emotional_beats.beat: Poltoratsky and his officers see the man they expected as a legend and find a man who looks back
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The gap between Russian mythology of the enemy and this present, human figure
emotional_beats.character_feels: Poltoratsky: surprise, then the reflex to manage the situation. Hadji Murad: contempt held very still.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: First contact — two worlds meet in silence before either speaks
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Mist, axe-cleared stumps, Russian detritus vs. Hadji Murad's immaculate weapons. His stillness against their disorder.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad asked particularly for the words with which Vorontsov had answered — assessing what he was walking into
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: His measuring gaze across the clearing before speaking; the twenty-pace halt that forces the Russians to come to him

inner_monologue_converted.original: He ordered his men to clean their weapons and look well — a matter of pride and tactical presentation
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Gleaming weapons and composed bearing visible against the Russians' vodka-breakfast disorder

adapted_scenes.transition: Poltoratsky's gesture toward the road — the column forms and moves into the forest; axe-sounds fade behind them

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. FOREST ROAD — CAUCASUS — MORNING
adapted_scenes.action: Poltoratsky and a handful of officers sit on upturned drums amid empty bottles and paper scraps. Freze tears bread. Someone pries the cork from a third bottle of porter. The men laugh about General Sleptsov — dead in some magnificent saber charge that all of them know, but none of them say, never happened that way. A rifle shot cracks from the left. The bullet hisses through mist and splinters a birch twenty meters off. Nobody flinches. Poltoratsky grins and sets down his cup.

dialog_draft.line: Hullo — that's our line. Kostya, now's your chance. Get back to the company. I'll bring the whole force up and we'll arrange a battle that will be simply delightful... and then we'll make a report.
dialog_draft.subtext: He performs valor the way Sleptsov performed it in the story they just told — for the record, for the report, for the woman back in Petersburg reading it.
dialog_draft.action_before: He turns toward the smoke with a faint, pleased smile, as though a card game has just become interesting.

dialog_draft.line: Sir.
dialog_draft.subtext: He understands the game. He does not question it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Freze is already on his feet before Poltoratsky finishes the sentence, brushing crumbs from his coat.

emotional_beats.beat: Officers mythologize Sleptsov's death as heroic saber-charge — the lie comforts them
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Irony — we see the gap between the story they tell and the war they are in
emotional_beats.character_feels: Poltoratsky: genuine pleasure in the ritual of military camaraderie; no conscious cynicism

emotional_beats.beat: Bullet passes close; Poltoratsky treats it as a pleasantry, dispatches Freze with a grin
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Unease — the casualness of the order is more disturbing than panic would be
emotional_beats.character_feels: Poltoratsky: elation, the specific lightness of a man who has stopped weighing consequences

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Imperial self-enchantment — the war is a story men tell each other to stay cheerful
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Empty bottles on drum-skins; birch bark split white where bullet struck; mist between trees; Poltoratsky's dapple-bay stamping at the edge of frame

inner_monologue_converted.original: Poltoratsky in that peculiar state of elation and kindly careless gaiety which he always felt when there was a possibility of danger
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Sets down cup with a faint pleased smile the moment the shot rings out; turns toward the smoke as though welcoming it

inner_monologue_converted.original: None of them saw in Sleptsov's death that most important moment of a life — they saw only the valour of a gallant officer
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Officers laugh and toast while telling the saber-charge story; no one pauses; a bottle is opened as the punchline lands

adapted_scenes.transition: Poltoratsky swings onto his horse; the dapple-bay's hooves churn frost from the road — cut to: the treeline where Freze's company disappears into smoke

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. RAVINE EDGE - FOREST OUTPOST - MORNING
adapted_scenes.action: Freze breaks from cover and runs toward smoke drifting up the slope. Poltoratsky's Kabarda dapple-bay is led forward; he mounts, signals his company, and rides them out along the skirt of the forest. The mist lifts. Across the ravine, at the edge of a young wood, half a dozen Chechen horsemen sit their saddles, watching. One raises a long rifle and fires. The line answers in a thin scatter of shots. The Chechens turn their horses and pull back into the trees. The smoke clears. The slope falls quiet. Poltoratsky rides up the line, looks at the empty wood, looks at his men, then lifts his hand and gives the order. The whole line opens fire. Cheerful little puffs of white smoke unfurl along the ridge. Soldiers laugh, ramming, working the locks, firing again.

dialog_draft.line: Splendid morning for it. Carry on, eh?
dialog_draft.subtext: He has decided to make a war out of nothing because nothing is what frightens him.
dialog_draft.action_before: Settles in the saddle, glances down the line, taps the bay's neck with his glove.

dialog_draft.line: As you say, your honor.
dialog_draft.subtext: He has seen this kind of order kill a man before breakfast.
dialog_draft.action_before: Pulls the pipe from between his teeth and tamps it slowly against his palm.

emotional_beats.beat: The threat passes — and the officer creates a new one.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Quiet dread.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Poltoratsky: vindication; Panov: silent dissent.

emotional_beats.beat: The line opens fire on an empty wood.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Recognition: this is how men die for nothing.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Soldiers: relief at distraction; Panov: stillness.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The cheerful violence of empire — boredom curdled into gunfire.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Lifting mist, low golden light through smoke; bay horse against grey slope; bright muzzle flashes in cold air; pipe ember.

inner_monologue_converted.original: The soldiers, pleased to have some distraction, hastened to load and fired shot after shot.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Soldiers laugh, ramming, working the locks, firing again — the cheerfulness IS the indictment.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Poltoratsky nevertheless gave orders to fire.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Tossed-off line 'Splendid morning for it' — the order issued as drawing-room banter.

adapted_scenes.transition: The last muzzle-flash echoes and folds into a single dry crack from across the ravine — one shot, finding its man.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. SHARPSHOOTER LINE - RIDGE - CONTINUOUS
adapted_scenes.action: A single shot snaps from the far wood. A rifle drops. Avdeev folds to his knees in the trampled grass and goes onto his side, then onto his face, both hands pressed flat against his stomach. He rocks himself, a small rhythmic motion, breath coming in soft moans. Soldiers gather around him in a loose half-circle, unsure where to put their hands. Poltoratsky rides up, dismounts, crouches. A young soldier from Avdeev's pair stands holding both rifles, his mouth working. Panov pushes through the ring with a tin can-lid of clear spirit. He kneels at Avdeev's head, slides one broad hand under it, and offers the lid to his lips. Avdeev sips, gags, hands the lid back. Panov holds it a moment, looks at it, then lifts it and drinks it down in one slow pull. He sets the lid on the grass and reaches…

dialog_draft.line: What is it, lad. Hit?
dialog_draft.subtext: He is trying to keep the salon voice on; the salon voice is failing.
dialog_draft.action_before: Drops to one knee, glove still in his hand, glance flicking from the wound to the soldier's face.

dialog_draft.line: Just going to load, your honor. Heard a click. Looked, and he'd dropped his gun.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is reciting because reciting is the only thing keeping him upright.
dialog_draft.action_before: Holds out both rifles like he doesn't know which is his.

dialog_draft.line: It doesn't hurt. Only stops me walking. A drop of vodka, your honor.
dialog_draft.subtext: It hurts. He is asking, without asking, to be eased out of the world.
dialog_draft.action_before: A small, half-laugh under the breath, eyes not meeting Poltoratsky's.

dialog_draft.line: My soul turns against it, brother. You drink.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is letting go of the body — of its small comforts — quietly, in advance.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sips, swallows once, and pushes the lid back with the side of his hand.

dialog_draft.line: Well, brother. Never mind.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is naming the thing as small so it will not crush them both.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lowers Avdeev's head onto his rolled coat, then takes the lid back.

emotional_beats.beat: The shot lands. The 'splendid morning' has a body in it now.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The cold of recognition.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Poltoratsky: the polish thinning; Panov: practiced grief; soldiers: shame they cannot name.

emotional_beats.beat: Avdeev refuses the vodka — declines the last comfort of the service.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: A man stepping quietly out of the empire.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Avdeev: clarity; Panov: tenderness disguised as practicality.

emotional_beats.beat: Panov drinks the lid himself. The pipe comes out.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Witness. Loyalty without speech.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Panov: bearing it for the boy; Poltoratsky: the first crack in the Guards-officer mask.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A man being seen, named, and not yet abandoned — at the cost of the officer's idea of war.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Trampled wet grass; dark stain spreading under pressed hands; tin lid catching cold light; Panov's pipe unlit, held in a steady hand.

inner_monologue_converted.original: He was lying prone, holding his wounded stomach with both hands, and rocking himself with a rhythmic motion moaned softly.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: The rocking kept literal — body language IS the interiority. No voice-over.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Panov, severely frowning, brought Avdeev a can-lid full.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Frown becomes the careful hand under the head; severity becomes tenderness disguised as routine.

inner_monologue_converted.original: 'My soul turns against it.'
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Kept verbatim — Avdeev's voice profile (peasant idiom, sideways feeling) is uncuttable here.

adapted_scenes.transition: Panov strikes a match. The flame holds. The pipe catches. Smoke drifts up and joins the smoke still hanging over the ridge — one body, the morning's only result.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. FOREST CLEARING NEAR VOZDVIZHENSK - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Avdeev lies on a spread cloak, face waxen, a darkening stain at his belt. Panov frowns hard, pours spirit into a tin can-lid and offers it. Avdeev's lips twitch; he hands the lid back without drinking. Panov drains it in one motion, tamps his pipe but does not light it. Hooves approach. The sergeant-major stiffens. Poltoratsky flicks his whip, mounts, and rides to meet a chestnut gelding emerging from the birches — Simon Vorontsov, his adjutant, a Cossack, a Chechen interpreter behind him. Soldiers lift the cloak with Avdeev on it and begin the slow walk back toward the fort.

dialog_draft.line: My soul turns against it. Drink it yourself.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will not have my last act be a flinch — and I want him to have it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Touches the lid to his lower lip, lowers it again, hands it back.

dialog_draft.line: Well, brother. Never mind.
dialog_draft.subtext: I cannot bear this and I will not let you see it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Drains the spirit, sets the lid down with care, looks past Avdeev's face.

dialog_draft.line: Your honor — the colonel.
dialog_draft.subtext: Compose yourself, the institution is here.
dialog_draft.action_before: Straightens, eyes on the trees.

dialog_draft.line: All right. Will you see to him?
dialog_draft.subtext: I am abandoning him to you because the rank requires it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Glances down at Avdeev, then away too quickly, flourishes the whip.

emotional_beats.beat: A wounded peasant soldier refuses comfort and gives it instead
emotional_beats.audience_feels: A small dignity in the face of being spent
emotional_beats.character_feels: Avdeev: lucid surrender; Panov: silent grief held in the hands

emotional_beats.beat: The officer leaves the dying man for the live appointment
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The cold arithmetic of empire arriving as good manners
emotional_beats.character_feels: Poltoratsky: relief disguised as duty

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The cost of empire is paid in another room from where it is announced
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Birch trunks, low slanting light; tin can-lid, papakha sheepskin in distance, bloodied cloak; muted greens and rust

inner_monologue_converted.original: Panov, severely frowning, brought Avdeev a can-lid full.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Panov frowns and pours; Avdeev's refusal lets Panov drink — the gesture carries the unspoken loyalty

adapted_scenes.transition: The cloak with Avdeev moves one direction through the birches; Vorontsov's chestnut moves the other — two parallel lines, one toward the fort, one toward Hadji Murad.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. SHALIN GLADE - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: A wide birch glade. Vorontsov sits his English chestnut at the center, adjutant and Chechen interpreter flanking. Sharpshooters thread silently into the brush at the rim. Hooves muffled in pine needles — Hadji Murad rides into the clearing on a white-maned horse, weapons gold-ornamented, turbaned, four murids behind him. He reins in three paces from Vorontsov. For a long moment neither moves; the forest settles. Hadji Murad addresses Vorontsov in Avar through the interpreter; he draws his palms slowly down his beard before speaking. Vorontsov dismounts first — the small courtesy of a host. He extends a hand, French-light. Hadji Murad considers it, then takes it. Their grip is brief, exact. Behind them, Poltoratsky reins up at the edge of the glade and stays mounted, watching.

dialog_draft.line: Hadji Murad. You are welcome here. You have my word.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will protect you as far as my rank allows — and we are about to discover together how short that distance is.
dialog_draft.action_before: Dismounts, removes a glove, steps toward the white horse.

dialog_draft.line: Sardar. Hadji Murad has come. It is well.
dialog_draft.subtext: The decision is made in my head; I have crossed the line and there is no further discussion.
dialog_draft.action_before: Draws his palms slowly down his beard, then dismounts.

dialog_draft.line: It is only a matter of arrangements now. My father's house will receive you, mon cher. You are quite, quite safe.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am converting a political prisoner into a personal guest because that is the only register I can decently inhabit.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lays a gloved hand briefly on Hadji Murad's shoulder, withdraws it.

dialog_draft.line: I am the Tsar's servant. Allah knows.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am performing the role required for survival; the mountains remain my only true allegiance — and this topic ends here.
dialog_draft.action_before: Looks past Vorontsov to the line of sharpshooters in the trees, then back.

dialog_draft.line: Splendid. We shall ride together to the fort.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am ending the formal address before either of us says anything that cannot be unsaid.
dialog_draft.action_before: Gestures toward his horse, voice lighter, almost too light.

emotional_beats.beat: Two men measure each other across an empire's seam, courteously
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The dread under polite ceremony — a treaty signed in glances
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad: calculating distances, escapes, and the cost; Simon Vorontsov: relieved, vindicated, and quietly aware he is already over-promising

emotional_beats.beat: The handshake — equal weight, unequal authority
emotional_beats.audience_feels: A door closing behind both of them
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad: the door of the mountains; Simon Vorontsov: the door of his own honour

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Mutual measuring disguised as hospitality — the moment surrender becomes a contract neither man fully owns
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Birch glade, sharpshooters dissolved into brush; white-maned horse, gold-ornamented weapons, English chestnut; cold filtered light; greens, bone-white, gold accent

inner_monologue_converted.original: Vorontsov, with difficulty repressing a smile of pleasure
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Voice becomes too light at 'splendid'; the over-pitch is the smile he is hiding

inner_monologue_converted.original: that smile struck Poltoratsky by its childlike kindliness — he had never expected the terrible mountain chief to look like that
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Poltoratsky stays mounted at the rim, watching: his face goes still — the assumption breaking in real time, no line needed

adapted_scenes.transition: The two horses turn together toward the fort road; the gold ornament catches once in the slanting light, then the glade is empty except for the sharpshooters quietly stepping out from the trees.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. FORT VOZDVIZHENSKAYA - ROAD - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: A column of five riders halts on the frozen road before the fort gate. Vorontsov rides out to meet them, white-gloved hand extended. Hadji Murad dismounts first, places his right hand on his heart, and speaks in Avar. The interpreter leans forward. Vorontsov listens without expression. When the hand is offered, Hadji Murad studies the gloved fingers a long beat before pressing them. Behind him, his four murids sit their horses in absolute stillness — Eldar broad and watchful, Gamzalo one-eyed and scarred, Khanefi holding a stud-horse by a taut lead, Khan Mahoma the only one who smiles. No weapons drawn, no words wasted. The men look at each other across an unbridgeable distance.

dialog_draft.line: I surrender myself to the will of the Russian Tsar. I wish to serve him. I wished to long ago — Shamil would not let me.
dialog_draft.subtext: This is not defeat; it is a tactical repositioning. The words are for the record, not the heart.
dialog_draft.action_before: Dismounts. Hand pressed flat to chest. Does not remove his papakha.

dialog_draft.line: You honor us. My father has spoken of you.
dialog_draft.subtext: He needs this more than HM does. The Vorontsov name needs the trophy.
dialog_draft.action_before: Extends gloved hand. Waits. Lets the pause breathe — he too understands ceremony.

dialog_draft.line: These men serve the Russians. As I do.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is claiming his murids as non-negotiable; they are not prisoners, they are his household.
dialog_draft.action_before: A slight turn toward his retinue. One open-palm gesture — presenting, not surrendering.

dialog_draft.line: They are welcome here.
dialog_draft.subtext: He means: within limits I have not yet disclosed.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nods toward the murids. Eye contact settles briefly on Gamzalo's scarred face, then moves away.

emotional_beats.beat: HM dismounts and formally surrenders — but on his own terms, with full ceremony
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Held breath — this is not submission, this is a chess move
emotional_beats.character_feels: Controlled: pride compressed into ritual; calculating every second

emotional_beats.beat: The gloved handshake — HM hesitates before pressing it
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The cost of this moment landing physically
emotional_beats.character_feels: Ambivalence: he is crossing a threshold he cannot uncross

emotional_beats.beat: Vorontsov nods to the murids; Khan Mahoma alone smiles back
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Brief human warmth in a cold transaction
emotional_beats.character_feels: Khan Mahoma: instinctive ease; the others: wary, unreadable

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Dignity preserved under surrender — a warrior entering captivity as if entering alliance
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Frost-bleached road, grey fort walls behind. Five riders backlit. White glove against dark sheepskin coat. Khan Mahoma's smile the only warmth in the frame.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Poltoratsky had never expected to see the terrible mountain chief look like that — he had expected a morose, hard-featured man
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Vorontsov's micro-expression of surprise when HM smiles: the gloved hand momentarily stills before extending

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad looked at the hand hesitatingly for a moment
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Two-second visible pause before the handshake — scripted beat, not edited away

adapted_scenes.transition: The creak of the fort gate opening carries into the next interior — stone walls closing around the sound of hooves

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. FORT VOZDVIZHENSKI — ROAD TO GATES — DAY
adapted_scenes.action: The column enters the fort road. Russian soldiers break from wood-felling lines and crowd the verge, staring. Simon Vorontsov raises a hand — his officer begins to shout them back, but Vorontsov waves the officer silent. Hadji Murad rides upright, eyes forward. Behind him: Khan Mahoma grins at the staring men; Eldar and Gamzalo flank HM like a wall. Gamzalo's single red eye passes across the faces — no contempt, no fear, nothing readable — then settles back on his horse's ears. One soldier points at Gamzalo and says something to his neighbor. HM catches the gesture. Without turning, the ghost of a smile moves through his eyes only.

dialog_draft.line: Do you know who that is?
dialog_draft.subtext: I want this name to land — I want them to feel what I've done today.
dialog_draft.action_before: Reins in beside the nearest infantryman at the road's edge, speaks slowly, in his rounded officer's Russian.

dialog_draft.line: No, Your Excellency.
dialog_draft.subtext: Wary — officers who single you out usually mean trouble.
dialog_draft.action_before: Straightens, axe still in hand, sawdust on his jacket.

dialog_draft.line: Hadji Murad.
dialog_draft.subtext: Just the name. Let it do the work.
dialog_draft.action_before: Pauses a full beat before speaking, watching the soldier's face.

dialog_draft.line: We've beaten him many a time, Your Excellency. And he's given us plenty back.
dialog_draft.subtext: Respect without submission — the man's a soldier too, and soldiers know soldiers.
dialog_draft.action_before: Glances past Vorontsov at HM's profile, then back.

dialog_draft.line: Let them look.
dialog_draft.subtext: This moment is mine — and theirs. It will travel.
dialog_draft.action_before: Half-turn to his officer, a small staying gesture.

emotional_beats.beat: Soldiers mob the road edge to see the famous enemy.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The weight of a name — this man has killed people standing in this very line.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad: controlled, performing dignity he does not feel.

emotional_beats.beat: Gamzalo's eye sweeps the crowd and returns to the horse's ears — unmoved.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Dread — this man is not afraid. He is measuring exits.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Gamzalo: no feeling shown. Internally cataloguing every face.

emotional_beats.beat: Vorontsov names HM to the soldier; the name produces a hush.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: HM is simultaneously trophy and threat — Vorontsov is proud, soldiers are unsure.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Vorontsov: satisfaction tinged with anxiety (Meller-Zakomelsky).

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The caged falcon displayed — honored captive, or prize of war?
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Amber afternoon light, sawdust floating, rough-coated soldiers against pale stone. Gamzalo's red beard catches the sun; his eye alone is shadow.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad smiled brightly with his eyes (Tolstoi: internal register of the soldiers' recognition).
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: The ghost of a smile through his eyes only — no change in posture, no acknowledgment. The body remains stone.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Vorontsov returned in a very cheerful mood (authorial summary of his pride).
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Vorontsov names HM to the soldier unprompted — the performance of ownership. His riding posture loosens after the exchange.

adapted_scenes.transition: Gamzalo's eye, fixed on the horse's ears, dissolves into the dark wood of Vorontsov's house door closing — the same stillness, different cage.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. VORONTSOV HOUSE - DRAWING ROOM - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: The drawing room: pale plaster walls, a French clock on the mantel, upholstered chairs that belong to Petersburg. HADJI MURAD stands at the center, papakha still on, filling the room with a stillness that the furniture does not understand. MARYA VASILEVNA, composed and curious, extends her hand. He takes it — briefly, formally — and a flush crosses his face before he masters it. A boy of six, BULKA, watches from beside his mother's skirt, wide-eyed, unblinking. Through the interpreter Hadji Murad speaks; the boy does not look away. SIMON VORONTSOV enters and finds his most feared warrior sitting with Bulka balanced on his knee, the boy's hand exploring the hilt of the kinzhal at Hadji Murad's belt. Hadji Murad rises at once, setting the child aside with a gentleness that surprises…

dialog_draft.line: Tell the prince: Hadji Murad is in his house. A kunak's house is sacred to him. His family is sacred.
dialog_draft.subtext: Establishing the terms of this arrangement on his own code — not Russian hospitality, adat.
dialog_draft.action_before: Places both hands flat against his chest, eyes on Vorontsov, speaking to the interpreter.

dialog_draft.line: If every time a kunak admired something you gave it to him — you would soon have nothing left at all.
dialog_draft.subtext: Testing how much of a man he is beneath the legend; flirting with danger at safe distance.
dialog_draft.action_before: Laughs, gesturing at the clock, the chairs, the room's inventory.

dialog_draft.line: Allah knows. A man keeps only what he does not show.
dialog_draft.subtext: He deflects the joke into philosophy — he will not play the exotic curiosity.
dialog_draft.action_before: Looks down at Bulka on his knee, adjusts the boy's grip on the kinzhal hilt without letting him unsheathe it.

dialog_draft.line: You will dine with us tonight, of course. My wife has been impatient to meet you.
dialog_draft.subtext: Covering his relief that the meeting went well — and his anxiety about Petersburg's reaction — with practiced grace.
dialog_draft.action_before: Enters, reads the scene in one glance, crosses to Marya Vasilevna's side.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad enters a world built to diminish him — European furniture, a woman's gaze, a child's curiosity — and holds his ground through ceremony alone.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Fascination; the tension of a hawk brought indoors.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Controlled vigilance; he reads every object and every face.

emotional_beats.beat: Bulka on his knee — the most powerful fighter in the Caucasus holding a child — reveals the human being the war has made invisible.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Unexpected warmth, followed by unease at how quickly it is suppressed.
emotional_beats.character_feels: A brief ease, then the discipline of hierarchy reasserts itself when Vorontsov enters.

emotional_beats.beat: Vorontsov enters to find his household already rearranged around this man.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The shift of power — Vorontsov is a guest in his own drawing room.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Pride mixed with the first cold premonition that this is larger than he planned.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Dignity under observation — the warrior submitting to domesticity without surrendering himself.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Pale room, dark figure. Kinzhal against upholstered chair. Boy's small hand on the blade. French clock ticking behind silence.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Vorontsov afraid his wife might be vexed with him for forcing on her this terrible stranger
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Vorontsov enters and reads the room in one glance — his relief visible as posture, not words.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad flushed when she held out her hand
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: A flush crosses his face; he masters it in the same breath — visible, then gone.

inner_monologue_converted.original: His smile pleased Marya Vasilevna
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: She watches him with the boy; her posture opens — she leans forward slightly, stops herself.

adapted_scenes.transition: The French clock chimes once — Hadji Murad does not look at it; Vorontsov does.
