adapted_scenes.slug: INT. VORONTSOV RESIDENCE - DRAWING ROOM / PRIVATE ROOM - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Hadji Murad rises the instant Simon Vorontsov enters, displacing Bulka from his knee in one controlled motion — the boy stumbles, surprised. HM's face shifts from warmth to formal gravity before Vorontsov has crossed the threshold. He sits only after the Prince sits. When Bulka presents the gifted dagger, Vorontsov draws the blade halfway, admiring it with rehearsed appreciation. The interpreter relays HM's sole request: permission to pray. Vorontsov summons a valet with a nod. Later, alone in his assigned room, HM locks the door with his gaze, tests its weight, counts the exits. He questions Eldar in rapid low whispers — horses, men, weapons. Eldar answers each query with two words. HM unclasps his belt, performs his prayers facing the qibla, then refastens his belt and sits cross-legged…

dialog_draft.line: Thy son, kunak?
dialog_draft.subtext: Testing the boundaries of this hospitality — how much warmth is real, how much performance
dialog_draft.action_before: Strokes Bulka's curly hair, eyes on Simon across the room

dialog_draft.line: A beautiful dagger. I thank thee.
dialog_draft.subtext: Accepting the gift also means accepting the obligation — he knows it
dialog_draft.action_before: Draws the blade halfway, turns it slowly in the light

dialog_draft.line: I want nothing. Only a place to say my prayers.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will not ask you for anything that lets you feel you hold me
dialog_draft.action_before: Eyes drop to the dagger as it slides back into its sheath

dialog_draft.line: Horses in the stable. Men in the barn. Arms not taken. Interpreter brings food.
dialog_draft.subtext: He has checked everything you would check. We are not yet prisoners.
dialog_draft.action_before: Enters the private room, closes door quietly, comes to stand before HM

dialog_draft.line: Good.
dialog_draft.subtext: Not reassured — that a cage is comfortable does not make it less a cage
dialog_draft.action_before: A single slow nod; reaches for the silver dagger Eldar carries

emotional_beats.beat: HM rises instantly for Vorontsov — public deference, private calculation
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Unease — we see the mask go on in real time
emotional_beats.character_feels: Alert, controlled; every gesture is deliberate armor

emotional_beats.beat: The dagger-gift exchange: generosity as mutual leverage
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Fascination at the ritual politeness concealing mutual suspicion
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: giving away a piece of himself to read the man; Vorontsov: pleased but aware of the cost

emotional_beats.beat: Alone at last — HM interrogates Eldar, then prays, then waits armed
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Dread; even the prayer feels like preparation for combat
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: acutely vulnerable beneath the controlled surface; trust extended to no one

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Elaborate civility as mutual threat assessment — two men measuring each other through ritual
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Warm salon lamplight vs. bare whitewashed private room; dagger blade catches both; HM's stillness on the divan absolute, belt buckled, eyes open

inner_monologue_converted.original: The better the reception, the less did Hadji Murad trust Vorontsov — he feared seizure, chains, Siberia, death
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: HM systematically interrogates Eldar about horses, men, weapons; sits armed on the divan rather than resting

inner_monologue_converted.original: His face altered when alone — pleased expression vanished, anxiety showed itself
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: The mask drops in a single beat as the door closes: HM's body changes — jaw tightens, shoulders drop their courtesy posture

on_the_nose_risk: Eldar's report lines are functional but spare — ensure delivery is clipped, not expository
adapted_scenes.transition: HM seated motionless on the divan, dagger across knees — cut to the interpreter's knock at the door summoning him to dinner

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. VORONTSOV RESIDENCE - DINING ROOM - AFTERNOON
adapted_scenes.action: Hadji Murad sits at the long dining table, rigid and watchful. Marya Vasilevna gestures to the pilau. He reaches past her portion, takes only from the side she has already served herself. Vorontsov produces his pocket watch, touches the spring — it chimes. Hadji Murad freezes. He reaches out slowly. Vorontsov sets the watch in his palm without a word.

dialog_draft.line: He takes from my side of the dish. Every time.
dialog_draft.subtext: He suspects poison — and he is right not to trust us.
dialog_draft.action_before: Leans to her husband, low, in French, eyes still on their guest.

dialog_draft.line: He will pray again at sunset. The fifth hour.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am showing him I pay attention — I want him to feel seen.
dialog_draft.action_before: Draws out the watch, presses the spring. It strikes four and a quarter.

dialog_draft.line: Again.
dialog_draft.subtext: This machine is strange power — I want to understand it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Looks at the watch. One finger extended toward it.

dialog_draft.line: Give it to him.
dialog_draft.subtext: A gift costs nothing and binds him to us.
dialog_draft.action_before: Touches her husband's sleeve, barely.

dialog_draft.subtext: He accepts — but the hand goes to his chest first, over his heart. The old courtesy. Not submission.
dialog_draft.action_before: Places right hand on chest, takes the watch, presses the spring three times, listens, nods once.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad eats only from Marya's side — silent accusation of distrust
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Uncomfortable recognition — the paranoia is rational
emotional_beats.character_feels: Vigilance, controlled fear beneath the stillness

emotional_beats.beat: The watch chimes — a gap opens between worlds
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Curious, gently comic — then moved by its strangeness
emotional_beats.character_feels: Genuine wonder, quickly disciplined into reserve

emotional_beats.beat: Vorontsov gives the watch; Hadji Murad receives it with the gesture of his own tradition
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Brief warmth — two men across an abyss, briefly touching
emotional_beats.character_feels: Gratitude held at arm's length — he will not owe this man his guard

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Provisional trust across an unbridgeable divide — every gesture charged with suspicion and counter-gift
adapted_scenes.visual_note: White tablecloth, brass watch catching afternoon light; HM's hands dark and scarred against the polished silver case; Eldar motionless at the door

inner_monologue_converted.original: He is afraid we shall poison him — he helped himself from where Marya took her helping
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Camera observes HM's deliberate reach past the untouched portion to Marya's side — no dialogue explains it

inner_monologue_converted.original: This evidently surprised Hadji Murad — the watch
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: HM's single extended finger, the freeze, the three test-presses of the spring — body does the surprise

adapted_scenes.transition: The watch chiming fades — a knock at the door cuts it off. An aide enters, hat under arm.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. MELLER-ZAKOMELSKY'S HEADQUARTERS - ANTEROOM - AFTERNOON
adapted_scenes.action: The aide-de-camp leads the group into a severe anteroom — plastered walls, a single oil lamp, a bench. Vorontsov and Marya Vasilevna stand. Hadji Murad and Eldar are directed to the bench. The aide-de-camp exits through the inner door. Hadji Murad does not sit. He stands at the bench, hands loose at his sides, reading the room the way a man reads terrain.

dialog_draft.line: The general is expecting you. A formality only.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is trying to soothe HM while managing his own dread of this meeting.
dialog_draft.action_before: Interprets through the aide, keeps his voice level, does not look at HM directly.

dialog_draft.line: I have waited before.
dialog_draft.subtext: I know what anterooms mean. Every power uses them the same way.
dialog_draft.action_before: A single glance at the closed inner door. Then he turns away from it.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad is directed to the bench like a petitioner — the bureaucratic machine shows its real face
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Dread — we see the trap tightening
emotional_beats.character_feels: Quiet fury beneath absolute stillness

emotional_beats.beat: He refuses to sit — stands at the bench, back straight
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Admiration — he preserves what he can
emotional_beats.character_feels: Pride as the last weapon

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Empire's contempt made spatial — the warrior reduced to a waiting room
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Bare plaster, single lamp, wooden bench; HM standing, Eldar half a step behind; the inner door heavy oak, no handle visible from this side

inner_monologue_converted.original: Meller displeased, required HM brought to him without delay — protocol assertion
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: The spatial arrangement: aide directs HM to bench, keeps him in anteroom — rank performed architecturally

adapted_scenes.transition: Inner door opens. Meller-Zakomelsky appears in the frame — uniform immaculate, expression calibrated to controlled displeasure.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. MELLER-ZAKOMELSKY'S HEADQUARTERS - STUDY / WAITING ROOM - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Two spaces, simultaneous. In the STUDY: Meller-Zakomelsky plants himself between desk and door, blocking Vorontsov from the room's center. The general does not offer a chair. He speaks at the window, not to Vorontsov. In the WAITING ROOM adjacent: Hadji Murad sits on a hard bench, papakha in his lap, listening to the muffled argument through the wall. He traces a finger along the bench's grain, patient as stone. In the study, Marya Vasilevna enters without knocking, her skirts loud against the floorboards — her presence cutting the argument the way a door cuts wind. She touches Meller's sleeve once. The general's jaw unclenches. Vorontsov extends his hand first. Through the wall, Hadji Murad hears silence replace the shouting. He sets the papakha back on his head and waits.

dialog_draft.line: All negotiations with the enemy pass through me. Why was I not informed?
dialog_draft.subtext: You made me invisible in my own command — I need that corrected on record.
dialog_draft.action_before: Stands at the window, back to Vorontsov, hands clasped behind him.

dialog_draft.line: His emissary came to me. He wished to surrender to no one else. I intended to report to you — there was no time to—
dialog_draft.subtext: The situation moved faster than protocol. I am not sorry for the result.
dialog_draft.action_before: Remains standing. Does not reach for a chair.

dialog_draft.line: I have not served twenty-seven years so that men who began yesterday, relying on family connections, should act under my nose.
dialog_draft.subtext: Your name got you here. My rank means nothing to you or to Petersburg.
dialog_draft.action_before: Turns from the window. Fixes Vorontsov with a look that measures him from collar to boots.

dialog_draft.line: Your Excellency, I ask you not to say things that are not correct.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will not let that stand — but I cannot afford to break with him.
dialog_draft.action_before: A small step forward. Voice level, barely.

dialog_draft.line: Come, Baron — Simon did not wish to displease you. You know what they say: a bad peace is worth more than a good quarrel.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am ending this before either of you does something irreversible.
dialog_draft.action_before: Enters, takes up the general's hand lightly, as if guiding a horse by the bridle.

dialog_draft.line: Very well. He stays here — under my jurisdiction — until I send him to the left flank commander.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will accept the peace, but the prize is mine to administer.
dialog_draft.action_before: Accepts Vorontsov's offered hand with one firm shake. Does not smile.

emotional_beats.beat: Meller asserts rank over Vorontsov as HM sits waiting
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The absurdity and indignity — two men argue ownership of a man in the next room
emotional_beats.character_feels: Meller: violated authority; Vorontsov: restrained contempt

emotional_beats.beat: Marya Vasilevna dissolves the standoff with a word and a touch
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Relief undercut by unease — nothing was resolved, only suppressed
emotional_beats.character_feels: Both men feel the humiliation of needing a woman to stop them

emotional_beats.beat: HM hears silence and replaces his papakha
emotional_beats.audience_feels: HM's fate decided without him — a man reduced to a case file
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: impassive readiness, but aware he is cargo

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: HM as administered object — the empire's machinery settling whose desk he lands on
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Study: dark wood, map on wall with pins, no natural light. Waiting room: bare bench, a single guard at the door. Papakha as HM's only prop — on and off marks his internal clock.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Vorontsov grew pale with excitement, expecting some rude expression
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Small step forward, voice held level — body braced for impact

inner_monologue_converted.original: The angry general capitulated to the enchanting laugh of the beauty
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: General's jaw unclenches at her touch; he accepts the handshake without warmth

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad waiting in the next room, unaware of outcome
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: HM traces bench grain, hears silence replace argument, replaces papakha — a man who knows how to wait for decisions made without him

on_the_nose_risk: Meller's '27 years' speech risks explaining rank-injury too directly — kept because Tolstoi's language is too precise to paraphrase, and the speech characterizes Meller's bureaucratic wound exactly
adapted_scenes.transition: The study door opens; a shaft of corridor light falls across the waiting-room floor — and across HM's boots. He does not look up.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. FORT HOSPITAL WARD - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: A bare wooden room. Four beds. Tallow candle throws unsteady light. Three patients watch as bearers lower AVDEEV onto the last empty bed. He groans once, loudly — then clamps it down, jaw tight. His hands press flat over the wound in his belly. His feet move in small restless arcs. Eyes fixed on the ceiling planks above him. The DOCTOR arrives, examines him quickly, orders him rolled to check the exit wound. Avdeev submits without sound. A long beat: the Doctor's face gives nothing away.

dialog_draft.line: Five shots. Just five.
dialog_draft.subtext: I took twenty in a raid and walked out. Why him?
dialog_draft.action_before: The soldier steps back from the bed, his bandaged hand pressed to his chest.

dialog_draft.line: Each man gets what fate sends.
dialog_draft.subtext: There is no justice here. Stop asking.
dialog_draft.action_before: The bearer sets down the stretcher pole and wipes his hands on his coat.

dialog_draft.line: Don't — don't fuss.
dialog_draft.subtext: I know what this is. Let me have some quiet with it.
dialog_draft.action_before: He reaches out and pushes the Hand-Wound Soldier's steadying hand away. Gently.

emotional_beats.beat: Avdeev is lowered onto the bed — ordinary, efficient, anonymous
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Dread at how routine this is
emotional_beats.character_feels: Avdeev registers his own situation without self-pity

emotional_beats.beat: The other patients watch; no one speaks of recovery
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The ward as a waiting room for death — empire's human ledger
emotional_beats.character_feels: Avdeev retreats inward, alone with his wound

emotional_beats.beat: Doctor examines, reveals nothing
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Helplessness — the institution cannot save him
emotional_beats.character_feels: Avdeev reads the silence

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The anonymous cost of empire — one man's life reduced to an entry in a ward log
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Bare planks, tallow light, four beds in a row. Avdeev's still hands over his belly. The candle flame trembles each time the door opens.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Avdeev trying to master his pain, looked fixedly before him
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Jaw tight, hands flat on wound, eyes locked on ceiling — no sound after initial groan

inner_monologue_converted.original: HM understood Vorontsov was important and Meller unimportant
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: CUT — this POV beat folded into prior HM scenes; redundant here

adapted_scenes.transition: The candle flame steadies as the ward goes quiet — cut to darkness, then distant torchlight on stone walls

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. FIELD HOSPITAL TENT - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Avdeev lies on a straw pallet, hands pressed over his stomach wound. The DOCTOR turns him onto his side; old flogging scars cross his back like a map. The doctor probes with a metal instrument, finds the bullet, leaves it. He plasters the wound and walks out without a word. Avdeev opens his eyes and looks around as if seeing a room he has never been in. PANOV and SEROGIN enter quietly, caps in hand. Panov sets his pipe on a crate and lays his palm against Avdeev's cold hand. A guttering candle on the floor throws low shadows up the canvas wall. Outside, the muffled clink of a horse's bit, distant Russian voices counting cartridges.

dialog_draft.line: What's this on his back?
dialog_draft.subtext: He is already filing the body — the man is paperwork now.
dialog_draft.action_before: Tilts Avdeev's shoulder, exposes the white scars.

dialog_draft.line: That was done long ago, your honor. For the money I drank. And there you are.
dialog_draft.subtext: Naming the punishment is easier than naming the wound; he is offering the doctor a story to file instead of a death.
dialog_draft.action_before: A long groan as he is rolled; his fingers find a thread on the blanket and pick at it.

dialog_draft.line: Petrukha. Have you no message for home?
dialog_draft.subtext: Treats him as a son, not a soldier — Panov has stopped soldiering and started witnessing.
dialog_draft.action_before: Crouches, sets the pipe aside without lighting it, takes the cold hand.

dialog_draft.line: Serogin... write so. 'Your son Peter has given orders that you should live long. He envied his brother — I told you about that today — and now he is himself glad. Don't worry him. Let him live. God grant it. I am glad.' Write that.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is asking, without asking, whether his life still belonged to anyone — and answering himself: it did, and it was small, and that is enough.
dialog_draft.action_before: Slow turn of the head, eyes finding Serogin's face.

dialog_draft.line: And your pipe — did you find it, brother?
dialog_draft.subtext: He has come too close to the truth and is stepping back from it; the pipe is the smallest, safest thing he can think to ask about.
dialog_draft.action_before: A pause; eyes returning to Panov; the smallest dip of the voice.

dialog_draft.line: Well then. Give me a candle to hold. That's how it is.
dialog_draft.subtext: He has accepted something he does not agree with — quietly, without ceremony.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lets go of Panov's hand; opens his palm upward on the blanket.

emotional_beats.beat: The doctor leaves the bullet inside the body and walks out.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Cold dread — the empire has already finished with him.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Avdeev: the small surprise of being abandoned mid-sentence by his own life.

emotional_beats.beat: Avdeev dictates a letter that pretends his death is a mercy his father will not have to grieve.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: A grief that is also a tenderness — he is protecting them from his own death.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Avdeev: relief, quiet pride at having something useful left to give.

emotional_beats.beat: He asks about the lost pipe before asking for the candle.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The smallness of a life on the way out — and its dignity.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Panov: a witnessing he cannot put into words; Avdeev: ordinary tenderness toward an ordinary man.

emotional_beats.beat: He asks for a candle to hold and closes his eyes.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The first named Russian death — a mirror laid down for the death that is coming.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Avdeev: small consent; Panov: a silence that will not heal.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The quiet, sideways tenderness of a man choosing how to be remembered while the empire that killed him is already filing him away.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Single candle, low canvas tent, straw pallet, white flogging-scars on a peasant back, an unlit pipe on a crate; cold daylight at the tent flap; muted browns and blacks, one warm flame.

inner_monologue_converted.original: He looked around as though amazed... he seemed to see not them but something else that surprised him.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Avdeev opens his eyes and scans the tent like a room he has never been in; eyes pass over Panov without recognition before they land.

inner_monologue_converted.original: His soul turns against it (Tolstoi's framing of the dying soldier's interior).
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He picks at a thread on the blanket; his fingers keep working a tiny task while everything else stops.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Avdeev, the unnoticed peasant whose death is a line in a dispatch.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: The doctor names the scars before he names the man; the bullet is left inside; the plaster is applied and the doctor walks out.

adapted_scenes.transition: Poltoratsky steps into the tent flap as Avdeev's hand opens; the pipe on the crate stays unlit — carries forward as the silent object of the next scene.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. CAMP - OUTSIDE HOSPITAL TENT - DUSK
adapted_scenes.action: Poltoratsky stands at the tent flap holding back the canvas with one gloved hand. Inside, the candle wavers in Avdeev's open palm. Panov's silhouette does not move. Poltoratsky lets the flap fall and walks a few steps into the camp. He takes a cigar from his case, looks at it, puts it back. A line of soldiers passes behind him with a stretcher, mud on their boots. He stops a young ORDERLY, opens his mouth to speak, then doesn't. He looks toward the mountains where the morning's expedition came from. The light is going.

dialog_draft.line: How goes it, my lad. Badly?
dialog_draft.subtext: He is using the lightest possible phrase because the heavier ones would crack his voice.
dialog_draft.action_before: Crouches at the pallet inside the tent; his polished boots out of place against the straw.

dialog_draft.line: Splendid fellow. Splendid.
dialog_draft.subtext: He cannot find the words a man of consequence is supposed to have here, so he reaches for the smallest, most useless one twice.
dialog_draft.action_before: Outside, alone now; his hand still half-raised toward the orderly who has already walked past.

dialog_draft.line: Sir?
dialog_draft.subtext: A simple question that exposes how empty the officer's hands are.
dialog_draft.action_before: Stops, turns, waits — too long.

dialog_draft.line: Nothing. Carry on.
dialog_draft.subtext: The first time tonight he means 'nothing' instead of 'splendid' — and it is the truer word.
dialog_draft.action_before: A small, tight nod; eyes flick away toward the mountains.

emotional_beats.beat: Poltoratsky cannot stay inside the tent and cannot leave the camp — he simply stops at its edge.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The Caucasus has stopped being a backdrop; we feel the floor tilt under him.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Poltoratsky: a sobering he does not yet have language for.

emotional_beats.beat: He puts the cigar back unlit.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: A small refusal of the salon mask.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Poltoratsky: shame that is not yet shame, only its first warning.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The Guards-officer mask is still on, but for the first time it does not fit; an empire's bookkeeping made flesh in one young commander's face.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Tent flap, single candle inside, polished officer's boots, mud, an unlit cigar; camp lanterns starting up; cold blue dusk against the dark line of mountains; brass and leather muted by the falling…

inner_monologue_converted.original: Poltoratsky's arc: bored, infatuated, half-playing soldier → sobered without being transformed; the Caucasus has stopped being a backdrop and become a thing that kills men he commanded.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He puts the cigar back unlit; he opens his mouth at the orderly and closes it; he looks toward the mountains where the morning came from.

adapted_scenes.transition: The unlit cigar in his hand rhymes with the unlit pipe on the crate inside — two small rituals refusing to begin; the dusk-line of mountains carries forward as the first frame of the Hadji Murat thread that will end the same way.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. FIELD HOSPITAL, VOZDVIZHENSK FORT - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: A wax taper is pressed between Avdeev's unresponsive fingers — they will not grip, so PANOV holds it upright for him. Avdeev's face: broad-cheeked, gone pale, utterly still. He does not look at Poltoratsky, who stands at the foot of the cot. He looks at the candle. The flame steadies. After a long moment Poltoratsky turns and leaves. Panov remains, holding the candle. He leans forward and listens at Avdeev's chest. Straightens. Sets the candle carefully on the cot ledge.

dialog_draft.line: Bring a candle. I am going to die.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is not asking for comfort — he is issuing his last directive, asserting the one act of will still open to him.
dialog_draft.action_before: Avdeev closes his eyes, then opens them and turns his head toward Panov — not toward Poltoratsky.

dialog_draft.line: How goes it, my lad. Badly?
dialog_draft.subtext: He already knows. The question is the only form of presence he knows how to offer.
dialog_draft.action_before: Poltoratsky steps inside, looks at the cot, looks at Panov, then back at Avdeev.

dialog_draft.line: It was in my gag.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is accounting for the candle — the small practical fact is all he has to offer against the size of what is happening.
dialog_draft.action_before: Panov produces the wax taper from his kit without being asked.

emotional_beats.beat: Avdeev ignores the officer and addresses only Panov — the man who will stay.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The hierarchy of the army dissolves at the moment of dying; only the fellow soldier counts.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Avdeev: clarity, no fear — a peasant's unsentimental acceptance.

emotional_beats.beat: Poltoratsky departs after five minutes. Panov stays and listens for the heart.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The cost of rank — Poltoratsky's exit is not cruelty but a different kind of helplessness.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Panov: the weight of witness, the necessity of staying to the end.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The dignity of an inconsequential death — one peasant soldier seen clearly by one other.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Single wax candle, lampblack shadows, whitewashed plank wall; Panov's large hands steady around Avdeev's slack fingers; Poltoratsky's silhouette at door.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Avdeev's dying wish and awareness of his death stated in narrative
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: His refusal to look at Poltoratsky, the deliberate turning toward Panov, the single imperative 'Bring a candle'

adapted_scenes.transition: Panov sets the candle down on the empty cot ledge — the flame burns in an empty frame as the sound of oat sheaves being beaten on frozen ground rises from silence.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT./INT. AVDEEV VILLAGE — WINTER DAY
adapted_scenes.action: The old man returns from town clutching a sealed document — not the sent letter, but a military notice. He stands in the doorway of the family izba, snow melting off his boots. The mother reads the clerk's seal and does not move. A long beat. Then she folds the notice with both hands as though folding laundry. Across the village, Aksinya — wrapped in black — wails loudly into her hands, neighbors gathering. Through the izba window we see the mother watch Aksinya's public grief, then turn away and pick up a bucket. She goes back to work.

dialog_draft.line: Which church do we go to for the requiem?
dialog_draft.subtext: She will not break. Grief is a task to be managed, not performed.
dialog_draft.action_before: She sets the military notice on the table without looking at it again.

emotional_beats.beat: The letter Peter never received returns as his death notice
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Hollowed — the bureaucratic formula extinguishes a man
emotional_beats.character_feels: The mother: numb duty over grief; Aksinya: genuine loss tangled with guilt

emotional_beats.beat: The mother watches Aksinya wail publicly, then resumes work
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Admiration and sorrow — different griefs, different dignities
emotional_beats.character_feels: The mother does not judge; she simply survives

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Empire erases a man with a clerk's phrase; the women who loved him find different silences
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Grey winter light, bare izba interior, the folded military notice on rough wood; Aksinya's black shawl against snow outside; mother's bucket — work as the only answer

inner_monologue_converted.original: The old woman wept for as long as she could spare time, and then set to work again
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Mother folds the death notice like laundry, asks about the church, picks up the bucket — work IS the grief

inner_monologue_converted.original: Aksinya lamented loudly when she heard of the death of her beloved husband
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Aksinya's public wailing seen through the window by the mother — contrast of two griefs

inner_monologue_converted.original: 'defending his Tsar, his Fatherland, and the Orthodox Faith' — the army clerk's formula
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: The military notice sealed with a bureaucratic stamp — the state's one-line erasure of a life

adapted_scenes.transition: The sound of Aksinya's wailing cuts abruptly — replaced by wind over open steppe, carrying into the next scene

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. VORONTSOV'S PALACE, TIFLIS - EVENING
adapted_scenes.action: A dust-caked courier enters the lamplit foyer, legs stiff from hard riding. A SERVANT takes the dispatch pouch and disappears. Vorontsov — silver-haired, erect, in black military coat with a single row of decorations — receives the sealed packet in the doorway of the dining room, thirty dinner guests visible behind him in candlelight. He reads standing, face unreadable. Then he raises his eyes to his wife, Elizabeth — composed, watching him from across the threshold. He gives a small, deliberate nod.

dialog_draft.line: It would seem our Caucasus has produced a surprise. Hadji Murad has crossed to our side.
dialog_draft.subtext: I now hold a piece the Tsar cannot ignore. This is my advantage.
dialog_draft.action_before: He folds the dispatch with two precise creases and passes it to the aide at his shoulder.

dialog_draft.line: Then we must receive him properly.
dialog_draft.subtext: I understand the political stakes — I will help you make this a performance.
dialog_draft.action_before: She turns from her guests toward him, smoothing nothing, voice below the room's murmur.

dialog_draft.line: Mon cher, we already are.
dialog_draft.subtext: The reception has begun the moment I chose to read this here, in front of witnesses.
dialog_draft.action_before: A faint inclination of the head toward the watching guests — acknowledging without addressing them.

emotional_beats.beat: Courier arrives mid-dinner; Vorontsov reads the news publicly
emotional_beats.audience_feels: We see the machinery of empire performing its rituals — power is always on display
emotional_beats.character_feels: Controlled satisfaction; calculation masked by elegance

emotional_beats.beat: The nod to Elizabeth — wordless conjugal shorthand
emotional_beats.audience_feels: These two have run this court together for years; she is not decoration
emotional_beats.character_feels: Partnership confirmed; she will manage what he cannot show

emotional_beats.beat: The final line — the reception IS the politics
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Vorontsov's civility is itself a weapon
emotional_beats.character_feels: Certainty; he has already begun shaping what Hadji Murad will become here

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Elegant power recognizing a prize — and immediately beginning to process it
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Candlelit dining room beyond glass doors; thirty faces turned; courier's dust against Vorontsov's pressed black; dispatch paper cream-white against dark coat

inner_monologue_converted.original: Vorontsov was in full possession of a facile, refined, and agreeable intellect which he used to maintain his power
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He reads the dispatch standing in the doorway — in view of all guests — then folds it with deliberate calm before speaking

inner_monologue_converted.original: He did not understand life without power and submission
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: His single nod to Elizabeth performs command; thirty guests read the room and fall quiet

adapted_scenes.transition: The dining room doors close on the gathered faces; silence — then, far off, the sound of a horse bell fading, the courier already gone

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. VORONTSOV PALACE - DINING ROOM - TIFLIS - EVENING
adapted_scenes.action: Candlelit banquet, thirty guests seated along a silver-laden table. Vorontsov takes his chair at center, adjusting the White Cross at his collar — a small, deliberate gesture. He leans forward and speaks. Conversation at the far end stops first, then the whole table falls silent. Footmen freeze mid-pour. The Carroty General sets down his fork. Every face turns toward Vorontsov.

dialog_draft.line: Simon a eu de la chance. Hadji Murad — Shamil's right hand — has come over to us. He will be in Tiflis within days.
dialog_draft.subtext: My son delivered me a political prize. This table should understand what that means for my command.
dialog_draft.action_before: Touches the White Cross briefly, then lifts his wine glass without drinking — letting the pause hold.

dialog_draft.line: Hadji Murad. Here.
dialog_draft.subtext: I know that name from another world — not the one at this table.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sets her spoon down. Looks at Vorontsov, not the general beside her.

dialog_draft.line: I have met him. More than once, Princess.
dialog_draft.subtext: I survived those meetings. I would rather not elaborate on the terms.
dialog_draft.action_before: Straightens. Wipes his mustache with the linen napkin, buying a moment.

dialog_draft.line: He will dine with us, I imagine. When he is ready.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is already mine to schedule — the threat is already archived.
dialog_draft.action_before: Drinks now, unhurried, as if the news were a weather report.

emotional_beats.beat: Vorontsov announces HM's defection to the assembled court
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Unease beneath the elegance — this is a predator announcing his catch
emotional_beats.character_feels: Controlled satisfaction; the news confirms his mastery, costs him nothing

emotional_beats.beat: The dining room falls silent
emotional_beats.audience_feels: HM's gravitational pull even in absence — the name alone stops the room
emotional_beats.character_feels: Guests recalibrate; the mountain war has just entered the dining room

emotional_beats.beat: The Carroty General's clipped answer
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The gap between salon gossip and battlefield reality
emotional_beats.character_feels: Guarded — survival memory surfaces under social decorum

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Imperial self-satisfaction masking the weight of what has been acquired
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Candlelight on silverware, White Cross at Vorontsov's throat, footmen frozen mid-pour — abundance stilled by a single name

inner_monologue_converted.original: for him alone not quite unexpected, because negotiations had long been going on
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Vorontsov drinks unhurried after announcing — the practiced ease of a man already past surprise

adapted_scenes.transition: The general's clipped 'more than once' hangs in the candlelit silence — cut to: HM's actual face for the first time, or the road to Tiflis at night

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. VORONTSOV PALACE - TIFLIS - EVENING
adapted_scenes.action: The dinner table glitters with crystal and candlelight. The Carroty General, carried away by his own tale, leans forward as he recounts Hadji Murad's ambush in the Dargo campaign. Vorontsov's smile stiffens almost imperceptibly. His fingers pause on the stem of his wine glass. The word 'rescue' falls a second time — the General does not hear what he has said. Around the table, guests stiffen. Manana Orbelyani cuts in smoothly, turning to the General with a question about his Tiflis quarters. The General blinks, derailed. Vorontsov's expression smooths back into graciousness — but his eyes have gone somewhere cold.

dialog_draft.line: It was he who cut the detachment in two at the rescue — if your Excellency will permit me to call it that.
dialog_draft.subtext: Oblivious to the social wound he is reopening; proud of his story, proud of Hadji Murad's cleverness.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sets down his fork and leans into the table, addressing Vorontsov directly.

dialog_draft.line: Where, did you say?
dialog_draft.subtext: He heard perfectly. He is testing whether the man will say the word again — and giving him one last chance not to.
dialog_draft.action_before: Turns his head slowly, squinting as though hard of hearing.

dialog_draft.line: At the rescue, your Excellency. He had us nearly encircled before the fresh troops arrived.
dialog_draft.subtext: Still oblivious — he admires Hadji Murad and wants credit for having witnessed greatness.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nods, pleased with his own precision.

dialog_draft.line: General — have you found comfortable quarters here in Tiflis? I hear the upper district is quite impossible this season.
dialog_draft.subtext: She is saving Vorontsov. This is a social rescue of her own — performed without acknowledgment.
dialog_draft.action_before: Touches her napkin to her lips and turns to the General with the smile of someone genuinely curious.

emotional_beats.beat: The General praises Hadji Murad's tactical brilliance — unwittingly indicting Vorontsov's command
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Secondhand embarrassment; fascination at the unaware damage being done
emotional_beats.character_feels: Vorontsov: controlled fury masked as pleasantry; General: pure enthusiasm

emotional_beats.beat: Vorontsov's false hearing — a trap the General walks into a second time
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Suspense: will the General realize? He doesn't.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Vorontsov: cold satisfaction that the man is a fool; also humiliated that the fool has power over him

emotional_beats.beat: Manana's intervention — the table rights itself
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Relief; respect for Manana's social intelligence
emotional_beats.character_feels: Vorontsov: gratitude he will never express; the General: mildly surprised but content to change subject

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The polished machinery of imperial court life — its capacity to absorb embarrassment and restore surface without ever touching truth
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Crystal and candlelight; Vorontsov's fingers on wine stem; long dinner table forcing distance between faces; Manana's decisive turn of the head

inner_monologue_converted.original: Vorontsov's face assumed an absent-minded and depressed expression — he knew the word 'rescue' carried its indictment
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Fingers pause on wine stem; smile calcifies; the false question 'Where?' — performed incomprehension as last warning

inner_monologue_converted.original: Some pretended not to notice, others nervously waited, a few exchanged glances and smiled
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Guests stiffen at table; a woman's hand stills on her glass; two officers share a glance — all held in one wide beat before Manana moves

adapted_scenes.transition: Vorontsov's eyes — cold beneath the candlelight — hold a beat on the General, then cut to: HM's own eyes, somewhere darker, somewhere else

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. VORONTSOV'S PALACE — WAITING ROOM — MORNING
adapted_scenes.action: A crowded antechamber. A BRISTLY-MUSTACHED GENERAL in full dress medals stands rigid near the door. An OFFICER'S WIDOW in black clutches a folded petition. A RUINED GEORGIAN PRINCE smooths his ornate costume. A FAIR-HAIRED AIDE-DE-CAMP emerges, calls a name, escorts one supplicant in, another out — a machine of small dignities. Then Hadji Murad enters. White Circassian coat, silver lace at the collar, the high turban. Every head turns. Whispers move through the room like wind through grain. He stands still under their gaze, neither seeking approval nor fleeing it.

dialog_draft.line: The prince will see you directly.
dialog_draft.subtext: You are not like the others. The rules bend for you.
dialog_draft.action_before: He approaches Hadji Murad, bypassing the waiting line without a word of explanation to the others.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad enters the empire's anteroom and every petitioner recognizes him instantly
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The asymmetry — these supplicants wait hours; he is seen. But also: he is still waiting, like them.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Composed alertness. He reads the room as a soldier reads terrain.

emotional_beats.beat: The aide-de-camp moves him to the front of the queue
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The empire's hierarchy is arbitrary — favor flows to the spectacular, not the deserving
emotional_beats.character_feels: Neither gratitude nor pride. He expected this. It is tactical, not personal.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The spectacle of imperial power sorted by usefulness — Hadji Murad among petitioners, yet apart from them
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Low morning light through tall windows. Medals, black mourning cloth, embroidered silk — a taxonomy of need. Hadji Murad's white coat catches the light differently than everything around him.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad enters the waiting room with his brisk though limping step; he heard his name whispered from various parts of the room
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: His controlled entry under a room full of eyes; the aide-de-camp moves to him immediately, bypassing the queue

adapted_scenes.transition: The aide-de-camp opens the cabinet door; the muffled sound of Vorontsov's voice carries out — cuts to the inner room

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. VORONTSOV'S CABINET, TIFLIS PALACE - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Sunlight cuts through green venetian blinds in long bars across an enormous oak table. Hadji Murad enters in a white cherkesska, the dagger at his belt, his lame gait making the walk a slow ceremony. He stops three paces from the table, places his small sunburnt hands on his chest where the white coat overlaps, and lowers his eyes. Vorontsov, white-haired, stands behind the table, gloved hands resting on a stack of dispatches; he does not smile. Prince Tarkhanov takes position at the corner of the table, half-step behind Hadji Murad. A clock ticks. Outside, a horse is led across gravel. Vorontsov gestures the smallest welcome with two fingers. Hadji Murad begins to speak in Kumyk, slow, distinct.

dialog_draft.line: Sardar. Hadji Murad places himself under the protection of the great Tsar and of yourself.
dialog_draft.subtext: I name you my patron because no other door is open. Watch what I withhold.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lowers his eyes, hands flat on his chest over the white coat.

dialog_draft.line: He places himself under the protection of the great Tsar and of Your Excellency.
dialog_draft.subtext: I render only the words; the meaning is between these two.
dialog_draft.action_before: Half-step forward, voice neutral, eyes on Vorontsov.

dialog_draft.line: Vorontsov. Shamil is my enemy and yours. I will serve in faith to the last drop. Allah knows.
dialog_draft.subtext: The door is closed on this question. Now move.
dialog_draft.action_before: Draws his palms slowly down his beard.

dialog_draft.line: Mon cher Hadji Murad. Such resolve, one might suppose, deserves a resolve to match it. Quite, quite.
dialog_draft.subtext: I have heard you and I will give you nothing today.
dialog_draft.action_before: Repeats the name softly, as if tasting it; lays one glove flat over the dispatches.

dialog_draft.line: My mother. My wives. My son Yusuf. They are in the imam's hand.
dialog_draft.subtext: Every hour you delay, a knife moves nearer to my son.
dialog_draft.action_before: His voice drops a fraction; the hands on his chest still.

dialog_draft.line: I shall write to Petersburg, mon ami. You are entirely free here, of course. Is it not so?
dialog_draft.subtext: You are placed in a cage of paperwork. You are entirely watched.
dialog_draft.action_before: Folds his hands. The smile is held one beat too long.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad delivers the formal submission without once raising his eyes from the parquet.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: the ceremonial weight of a man placing his life on a table he does not trust
emotional_beats.character_feels: calculation behind ritual courtesy

emotional_beats.beat: The two men's eyes meet over the interpreter's voice and a wordless exchange passes — recognition that neither believes the other.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: the chill of two civilities understanding each other exactly
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad measures the room and the guard; Vorontsov measures the prize

emotional_beats.beat: Vorontsov defers with French softeners; Hadji Murad names his son and the room narrows to that name.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: dread that the bureaucracy has already won
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad — grief held thin as wire; Vorontsov — pleasure at the cleanness of his trap

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Two stillnesses recognizing each other across an empire's table — courtesy as the first cut.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Green venetian-blind bars across oak; white cherkesska against dark wood; one inkwell, one stack of dispatches; cold Tiflis daylight; gloves on the table; dagger hilt low and visible.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Their eyes expressed much that could not have been put into words; without words they told each other the whole truth.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: A held look across the table while the interpreter's neutral voice continues — the men still, the room ticking.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Vorontsov did not believe a single word Hadji Murad was saying.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: A glove laid flat over the dispatches; the smile held one beat too long; the deferral 'I shall write to Petersburg.'

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad's lame gait and shorter leg.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: The slow swaying entrance across parquet — ceremony made of an injury.

adapted_scenes.transition: Vorontsov's gloved hand closes over the stack of dispatches as Hadji Murad turns; the soft click of the cabinet door carries into gravel under hooves outside.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. VORONTSOV'S RECEPTION CHAMBER - TIFLIS - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: A high-ceilinged room, curtains half-drawn against afternoon glare. VORONTSOV sits in a carved chair behind a low table; an INTERPRETER stands to his right. HADJI MURAD stands opposite — he does not sit despite Vorontsov's repeated gesture toward the divan. They hold each other's gaze through the interpreter's voice as through a pane of glass: every translation arrives a half-beat late, softening nothing. When Vorontsov finally speaks the word 'pardon,' Hadji Murad's jaw tightens almost imperceptibly. He presses both hands flat to his chest before answering. When Vorontsov asks about Shamil, Hadji Murad turns to face the shuttered window — then turns back, closes his mouth into a hard line, and sits only when Vorontsov asks a third time.

dialog_draft.line: Our sovereign is as merciful as he is mighty. I shall write to Petersburg on your behalf. Until then — you are my guest.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am placing you in administrative suspension. Petersburg will decide what to do with you; I merely hold the cage.
dialog_draft.action_before: Vorontsov sets down his tea glass. Studies Hadji Murad the way a naturalist studies a specimen.

dialog_draft.line: In 1839 I served the Russians faithfully. Akhmet Khan lied to the general. That is what drove me out.
dialog_draft.subtext: I need you to understand I was not the one who broke faith — the record must be corrected before any deal can hold.
dialog_draft.action_before: Through the interpreter, hands pressing chest. Not pleading — stating facts for the record.

dialog_draft.line: I know, I know. And Shamil — how exactly does he intend to revenge himself on Shamil?
dialog_draft.subtext: Your personal grievances interest me only as operational intelligence. Give me something useful.
dialog_draft.action_before: Vorontsov gestures again toward the divan. His tone does not change between 'I know' and the question about Shamil.

dialog_draft.line: Akhmet Khan is dead. I cannot reach him now. But Shamil lives. I will not die before I have settled that account.
dialog_draft.subtext: My war is not with Russia. Russia is the instrument. Shamil is the target.
dialog_draft.action_before: Hadji Murad turns to face the window. A long pause. He turns back. His brow contracts — the only visible sign of the weight behind the words.

dialog_draft.line: Very well. Send him to the Lesghian line — let us hear the full proposal.
dialog_draft.subtext: This may be useful. I will listen without committing.
dialog_draft.action_before: Vorontsov writes a brief note. Does not look up as he speaks.

emotional_beats.beat: Two men speak truths at each other through an interpreter — neither believes the other, both proceed as though they do
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Unease; the civility is a performance on both sides
emotional_beats.character_feels: Vorontsov: controlled assessment; Hadji Murad: disciplined self-restraint masking fury

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad refuses to sit — then finally sits on the third invitation
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Respect for his pride; sorrow that he must concede even this
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad: each concession costs something; Vorontsov: notes it, files it

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad names Shamil as his real target
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Clarity — now we understand the deal's true engine
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad: naked ambition and grief compressed into a single sentence; Vorontsov: recalibrates the man's usefulness

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Two forms of calculation meeting across a language barrier — mutual distrust armored in mutual courtesy
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Afternoon light through half-shuttered windows; Vorontsov in dark frock coat, Hadji Murad in sheepskin papakha — two opposing silhouettes. Tea glass, ink, divan as props of enforced civility.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Vorontsov's eyes said that he did not believe a single word Hadji Murad was saying
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Vorontsov's deliberate note-writing while Hadji Murad speaks — attention without trust

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad: 'That old man ought to be thinking of his death and not of war'
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Hadji Murad's gaze on Vorontsov's silver hair before answering — a beat of appraisal, then the mask back on

adapted_scenes.transition: Vorontsov's quill scratches the note — the sound carries into the next scene as he seals the letter

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. TIFLIS THEATER / VORONTSOV RESIDENCE - EVENING / NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: The theater glitters — chandeliers, bare shoulders, champagne. Hadji Murad enters the stalls beside Loris-Melikov, papakha on, turban prominent. He sits, watches the Italian opera with the patience of a man waiting out weather. At interval he rises without explanation, scans the audience with undisguised calm, and walks out. The next evening: Vorontsov's salon, louder, warmer, the wives worse undressed by mountain standards. Princess Vorontsova finds Hadji Murad near the wall and delivers pleasantries through the interpreter. Loris-Melikov translates. Hadji Murad answers briefly, in Avar. Vorontsov, across the room, watches without moving closer.

dialog_draft.line: Her Excellency asks whether the music was to your liking last evening.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am smoothing an awkward encounter — please give me something to work with.
dialog_draft.action_before: Loris-Melikov steps close, voice low, framing it as a private exchange between allies.

dialog_draft.line: Tell her the hall was bright.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will not perform appreciation I do not feel. I have noticed everything and said nothing that matters.
dialog_draft.action_before: Hadji Murad holds his tea glass without drinking, eyes moving to the door as he answers.

dialog_draft.line: He says it was magnificent, Your Excellency.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am the space between two worlds and I must keep both civil.
dialog_draft.action_before: A fractional pause before translating — Loris-Melikov adjusts the word.

dialog_draft.line: Tell our guest that the evening is his — no obligations tonight.
dialog_draft.subtext: I need him socialized, not defiant. This display must look like hospitality.
dialog_draft.action_before: Vorontsov approaches, unhurried, glass in hand, a host touring his own rooms.

dialog_draft.line: The prince is generous with his evenings. My family has no evenings.
dialog_draft.subtext: Remind you again — none of this pageant touches the one thing that matters.
dialog_draft.action_before: Hadji Murad turns from the dancers and meets Vorontsov's eyes directly for the first time in the room.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad in the theater — present, dignified, entirely elsewhere
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Disquiet — a hawk in a gilded cage
emotional_beats.character_feels: Contempt held absolutely still; strategic patience

emotional_beats.beat: Loris-Melikov translates 'bright' as 'magnificent'
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Complicity — we see the gap between what HM says and what the empire hears
emotional_beats.character_feels: Loris-Melikov: the cost of his position made visible

emotional_beats.beat: HM's last line — family has no evenings
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The salon deflates; the champagne suddenly irrelevant
emotional_beats.character_feels: Vorontsov: a micro-flinch he will not show; Hadji Murad: bare, accurate, unapologetic

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Civilized captivity — the mountain warrior on display in the empire's drawing room, unbroken, unreachable
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Chandeliers vs. papakha; bare-shouldered women vs. belted cherkesska; Loris-Melikov in the gap between them, always slightly turned to both

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad's 'Oriental Mohammedan dignity' in the theater — internal impassivity
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Sits still, leaves without ceremony, scans the audience like a commander reading terrain

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad's indifference to the half-dressed women — authorial observation
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He holds his tea glass without drinking; eyes go to the door, not the dancers

on_the_nose_risk: HM's line about family is direct — balanced by its brevity and the physical beat (eye contact) that carries the weight
adapted_scenes.transition: The sound of the salon orchestra fades; HM's last words hang in silence — cut to the bare room where he sleeps that night, the city noise outside, nothing of his own on the walls.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. HADJI MURAD'S QUARTERS - TIFLIS - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: A sparse room. A low divan, a writing table, two chairs. Loris-Melikov sits with a leather notebook open on the table, pen in hand. Hadji Murad sits opposite on the divan, hands resting on his knees, papakha set to one side. He listens to Loris-Melikov finish speaking. A long pause. Then Hadji Murad straightens his back slightly — not a refusal, not a concession — and begins.

dialog_draft.line: The Prince has read what others have written about you. He wants to hear it from you. I will write it down. It goes to the Emperor.
dialog_draft.subtext: Your story is already half-owned by the state. This is formality — and a leash.
dialog_draft.action_before: Opens the notebook to a clean page, smooths it flat with his palm.

dialog_draft.line: What the Prince commands, I will do. Ask what you wish to know.
dialog_draft.subtext: I give you words. What I keep is mine.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sits still for several seconds, eyes on the middle distance, then brings his gaze to Loris-Melikov.

dialog_draft.line: Begin at the beginning. How did you come to be what you are.
dialog_draft.subtext: I need the version that flatters neither you nor Shamil — I need a usable story.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lifts the pen but does not write yet. Waits.

dialog_draft.line: Allah knows where a man begins. I will tell you what I remember.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will give you the shape of the story. The truth inside it is my own.
dialog_draft.action_before: Presses his palms together once, then rests them on his knees.

emotional_beats.beat: Loris-Melikov frames the interview as a service to Hadji Murad — the Prince, the Emperor, the record
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The machinery of empire closing gently around a man
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad registers the trap's elegance without flinching

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad agrees — but his compliance is controlled, not yielding
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Tension between two men who respect each other across an unbridgeable distance
emotional_beats.character_feels: Loris-Melikov feels the weight of what he is being trusted to transcribe

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad's final line opens the biographical frame — the past will now enter the film
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Anticipation; a door swinging open onto another world
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad settles into himself, armored by memory

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A man consenting to be recorded — surrendering the form of his story while keeping its substance
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Sparse whitewashed walls; morning light; open notebook, blank page; pen hovering; HM's still hands against the divan's dark fabric

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad never interrupted anyone but always waited to see whether his interlocutor had not something more to say
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Hadji Murad holds stillness after Loris-Melikov speaks — a visible pause before answering, eyes settling before he meets the gaze

adapted_scenes.transition: Hadji Murad's voice begins — and we hear it over an image that is not this room

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. TIFLIS QUARTERS - DAY (STORY WITHIN STORY)
adapted_scenes.action: Loris-Melikov sits with an open notebook. Hadji Murad takes a stick from beside the divan and draws a small knife — ivory-handled, gold-inlaid — and begins to whittle. Shavings fall to the floor as he speaks, not looking at Loris-Melikov. His hand moves with the knife; his voice moves with the memory. He stops whittling. He touches his own side, pressing two fingers against his ribs as if locating a scar that is not there — his mother's scar, located by memory on his own body.

dialog_draft.line: Thou must tell me everything. From the beginning. Where thou wast born. Where thou didst live.
dialog_draft.subtext: I need usable intelligence, but this ritual of asking matters too — he must feel heard.
dialog_draft.action_before: Loris-Melikov opens his notebook, uncaps his pen, waits with formal patience.

dialog_draft.line: There is much to tell. Many events have happened.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am not sure you can hold what I am about to give you.
dialog_draft.action_before: Hadji Murad looks at the stick in his hands. Then picks up the knife.

dialog_draft.line: Born in Tselmess. A small aoul — the size of an ass's head, as we say in the mountains. Not far from Khunzakh, where the Khans lived. Our family was closely connected with them.
dialog_draft.subtext: This is the geography of everything I am.
dialog_draft.action_before: First shaving falls. He does not look up.

dialog_draft.line: My mother nursed the Khan's sons. She reared Umma Khan. When I was born — when the Khansha bore Bulach Khan — my father ordered her to go again as wet-nurse. She refused. She said: I should again kill my own son, and I will not go.
dialog_draft.subtext: She chose me. She is the only one who ever chose me without wanting something in return.
dialog_draft.action_before: The knife slows. The whittling becomes more deliberate, almost ceremonial.

dialog_draft.line: My father struck her with a dagger.
dialog_draft.subtext: This is the first violence. Everything after is in its shadow.
dialog_draft.action_before: He stops whittling. Sets knife and stick on the divan beside him. Presses fingers against his own ribs.

dialog_draft.line: The song she composed — you must tell that too.
dialog_draft.subtext: I almost said nothing. But if I say nothing I will lose the thread.
dialog_draft.action_before: Loris-Melikov has stopped writing. He watches Hadji Murad's hand pressed against his side.

emotional_beats.beat: HM begins to tell his life — the intimacy of the Khan's household, his mother's milk, the wet-nurse arrangement
emotional_beats.audience_feels: We are entering HM's interior world for the first time through his own voice
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM is neither boasting nor confessing — he is laying down a map

emotional_beats.beat: HM describes his father's violence toward his mother
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The origin wound arrives without warning, in the same flat tone as geography
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM has told this before to no one. The flatness is the cost of carrying it.

emotional_beats.beat: HM presses fingers to his own ribs — his mother's scar, located on his body
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The physical gesture makes the bond between mother and son visceral, indelible
emotional_beats.character_feels: She survived. She sang about it. He carries the scar by proxy.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The origin wound — the first violence that made Hadji Murad who he is, carried in his body as someone else's scar
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Ivory-handled knife, curling wood shavings on stone floor; afternoon light through a single window; HM's hand pressed flat against his own ribs

inner_monologue_converted.original: He remembered how his mother had laid him to sleep beside her under a fur coat on the roof of the saklya, and he had asked her to show him the place in her side where the scar of her wound was still visible.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: HM presses two fingers against his own ribs — locating his mother's scar on his own body. Loris-Melikov stops writing and watches.

adapted_scenes.transition: Loris-Melikov's pen scratches across paper — the sound carries into the next beat, steady as a metronome against whatever comes next

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. TIFLIS GUESTHOUSE - LORIS-MELIKOV'S STUDY - DAY (FRAME) / EXT. KHUNZAKH PALACE ROOFTOP - NIGHT (FLASHBACK)
adapted_scenes.action: Loris-Melikov sets down his pen and waits. Hadji Murad stares past him at the window — not at anything outside. After a long pause he begins to speak in a low murmur, and as he does the room seems to contract: we are on a rooftop in Khunzakh, a boy of five lying against his mother under a fur coat. The woman lifts the edge of her blouse and places the boy's small hand on a puckered scar. Back in the study: Hadji Murad's hand rests flat on the table, fingers slightly spread, pressing down as if feeling something beneath the wood.

dialog_draft.line: You must tell everything. It is necessary.
dialog_draft.subtext: The more you give me, the more I can protect you — but also the more I own.
dialog_draft.action_before: Loris-Melikov pushes the ink-pot aside, clasps his hands — signaling the pen is down, the record is paused.

dialog_draft.line: My mother was wounded. She sang of it.
dialog_draft.subtext: I carry her wound the way she carried me — it shaped me before I could choose.
dialog_draft.action_before: A long silence. Hadji Murad turns his palm upward on the table, examines it.

dialog_draft.line: My white bosom was pierced by bright steel — but I laid my dear boy close upon it, till his body was bathed in my blood. She did not fear death. She taught me the same.
dialog_draft.subtext: My courage is not mine. It is hers. I am reciting a debt I cannot repay.
dialog_draft.action_before: Eyes still unfocused, voice dropping to near-silence — the words arrive as if he is hearing them, not speaking them.

dialog_draft.line: She is in Shamil's hands now. She must be rescued.
dialog_draft.subtext: Everything I have done — coming here, tolerating you, this room, these questions — is for her.
dialog_draft.action_before: He refocuses abruptly, eyes finding Loris-Melikov's face for the first time since he began speaking.

dialog_draft.line: I grew up in the Khans' palace. I was like a brother to them. Abu Nutsal Khan. Umma Khan. Bulach Khan — the youngest. Shamil threw him over a cliff. But that came later.
dialog_draft.subtext: These names are a roll call of the dead. I rehearse them so I do not forget whose blood Shamil owes.
dialog_draft.action_before: He picks up a small ceramic cup from the tray between them, turns it in his fingers without drinking.

dialog_draft.line: And Hamzad — when did he enter?
dialog_draft.subtext: I need the sequence. Give me a wound I can use.
dialog_draft.action_before: Loris-Melikov reopens his notebook slowly, not looking down — watching Hadji Murad's hands.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad recites his mother's lullaby-wound-song
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The depth of his loyalty is revealed — it is not political, it is maternal and pre-rational.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Grief held in ceremony; the song is the only form in which he permits himself tenderness.

emotional_beats.beat: He names the three Khan brothers, ending with Bulach's death
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The massacre is approaching in the story — the audience understands these names as gravestones.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Each name is a small act of witness — keeping them alive through speech.

emotional_beats.beat: He refocuses on Loris-Melikov after naming his mother's captivity
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The purpose behind the oral history is suddenly visible — this is not confession, this is negotiation.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Calculating — he gives Loris-Melikov just enough to be useful.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Debt — the unpayable debt a son owes a mother, and the way that debt becomes the engine of everything that follows.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Warm amber lamplight on dark wood. The ceramic cup turning in HM's hands. Loris-Melikov's open notebook, pen resting across it — ink not flowing.

inner_monologue_converted.original: He remembered how his mother had laid him to sleep beside her under a fur coat on the roof of the saklya, and he asked her to show him the scar of her wound.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: HM's hand pressing flat on the table — fingers spread — as if he is feeling the texture of something beneath it. The gesture externalizes tactile memory without narration.

inner_monologue_converted.original: He remembered the fountain, the lean dog, the smell of lepeshki, his wrinkled grandfather hammering silver.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: CUT — these childhood micro-memories are pure interiority and cannot be individuated on screen without a montage that dilutes the scene's focus. The mother's scar and the lullaby carry the full emotional weight.

on_the_nose_risk: Line 'She must be rescued' is direct statement of intent — justified here as it marks HM's shift from memory to mission, the pivot of the scene.
adapted_scenes.transition: Loris-Melikov's pen descends to the page — the scratch of nib on paper carries into the next scene as the first sound of Hamzad's world.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. HADJI MURAD'S QUARTERS - TIFLIS - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Hadji Murad sits across from Loris-Melikov, hands resting on his knees, eyes closed. He speaks without looking at the interpreter — the story comes from somewhere deeper than this room. His fingers press together, then release, a slow rhythm. Loris-Melikov writes nothing for a moment. He sets down his pen. The story is pulling him in.

dialog_draft.line: The Russian general did not receive us. He sent officers. They played cards with Umma Khan. They made him drunk. When I dragged him out, he had nothing left.
dialog_draft.subtext: The Russians showed their contempt without a word — and I saw clearly what we were to them.
dialog_draft.action_before: Opens his eyes. Looks past Loris-Melikov, at the wall, as if seeing Tiflis again.

dialog_draft.line: And that is why you changed your mind? Because of Rosen?
dialog_draft.subtext: He is trying to locate the turning point — find it, report it, contain it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Picks up his pen again. Poises it above the page.

dialog_draft.line: No. There was another reason.
dialog_draft.subtext: The real wound is not political. It is older and harder to name.
dialog_draft.action_before: A long pause. He closes his eyes again.

dialog_draft.line: Near Tselmess. A murid. I shot him. He was still alive when I came to take his weapons. He looked at me and said — 'Thou hast killed me. I am happy. But thou art a Mussulman. Join the Ghazavat. God wills it.'
dialog_draft.subtext: The dying man's words lodged in him and never left — not as command but as splinter.
dialog_draft.action_before: He speaks slowly, as if weighing each word against what it costs to say it.

dialog_draft.line: And did you join?
dialog_draft.subtext: He already knows the answer. He asks because the story demands it be said aloud.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sets the pen down again. Watches Hadji Murad's face.

dialog_draft.line: I did not. But it made me think.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is saying: a man I killed judged me. And he was not wrong.
dialog_draft.action_before: Opens his eyes. Meets Loris-Melikov's gaze for the first time in the scene. Then looks away.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad recounts Russian indifference — Rosen's refusal, Umma Khan's corruption
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Cold contempt for imperial condescension
emotional_beats.character_feels: Old humiliation, precise and unhealed

emotional_beats.beat: The dying murid's words — the moment that planted doubt
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The weight of a death that did not end cleanly
emotional_beats.character_feels: Guilt held in the body, not the mind

emotional_beats.beat: HM and Loris-Melikov exchange a look — the story has cost something to tell
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The intimacy between a man who watches and a man who is watched
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: exposed but not broken; Loris-Melikov: unsure what to write

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A man remembers the moment he first doubted himself — and cannot say whether the doubt was faith or failure
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Sparse room, midday light, dust in the air; Loris-Melikov's blank page; HM's hands still as stone while his voice moves

inner_monologue_converted.original: HM's internal shift toward Ghazavat after Tiflis visit
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Reported as closed-eye oral narration; Loris-Melikov's pen going still marks the weight

inner_monologue_converted.original: The dying murid's spiritual appeal registers in HM's conscience
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: HM slows his cadence, closes his eyes mid-sentence; the pause before 'I did not' carries the unresolved guilt

on_the_nose_risk: Dying murid's line is direct quotation from source — kept because it is reported speech within oral narration, not on-the-nose dialogue in real time
adapted_scenes.transition: Hadji Murad's gaze drops to his hands — cut to those hands, older, bloodied, at the Khunzakh massacre

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. KHANSHA'S CHAMBER, KHUNZAKH PALACE - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: A young Hadji Murad stands at the window, hand on his dagger, watching the courtyard below where horses are being saddled. The Khansha sits stiff-backed on a low divan, her veil pushed back, fingering a string of amber beads. Abu Nutsal Khan paces the carpet in his ceremonial cherkesska, fastening a silver-chased belt. A servant girl pours tea no one drinks. The Khansha lets the beads click — once, twice — then stops. Outside, distant murid singing rises and falls. Hadji Murad turns from the window. Abu Nutsal will not meet his eyes.

dialog_draft.line: Sardar. The first brother went. The second went. A third does not return what the first two could not bring back.
dialog_draft.subtext: It is a trap. Bulach is already a hostage; Umma is already lost.
dialog_draft.action_before: Steps closer to Abu Nutsal, lowers his voice so the Khansha will not hear; his hand stays on the dagger hilt.

dialog_draft.line: I see thou art afraid.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will use the only weapon I have left — his shame — because I cannot bear to be wrong about Umma.
dialog_draft.action_before: She lifts the amber beads, lets one click against the next, eyes fixed on her son's belt buckle, not his face.

dialog_draft.line: Afraid? I am not afraid. Saddle the bay.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am terrified. If I stay, I become her, and she is already a corpse.
dialog_draft.action_before: His face floods red; he turns from his mother and speaks past her, to the servant — the command is for himself.

dialog_draft.line: Then I ride at thy stirrup.
dialog_draft.subtext: I cannot stop this; I can only refuse to let you die alone.
dialog_draft.action_before: Draws his palms slowly down his beard, exhales once, then walks to the door without waiting for permission.

emotional_beats.beat: The mother weaponizes shame; the son cannot survive being called a coward in his own house.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Dread — we already know how this ends; we are watching a door close.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Khansha: brittle pride masking panic. Abu Nutsal: humiliated, performing courage. HM: quiet, calculating loss.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad's warning is heard and ignored — his loyalty surrenders to a duty he cannot refuse.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The first weight of the guilt that will define him.
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: the cold registration of an oath that cannot be broken even when it leads off a cliff.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Pride choosing extinction over the appearance of fear.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Cold daylight through latticed windows. Amber beads, silver belt-chasing, cooling tea. Color palette: bone-white plaster, dark walnut, dull gold; deep red cherkesska as warning.

inner_monologue_converted.original: I saw there was treachery and told her not to send him; but a woman has as much sense in her head as an egg has hair.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: HM's quiet warning to Abu Nutsal — bypassing the mother — and his beard-stroke of resignation when the order is given anyway.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Like a bee she knew where to sting him most painfully.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: The amber beads click once, twice; the line 'I see thou art afraid' lands like a verdict, his face floods red.

adapted_scenes.transition: The bay horse's hoofbeats on stone carry into the next scene; the amber beads click once more on the empty divan.
