adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. HAMZAD'S CAMP, RIDGE BELOW KHUNZAKH - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Hamzad's banners crest the slope. A ring of murids in dark cherkesskas wheels around the arriving party, firing into the sky, their voices rising in war-song. Hamzad himself comes forward on foot — a courtesy reserved for kings — and lays his hand on Abu Nutsal Khan's stirrup. Shamil stands two paces behind him, motionless, beard untouched, eyes on Hadji Murad. Abu Nutsal dismounts. Hamzad gestures him into the great tent. Hadji Murad makes to follow. Shamil steps once into his path — no word, no hand raised — and Hadji Murad stops. The tent flap falls. Hadji Murad is left holding the bridles of two horses on the empty hillside. Wind. The murids' singing thins. Then — from inside the tent — the muffled, unmistakable crack of pistol fire. Two shots. A pause. A third. The horses shy. Hadji…

dialog_draft.line: Be at peace in my tent, Khan. I have done thy house no harm and wish to do none.
dialog_draft.subtext: You are already sentenced. The hospitality is the cage.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lays his palm flat on the stirrup-leather, voice level, eyes never leaving Abu Nutsal's; the hand does not lift until the Khan dismounts.

dialog_draft.line: Sardar — I follow.
dialog_draft.subtext: I do not trust this courtesy. I will not let him cross that threshold alone.
dialog_draft.action_before: Swings down from his horse, gathers both bridles in one hand, takes a single step toward the tent flap.

dialog_draft.line: Thou hast not been asked.
dialog_draft.subtext: You are not Khan. You are witness. Stay where you are placed.
dialog_draft.action_before: Strokes his beard once, slowly, then steps into HM's path; he does not raise a hand — the stillness is the command.

dialog_draft.line: It is well.
dialog_draft.subtext: I see what is about to happen and I cannot stop it. Allah knows what I am about to fail to prevent.
dialog_draft.action_before: Holds Shamil's gaze a long beat, then looks down; his palms draw slowly down the length of his beard, the bridles tight in his fist.

emotional_beats.beat: The lethal courtesy: hospitality formula spoken over a sentence already passed.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Cold recognition — every word of welcome is a lock turning.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hamzad: the calm of a verdict. Abu Nutsal: a young noble swallowing his terror to enter the tent like a Khan.

emotional_beats.beat: Shamil silences Hadji Murad without violence — the first time HM is made small by the Imamate.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The architecture of his future captivity, built in a single look.
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: a wound being scored that will never close — he obeyed, he stayed, he heard the shots.

emotional_beats.beat: The shots offstage. The wound that defines a life.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: A door slamming on a world. The birth of Hadji Murad's blood-debt.
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: grief that has nowhere to go yet — only the slow gesture of the hand on the beard.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The sealing of a guilt that will outlive every other allegiance.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: High mountain light, banners in motion, murid cherkesskas in deep indigo and black. Tent of bleached cream wool. After the shots: stillness, two trembling horses, wind in the empty grass; no blood…

inner_monologue_converted.original: He said to me, 'Thou hast not been asked. It was the Khan!'
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Shamil's single line plus the silent step into HM's path; HM's stoppage without protest is the entire submission.

inner_monologue_converted.original: I was some way down the hill when I heard shots fired in Hamzad's tent.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: HM left holding bridles; the offstage cracks of pistol fire; the slow palm down the beard — the kill is heard, never seen.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hamzad received him well and sent to invite the two elder brothers also.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Hamzad's hand on the stirrup — the king-courtesy that masks the trap.

adapted_scenes.transition: Wind in empty grass after the third shot; the bridles in HM's fist rhyme forward into the bridle of his own horse decades later, when he rides toward the Russians.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. TIFLIS QUARTERS - LORIS-MELIKOV'S STUDY - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: Hadji Murad sits rigid. His sunburnt face flushes deep red as he reaches the final words — he ran. The admission drops into silence. He does not look at Loris-Melikov. Then, after a long pause, he draws from his breast pocket a gold repeater watch, presses the spring. Twelve chimes and a quarter ring out. The absurd delicacy of the sound breaks the weight in the room. HM turns the watch in his fingers, studying it like a man studying something that does not belong to him. In the adjacent room, visible through an open doorway, Gamzalo sits cross-legged on a burka, plaiting a bridle in fast, tight movements. Khan Mahoma stands over him, teeth showing, talking in low rapid Avar. Eldar is at the saddle, sleeves rolled, polishing leather in slow strokes. Three men who wait without waiting.

dialog_draft.line: I was seized with fear. I ran away.
dialog_draft.subtext: The only time in his life he fled — and he has carried it ever since.
dialog_draft.action_before: Stops mid-sentence. His jaw tightens. Eyes go to the middle distance.

dialog_draft.line: I thought thou never wast afraid.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is testing — not mocking. He wants to know if this man breaks.
dialog_draft.action_before: Does not write. Sets down his pen on the notebook without looking at it.

dialog_draft.line: Never after that. Since then I have always remembered that shame — and when I recalled it, I feared nothing.
dialog_draft.subtext: The cowardice became the engine. Shame is the only fuel that never runs out.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lifts his eyes to Loris-Melikov for the first time since the confession.

dialog_draft.line: It is time for me to pray.
dialog_draft.subtext: Enough. I have given you enough of myself for one night.
dialog_draft.action_before: Presses the watch spring. Listens to the chimes with a faint, unguarded smile.

dialog_draft.line: Kunak Vorontsov's present.
dialog_draft.subtext: An acknowledgment — not gratitude, not contempt. Just: I know what this is.
dialog_draft.action_before: Holds the watch up briefly toward Loris-Melikov, as if offering him a look.

dialog_draft.line: It is a good watch. Pray, then. I will wait.
dialog_draft.subtext: He understands: the interview is over for the night. He will sit with what he has heard.
dialog_draft.action_before: Takes up his pen. Opens the notebook. Does not yet write.

emotional_beats.beat: HM confesses the one moment of cowardice in his life — running from Khunzakh as Abu Nutsal died
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Shock — this man ran. Then recognition: it made him everything he became.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Exposed. The shame is not past — it is present, alive, functional.

emotional_beats.beat: HM reframes the cowardice as the source of all subsequent fearlessness
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The paradox lands: the wound became the armor.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Not pride — clarity. This is simply what happened to him.

emotional_beats.beat: The watch chimes — absurd, beautiful, out of place
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Tonal relief, then unease: a Russian gift marks time in a Avar warrior's pocket.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Something childlike surfaces briefly. He lets it show.

emotional_beats.beat: Loris-Melikov glimpses the murids through the doorway — three men simply existing
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The domesticity of it is quietly devastating: these men will die with him.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Loris-Melikov: the weight of what he is recording settles in.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Shame as foundation — the one flinch that became a lifetime of steel
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Amber oil-lamp. Gold watch in dark hands. Open doorway to murid room — leather, burka, three men in wordless occupation. No excess light.

inner_monologue_converted.original: HM flushed dark red and his eyes became bloodshot
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Face flushes visibly; he does not look at Loris-Melikov; jaw tightens before he speaks

inner_monologue_converted.original: Loris-Melikov guessed the speakers were Hadji Murad's murids
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He crosses to the open doorway and observes — camera follows his sightline into the murid room

inner_monologue_converted.original: Childlike smile when the watch strikes
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Unguarded half-smile, quickly contained — visible to Loris-Melikov, noted but not named

adapted_scenes.transition: Loris-Melikov writes in his notebook. His pen scratches. The sound continues under cut — into silence of the murid room, where Gamzalo's hands keep working without pause.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. HADJI MURAT'S QUARTERS - ANTEROOM - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Loris-Melikov enters the anteroom. Khan Mahoma grins wide, already extending a hand. Gamzalo sits motionless across from him, polishing a dagger hilt. Loris-Melikov produces a silver cigarette-case. Khan Mahoma's eyes light up — he takes one, glances at Hadji Murat's closed door, then lights it, blowing smoke with exaggerated care. Gamzalo watches, sets down the dagger, and walks out without a word.

dialog_draft.line: Eh — Gamzalo says Shamil is a saint. A holy man. A great dzhigit.
dialog_draft.subtext: I want to entertain our guest at Gamzalo's expense.
dialog_draft.action_before: Takes the cigarette, winks conspiratorially at the closed bedroom door.

dialog_draft.line: Mansur — now he was a saint. When Mansur rode through the aouls, people came out and kissed the hem of his coat. Shamil? Shamil is a lamorey.
dialog_draft.subtext: I define holiness by results — and Shamil's results don't impress me.
dialog_draft.action_before: Blows smoke badly, lips pouted, watching Gamzalo's back.

dialog_draft.line: Lamorey means mountaineer. It is in the mountains that the eagles dwell.
dialog_draft.subtext: You will not diminish him with a word I can turn back on you.
dialog_draft.action_before: Pauses at the doorway. Does not turn around.

emotional_beats.beat: Khan Mahoma smokes in defiance of house rules, performing ease for the Russian visitor
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Wry amusement — and a flicker of unease at the gap between the murids
emotional_beats.character_feels: Khan Mahoma: delight in small rebellion; Gamzalo: quiet contempt for both the smoking and the audience

emotional_beats.beat: Gamzalo's exit — the dagger set down, one devastating line, then silence
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Respect for Gamzalo's economy; the room feels smaller without him
emotional_beats.character_feels: Gamzalo: loyalty is not a debate; Khan Mahoma: pleased his adversary landed the better line

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Division within loyalty — two murids who serve the same master and cannot agree on what loyalty means
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Anteroom: saddle on nail, cigarette smoke drifting toward closed door, silver case catching light; Gamzalo's back in doorframe

inner_monologue_converted.original: Gamzalo severely disapproves of smoking and leaves
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Gamzalo sets the dagger down deliberately, delivers one line, exits without waiting for response

adapted_scenes.transition: Gamzalo's receding footsteps; door to inner room remains shut — HM's presence implied by absence

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. TIFLIS COURTYARD - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: A fire burns low in the center of a courtyard. The four murids sit around it, each apart from the others — not a circle of fellowship but four separate orbits. Eldar sits upright, back straight, cleaning a rifle with slow methodical strokes. Gamzalo squats closest to the flame, face unreadable, a cartridge belt across his knees that he neither counts nor adjusts. Khan Mahoma whittles a stick, blowing shavings off his knife with small puffs, glancing at the Russian quarters windows. Loris-Melikov stands in the shadow of an archway, notebook open, writing nothing. He watches. Hadji Murad is absent — inside, sleeping or praying. The fire pops. No one speaks. A Russian sentry passes the far gate without looking in.

dialog_draft.line: Eh — Russian bread again tomorrow. White, soft. Like something a sick man eats.
dialog_draft.subtext: Testing whether anyone shares his discomfort, probing if Gamzalo will bite
dialog_draft.action_before: Khan Mahoma flicks a wood shaving into the fire, watches it catch

dialog_draft.line: It is enough.
dialog_draft.subtext: Stop complaining. We are alive. The mission holds. Do not weaken it with words.
dialog_draft.action_before: Gamzalo does not look up from the cartridge belt

dialog_draft.line: For you, Gamzalo, stones are enough. Allah gave you a simple appetite.
dialog_draft.subtext: Deflecting with humor; genuinely unsettled by Gamzalo's immovability
dialog_draft.action_before: Khan Mahoma grins, tilts his head toward Eldar — looking for an ally

dialog_draft.line: The horses are watered. I will sleep by them.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am not part of this conversation. My post is my answer.
dialog_draft.action_before: Eldar sets the rifle across his lap, looks at Khan Mahoma without expression, then back at the rifle

dialog_draft.line: Both of you. Like stones. Inshallah.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is lonely here. Neither of them will meet him.
dialog_draft.action_before: Khan Mahoma looks at the Russian windows, the smile fading

dialog_draft.line: If God wills.
dialog_draft.subtext: That is the only answer that matters. Everything else is noise.
dialog_draft.action_before: Gamzalo finally looks up — not at Khan Mahoma, but at the dark arch where Loris-Melikov stands

emotional_beats.beat: Khan Mahoma's joke lands in silence — Gamzalo doesn't engage, Eldar departs mentally
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The isolation of the group within their own ranks; four men who share a loyalty but not a language of comfort
emotional_beats.character_feels: Khan Mahoma: masked loneliness; Gamzalo: vigilance mistaken for coldness; Eldar: duty as the only identity available

emotional_beats.beat: Gamzalo's final glance at Loris-Melikov — the only acknowledgment the Russian observer exists
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Low-grade threat; Gamzalo has clocked the watcher and filed it away
emotional_beats.character_feels: Gamzalo: controlled suspicion; Loris-Melikov: the discomfort of being seen by the man who never speaks

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Solidarity made of silence — four men bound by a single oath that none of them can articulate aloud
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Low firelight; four pools of shadow, each self-contained. Gamzalo's face copper-lit. Loris-Melikov's notebook pale in the arch. No stars visible — overcast.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Tolstoy's portrait-by-portrait description of each murid's character and inner disposition
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Posture, spatial distance, and behavior at the fire: Eldar's methodical cleaning = pure function; Gamzalo's stillness = grim readiness; Khan Mahoma's whittling and glances = restless sociability

inner_monologue_converted.original: Loris-Melikov's observational role as interpreter/recorder
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He stands in the arch with an open notebook, writing nothing — his observation is the scene's framing device, not narration

adapted_scenes.transition: Gamzalo's eyes hold on the archway; Loris-Melikov closes the notebook without having written — the soft click of the clasp carries into the interior where Hadji Murad lies awake

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. TIFLIS QUARTERS - ANTEROOM - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Loris-Melikov moves through the anteroom where Gamzalo sits on the floor oiling a rifle, not looking up. Loris-Melikov tries a question — Gamzalo answers without raising his eye from the barrel, a single growled syllable. Loris-Melikov turns. Khanefi enters carrying a sack of rice, hairy forearms straining. Loris-Melikov blocks his path with a quiet question. Khanefi sets the sack down, straightens, and holds Loris-Melikov's gaze without blinking — then speaks. From the next room: Hadji Murad's voice calls out. Eldar appears in the doorway, wipes his hands on his coat, nods once toward the inner room.

dialog_draft.line: You don't find it dull, staying indoors?
dialog_draft.subtext: Tell me something. Give me something to work with.
dialog_draft.action_before: Crouches slightly to bring himself to Gamzalo's level — a gesture of courtesy that lands as condescension.

dialog_draft.line: No.
dialog_draft.subtext: You are not worth more than one word.
dialog_draft.action_before: Blows a thread of oil from the rifle's sight. Does not look up.

dialog_draft.line: How long have you been with him?
dialog_draft.subtext: I need your story — I need proof of loyalty, or treachery.
dialog_draft.action_before: Steps toward Khanefi, who has gone still, rice sack at his feet.

dialog_draft.line: Five years. My father killed his uncle. They wished to kill me. I asked to become his brother.
dialog_draft.subtext: The debt is older than your army. You cannot measure it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sets the sack down slowly. Looks straight at Loris-Melikov — not defiant, simply factual.

dialog_draft.line: Brother — what does that mean, exactly?
dialog_draft.subtext: I cannot parse this. I need categories.
dialog_draft.action_before: Takes out his notebook, then thinks better of it, closes it.

dialog_draft.line: Two months — no shaving, no cutting of nails. Then I came. His mother gave me her breast. That is all.
dialog_draft.subtext: There is no metaphor here. This is the bond. It is not translatable.
dialog_draft.action_before: Picks the rice sack back up. Waits — done answering, ready to work.

emotional_beats.beat: Gamzalo refuses even minimal engagement
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The wall around these men is absolute
emotional_beats.character_feels: Gamzalo: contempt. Loris-Melikov: unsettled.

emotional_beats.beat: Khanefi explains the milk-brotherhood with complete plainness
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Something old and bodily and unanswerable has been stated
emotional_beats.character_feels: Khanefi: no emotion — this is simply fact. Loris-Melikov: inadequacy; his categories don't fit.

emotional_beats.beat: Eldar appears — the call from inside ends the scene
emotional_beats.audience_feels: HM commands without being seen; his gravity organizes everything
emotional_beats.character_feels: Loris-Melikov: reminded of his role as visitor, not interrogator.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The impossibility of translating loyalty — Loris-Melikov faces bonds that pre-date and outlast any state.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Dim anteroom, oil-smell and rice-dust. Gamzalo's rifle disassembled on cloth. Khanefi's arms bare to the elbow. Loris-Melikov's closed notebook.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Loris-Melikov's suspicion that Gamzalo's open hatred betrays the murids' true intentions
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Loris-Melikov's notebook gesture — reaching for it, then putting it away — shows the gap between his analytical instinct and the reality before him.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Khanefi described internally as strong, hard-working, obedient
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He carries the rice sack, sets it down to answer, picks it back up — the action performs the description.

adapted_scenes.transition: Eldar's gesture toward the inner room — Loris-Melikov straightens and walks toward the light beyond the doorway.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. TIFLIS HOUSE - DRAWING ROOM - EVENING
adapted_scenes.action: Hadji Murad sits cross-legged on a low divan, a cup of cooling tea untouched at his knee. Loris-Melikov sets aside an open notebook, dips his pen, meets HM's eye. In the doorway behind them: Eldar, motionless, hand resting on the hilt of his dagger; further off, Khan Mahoma stretches by the samovar like a cat, grinning at the steam. HM lifts the cup, sets it down again without drinking. Loris-Melikov pulls the notebook back across his knee. The room narrows to two men, a pen, and a story. Outside the shuttered window, a Russian sentry's footsteps pass and recede.

dialog_draft.line: Khan Mahoma — a jolly fellow. I have been in to have a talk with thy henchmen. The young one, Eldar — I liked him. Quiet. He watches the door even while he stands still.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am taking the measure of your men so I know what to write down for the Prince. Do not mistake my pen for blindness.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sits, sets notebook aside, meets Hadji Murad's eye.

dialog_draft.line: Loris-Melikov. Sit. Eldar is young. Made of iron. He has not yet had to bury his own.
dialog_draft.subtext: Naming you first as protocol. While I tell stories on this divan my mother and Yusuf are still in Vedeno. Carry that to the sardar.
dialog_draft.action_before: Settles deeper on the divan, hands quiet on his knees.

dialog_draft.line: Eh — the prince's tea is hot, his bread is white. A man could forget where his saddle is.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am watching every door in this house; I have not forgotten anything.
dialog_draft.action_before: Grins, stretches by the samovar.

dialog_draft.line: Where the forelegs go, the hind legs must follow. So it was with Hamzad's house. Aseldar struck the Khansha from behind. A man who kills women from behind teaches his master the same hand. Shamil learned it well.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am telling you what kind of man holds my son. Move your sardar, or Yusuf goes over a precipice like the Khan's youngest.
dialog_draft.action_before: Eyes flat on Loris-Melikov.

dialog_draft.line: Permit me — 'where the forelegs have gone, the hind legs must follow.' Say it again, brother. I want it on the page exactly so. Not for the Prince alone — for the record itself.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will not soften your law into Russian comfort. Whatever they decide about you, your truth will outlast the man who files it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Switches to Tatar, dips the pen, lowers his voice.

dialog_draft.line: Allah knows the rest. Shall I go on, or has the sardar heard enough for one evening?
dialog_draft.subtext: I am closing this door. The next words must come from your side — troops, or I am wasting breath I owe my dead.
dialog_draft.action_before: Draws his palms slowly down his beard.

emotional_beats.beat: Loris-Melikov frames the recital as observation; HM frames it as transaction.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Quiet dread — both men know this is a deposition disguised as hospitality.
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: cold patience. Loris-Melikov: dawning unease at what he is being asked to record.

emotional_beats.beat: HM names Aseldar's blow from behind — the proverb lands as a verdict on Shamil.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The room tightens; tea stops being tea.
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: grief routed through doctrine. Loris-Melikov: the bureaucrat receding behind the man.

emotional_beats.beat: HM closes the door with 'Allah knows the rest' — the next move belongs to the Russians.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The weight of an unanswered demand.
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: every word a coin spent against Yusuf's life.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A man dictates his blood-feud as a warning that the sardar must read between the lines.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Lamp pooled on the notebook; Eldar a vertical shadow at the door; Khan Mahoma's grin in the samovar's curve; HM's untouched cup; pen-nib catching light.

inner_monologue_converted.original: HM's grief for his captive son and dead brother filters through the recital
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Untouched tea cup, palms drawn slowly down beard, eyes flat on the listener — never named in dialogue

inner_monologue_converted.original: Loris-Melikov's discomfort at being made an instrument of the Prince
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Switch from Russian to Tatar mid-page, voice lowered 'for the record itself' — the bureaucrat briefly dissents through diction

adapted_scenes.transition: HM's voice carries on as image cuts back fifteen years — same proverb, different room: a mosque, drawn swords.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. KHUNZAKH MOSQUE - DAY (FLASHBACK, 1834)
adapted_scenes.action: Cool stone, dust in shafts of light. YOUNG HADJI MURAD (early 30s) and his brother OSMAN stand in burkas at the back wall, pistols hidden, daggers ready. Hamzad enters with thirty murids, swords drawn, ASELDAR at his shoulder still spotted with the Khansha's blood. Aseldar shouts, points: 'Off the burkas.' He strides toward HM. HM draws the dagger and drives it up under the ribs in one motion; Aseldar drops. Osman fires once — Hamzad staggers, turns dagger-first toward Osman. HM crosses the floor and finishes Hamzad with a single blow to the head. The murids close in. Osman falls under three swords. HM keeps his back to a pillar, dagger working, blood on his sleeve to the elbow. The frame holds on him alone among the dead — younger face, same stillness as the man on the divan.

dialog_draft.line: The Ghazavat asks one hand, one sword. Whoever stands apart from it stands against God — and I do God's work.
dialog_draft.subtext: Submit by sundown or share Khansha's rafter. Verdict already signed.
dialog_draft.action_before: Enters the mosque, voice flat, weighted, final.

dialog_draft.line: Hadji Murad has said it. For the blood of the Khans.
dialog_draft.subtext: Naming himself in the third person — this is no longer a man speaking, it is an oath.
dialog_draft.action_before: Drops the burka. Dagger already low at his thigh.

dialog_draft.line: Brother — now.
dialog_draft.subtext: Two against thirty; the time for waiting is over.
dialog_draft.action_before: Cocks the second pistol.

dialog_draft.line: Allah knows the rest.
dialog_draft.subtext: If we die here, we die in the right.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sets his back to the pillar, dagger up.

emotional_beats.beat: Hospitality formula meets ambush — the proverb from the divan becomes blood on stone.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Vertigo: the calm storyteller and the boy in the mosque are the same throat.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Young HM: vow over fear. Hamzad: certainty cracking too late.

emotional_beats.beat: Osman falls; HM survives alone — the moment that made the man.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Awe and dread in equal measure.
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: a grief that will not be wept here, only carried.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The act that earned Hadji Murad his name — and the vendetta that has never closed.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Dust in stone-cut light; burkas dropping like shed skin; one streak of Khansha's blood already on Aseldar's blade; HM's sleeve darkening; Osman's open eyes; pillar he sets his back to.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Tolstoi's reported speech: 'I gave him a finishing blow on the head'
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Single dagger-strike across the floor; no line of dialogue narrates it — the act IS the line

inner_monologue_converted.original: Authorial summary: 'They killed my brother Osman, but I kept them at bay'
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Osman's open eyes; HM's back to the pillar, blood to the elbow — final image holds without voiceover

adapted_scenes.transition: Cut on young HM's stillness against the pillar to old HM's stillness on the divan — same eyes, fifteen years apart; Loris-Melikov's pen scratches into the silence.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. KHUNZAKH FORTRESS COURTYARD - DAY (FLASHBACK, 1834)
adapted_scenes.action: Russian soldiers wrestle Hadji Murad to his knees beside an iron cannon. They wind chains around his wrists and lash the chains to the gun-carriage. Akhmet Khan watches from the gallery, hands folded inside his sleeves. A scribe presents him a sealed paper — Rosen's old commission to Hadji Murad — and Akhmet Khan slowly tears it in half. Hadji Murad does not struggle. He looks once at Akhmet Khan, then draws his palms down his beard. A soldier kicks dust over his boots. Behind him, on a low table, lies the discarded turban; a guard nudges it off the table with a rifle butt. Days pass in cuts: water refused, sun, frost, the cannon's shadow rotating across his face.

dialog_draft.line: Let it be known. The son of Hitsl Mahoma has donned the turban of Shamil.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am rewriting his loyalty into treason because he refused my house.
dialog_draft.action_before: Holds up the torn commission so the courtyard can see it.

dialog_draft.line: The law of the mountains forgives no man twice.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will have him killed before any general's letter can save him.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lets the two halves of the paper fall onto the dust at Hadji Murad's knees.

dialog_draft.line: Akhmet Khan. Hadji Murad has not turned.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will not plead, and I will not be named what I am not.
dialog_draft.action_before: Looks up once, voice low, then draws his palms slowly down his beard.

dialog_draft.line: Allah knows.
dialog_draft.subtext: This conversation is finished. The next words will be in another place.
dialog_draft.action_before: Turns his face away from Akhmet Khan toward the cannon, eyes settling on the rope at his wrist.

emotional_beats.beat: A commission from a Russian general, the only paper that protected him, is torn in front of him.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Outrage at the manufactured charge; recognition that paper does not bind a khan's vendetta.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Cold, clear understanding that no patron will save him.

emotional_beats.beat: The discarded turban becomes evidence by being declared evidence.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The chill of how identity is weaponized.
emotional_beats.character_feels: A controlled fury, displaced into the slow gesture along his beard.

emotional_beats.beat: Six days of stillness in chains.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Compressed dread; he is already calculating.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Patience as strategy; the cliff is forming in his mind.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Betrayal as bureaucratic theater — the moment Hadji Murad learns that Russian protection is paper and Avar enmity is iron.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Iron cannon, torn commission paper, the loose turban on a low table, ochre dust, chains heating in sun then frosted at dawn — palette of rust, parchment, slate.

inner_monologue_converted.original: He believed me to be the cause of this... he hated me and sent his henchmen to kill me.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Akhmet Khan tears the commission himself — public theatre replaces internal grievance.

inner_monologue_converted.original: He said that I had donned a turban -- this one (Hadji Murad touched his turban).
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: The turban already lies discarded on a low table; a guard knocks it to the dust as Akhmet declares the charge.

adapted_scenes.transition: The cannon's long shadow rotates across his face one last time, then a chain rasps free — match-cut to forty rifles slung against the morning.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. MOUNTAIN PATH ABOVE MANSOKHA RAVINE - DAY (FLASHBACK)
adapted_scenes.action: Forty Russian soldiers march Hadji Murad in single file along a cliff path, his hands roped at the wrists, a soldier's musket-strap fixed to his bound arms. The path narrows. To their right the rock falls away into a hundred-and-twenty-yard drop; mist coils below. Hadji Murad slows, measures the abyss with one slow look, then with his bound hands seizes the strap of the soldier behind him and steps off the edge, dragging the man with him. Two bodies fall together against pale sky and stone. Far below, the mist closes. Hold on the empty path; the remaining soldiers freeze, one crosses himself. Slow descent of the camera's gaze through the ravine: the soldier sprawled on rock, motionless. Beside him, Hadji Murad alive, bones broken, dragging himself with one elbow toward a crevice in the…

dialog_draft.line: On the seventh day they untied me. Forty rifles. Hands bound.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am telling you what no Russian record will tell you — count the men, count the guns.
dialog_draft.action_before: In the audience chamber, eyes on the candle, voice flat as a ledger entry.

dialog_draft.line: The path narrowed. To the right, the abyss.
dialog_draft.subtext: The escape was not courage. It was geometry.
dialog_draft.action_before: On the cliff path, his glance drifts once to the right and stays.

dialog_draft.line: I went to the right. The soldier wished to stop me. I took him with me.
dialog_draft.subtext: I chose a death that might still be life; the soldier did not choose.
dialog_draft.action_before: Bound hands close around the leather strap of the man behind him.

dialog_draft.line: He was killed. I, as you see, remained alive.
dialog_draft.subtext: Allah decided; do not ask me to thank Him for it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Back in the chamber. Draws his palms slowly down his beard.

dialog_draft.line: Permit me — your ribs.
dialog_draft.subtext: I have heard enough that I cannot keep my composure intact; I am buying a moment.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sets his pen down and lifts his eyes from the page for the first time in many lines.

dialog_draft.line: Ribs. It is so. Allah knows.
dialog_draft.subtext: The cost is named and the topic ends here.
dialog_draft.action_before: Right hand drifts to his side, presses once through the cherkesska, drops away.

emotional_beats.beat: An iconic image — bound man drags armed guard into the abyss.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Awe and horror, the kind that fixes a face in legend.
emotional_beats.character_feels: The clean, terrible peace of a decision already taken.

emotional_beats.beat: Survival as a wound, not a victory.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The lift of the leap collapses into broken bone.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Allah's verdict received without gratitude.

emotional_beats.beat: Loris-Melikov's pen pauses.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: A bureaucrat just became a witness.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Brief, faint warmth — someone, at least, has heard him.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The making of a legend, told in the exact voice of a man who paid for it with bones.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Pale Caucasus sky over slate ravine, ropes on bare wrists, leather musket-strap, mist below, candlelit chamber inkwell — palette: sky-grey, rope-brown, ink-black.

inner_monologue_converted.original: I knew that they had orders to kill me if I tried to escape.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Single shot of forty muskets slung at the ready as Hadji Murad's eye measures the drop.

inner_monologue_converted.original: He was killed outright but I, as you see, remained alive.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Camera-gaze descends into ravine: dead soldier on rock, Hadji Murad crawling on one elbow toward a crevice.

adapted_scenes.transition: Inkwell on the chamber table catches candlelight; ink-ripple dissolves into mist closing over the ravine of the next remembered country.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. TIFLIS RESIDENCE - LORIS-MELIKOV'S STUDY - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Hadji Murad stands at the window, back to the room. He pulls two folded letters from a worn leather portfolio and places them on the desk without looking at Loris-Melikov. Loris-Melikov picks up the first letter — the paper is yellowed, creased. He reads in silence. Hadji Murad turns, lifts the hem of his right trouser leg, exposing the shortened, slightly twisted limb. He presses it flat against the floor as if testing it, then drops the trouser hem. He says nothing. When Loris-Melikov sets down the first letter, Hadji Murad holds out the second, then pauses — keeping it in his own hand a moment while he speaks.

dialog_draft.line: General Klugenau writes that Akhmet Khan brought the accusation. That you had gone to Shamil.
dialog_draft.subtext: I need to understand the full chain of betrayal before I can write anything useful to Vorontsov.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sets the first letter face-down on the desk, aligning its edge carefully.

dialog_draft.line: The Khan wanted me dead. A letter is a clean way to arrange it.
dialog_draft.subtext: I have carried this truth for years. It requires no elaboration.
dialog_draft.action_before: Looks at the letter in Loris-Melikov's hands, not at the man.

dialog_draft.line: And what did you write back to Klugenau?
dialog_draft.subtext: I want to hear your words, not the official record.
dialog_draft.action_before: Dips his pen but does not write — holds it above the page.

dialog_draft.line: I wrote that the turban was for Allah, not for Shamil. That I could not go to the Russians — I had been dishonored. A bound man was spat upon in Khunzakh. That man was me.
dialog_draft.subtext: This is the wound beneath every alliance I have ever made or broken.
dialog_draft.action_before: Finally hands over the second letter. Straightens. Touches the portfolio as if closing it.

dialog_draft.line: The cliff. You pulled the soldier with you.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am still trying to understand what kind of man survives that choice.
dialog_draft.action_before: Puts the pen down. Looks up from the page.

dialog_draft.line: He died. I did not. It is well.
dialog_draft.subtext: There is no guilt or regret in this — only fact. Fate decides; I acted.
dialog_draft.action_before: Moves the crooked leg slightly, settling his weight onto it. A small, deliberate gesture.

emotional_beats.beat: Akhmet Khan's name surfaces as the man who sent the false letter to Klugenau
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The betrayal becomes concrete — a face behind the persecution
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad holds the name like a coal he has carried for decades — without drama, without release

emotional_beats.beat: HM recounts the cliff jump and the dead soldier with complete flatness
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Unsettled — the absence of guilt or self-congratulation is more disturbing than either would be
emotional_beats.character_feels: This is simply what happened. Survival was not triumph; it was continuation.

emotional_beats.beat: HM names his dishonor — the spitting in Khunzakh — as the reason he could not join either side
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The trap closes: honor-culture makes him stateless, caught between two empires
emotional_beats.character_feels: Naming it is not relief; it is testimony. He expects nothing from the telling.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A man recites the documentary evidence of his own destruction — without self-pity, and without absolution.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Yellowed letters on dark wood desk; a single oil lamp; the shortened leg pressed flat against floorboards; the worn portfolio, its clasp tarnished.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad recounts his survival from the cliff with matter-of-fact pride
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He presses the shortened leg to the floor — a physical proof offered without comment

inner_monologue_converted.original: HM's internal sense of honor-wound over the Khunzakh spitting incident
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He withholds the second letter until he has spoken the dishonor aloud — the document only comes after the wound is named

on_the_nose_risk: HM's line 'I had been dishonored' risks being slightly direct — softened by contextualizing it as what he wrote, not what he now declares
adapted_scenes.transition: Loris-Melikov's pen finally touches the page as Hadji Murad goes silent — the scratch of nib on paper carries into the next scene.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. TIFLIS RESIDENCE - LORIS-MELIKOV'S STUDY - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: Hadji Murad sits across from Loris-Melikov, who holds a yellowed letter. The lamp throws hard shadows. Hadji Murad speaks without expression — the words come like a verdict already delivered long ago. Loris-Melikov reads aloud from Klugenau's letter, his voice careful, formal. When he finishes, Hadji Murad takes the paper back, folds it once, and sets it on the table between them. He does not look at it again.

dialog_draft.line: I did not believe him. The chief thing for me was Akhmet Khan. The Russians could not give me that.
dialog_draft.subtext: Justice was never the point — revenge was, and he chose the path that made revenge possible.
dialog_draft.action_before: Takes the folded letter from Loris-Melikov's hand. Does not unfold it.

dialog_draft.line: And Shamil could.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is mapping the logic of a man who chose the Imamate not from faith but from necessity.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sets his pen down. Watches Hadji Murad's hands.

dialog_draft.line: Shamil promised me Akhmet Khan's death and Avaria. I considered it a long time. Then I went.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is not ashamed. He is explaining causality, not seeking absolution.
dialog_draft.action_before: Looks at the wall past Loris-Melikov's shoulder — not evasion, calculation.

dialog_draft.line: And from that time you fought the Russians.
dialog_draft.subtext: He knows the answer. He is closing the record — giving Hadji Murad the dignity of completing the sentence himself.
dialog_draft.action_before: Opens his ledger. Does not write. Waits.

dialog_draft.line: Allahu a'lam.
dialog_draft.subtext: God knows — and the God who knows has seen him on both sides of every line.
dialog_draft.action_before: A pause. Then a single nod. As if the accounting is complete.

emotional_beats.beat: HM recounts the Klugenau letter — a Russian general's promise he chose not to trust
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Recognition: the promise was reasonable; so was the refusal
emotional_beats.character_feels: No regret — only the clean logic of a decision made under siege

emotional_beats.beat: HM states that Akhmet Khan's hostility, not ideology, drove him to Shamil
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The tragedy of contingency — history turned on a personal vendetta
emotional_beats.character_feels: Precise, factual — he is describing weather, not confession

emotional_beats.beat: Loris-Melikov completes HM's sentence; HM closes with 'Allahu a'lam'
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The weight of what cannot be undone
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: sealed. Loris-Melikov: the distance between them has just become measurable.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The past as unalterable mechanics — a man explains why he became the enemy, without apology
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Yellowed letter on dark table between two men; lamplight; Loris-Melikov's open ledger with no entry written

inner_monologue_converted.original: HM's internal reckoning with Klugenau's offer — weighing trust vs. the vendetta against Akhmet Khan
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: HM takes the letter back physically and sets it down — the gesture performs the refusal

inner_monologue_converted.original: HM's decision to join Shamil narrated in past tense by the novel
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Three-line statement, declarative register, no qualifier — the decision was made; he reports it like a military fact

on_the_nose_risk: HM's line 'I did not believe him' — kept short and factual to avoid confessional weight; works within his voice profile
adapted_scenes.transition: Loris-Melikov closes the ledger without writing; the sound of the latch carries into silence — then street noise from outside, the city indifferent

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. WAR MINISTRY ANTEROOM — ST. PETERSBURG — DAY
adapted_scenes.action: A courier hands CHERNYSHOV a dispatch packet sealed with Vorontsov's crest. Chernyshov breaks the wax without haste, reads standing at a tall writing desk near a frost-rimed window. His expression does not change. He folds the letter once, precisely, and sets it beside a stack of identically folded dispatches. He crosses to a cabinet, selects a fresh sheet, dips his pen — then pauses, listening to the muffled clatter of the palace corridor beyond the door. He writes the first line, stops, crosses it out, and writes again.

dialog_draft.line: Another petition from the Caucasus. His Excellency frames it as an opportunity.
dialog_draft.subtext: Vorontsov wants credit; I must decide how much to let him have.
dialog_draft.action_before: Chernyshov sets the letter flat on the desk, smoothing it with one finger as though pressing out a wrinkle.

dialog_draft.line: His Imperial Majesty will want the name. And the number of men Vorontsov intends to spend recovering what this man's family is worth to him.
dialog_draft.subtext: The Tsar will ask the cost before the prize. I must answer before he asks.
dialog_draft.action_before: He lifts his pen, rotates it slowly between two fingers without writing.

emotional_beats.beat: Chernyshov reads Vorontsov's letter about Hadji Murad's defection
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The vast, indifferent machinery of imperial bureaucracy receiving a human life as paperwork
emotional_beats.character_feels: Calculated assessment — opportunity weighed against exposure

emotional_beats.beat: Chernyshov begins drafting his summary for the Tsar, crossing out his first attempt
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Unease — the story of HM's captive family reduced to a line in a report
emotional_beats.character_feels: The careful self-erasure of a man who survives by reflecting his master's expectations

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Imperial distance — the Petersburg corridor where a man's fate becomes an administrative calculation
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Grey winter light through tall frosted windows; stacked dispatch packets uniformly folded; a single oil lamp warm against the cold glass; Chernyshov's dark uniform against white paper

inner_monologue_converted.original: Vorontsov's letter reporting Hadji Murad's arrival and the question of what use to make of him
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Chernyshov reading and re-reading the dispatch; crossing out his first draft line — the hesitation is the thought

inner_monologue_converted.original: Chernyshov's role as intermediary between Vorontsov and the Tsar, filtering information to please
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Physical gesture: he smooths the letter flat, then sets it beside identical dispatches — the bureaucratic equivalence of a human life with all other paperwork

adapted_scenes.transition: The scratch of Chernyshov's pen on paper — then silence as he lifts it — carries into the sound of HM's voice from the previous scene: 'the end of the rope is in Shamil's hands'

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. LORIS-MELIKOV'S OFFICE - TIFLIS - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: A modest government office. Maps on the wall, a writing desk crowded with paper. Loris-Melikov sits across from Hadji Murad, quill moving. Hadji Murad speaks slowly, watching the pen scratch each word into paper. He raises his eyes and both hands to the ceiling — a brief, complete gesture of surrender to God — then lowers them and waits. Loris-Melikov does not look up from the page.

dialog_draft.line: He says Shamil will not release them. Perhaps a pardon instead — your former rank restored. That offer will come.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am giving you the worst news as a bureaucratic forecast, not as cruelty — but it is cruelty.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sets the quill down. Straightens the page with one finger.

dialog_draft.line: Shamil will not forgive. He cannot. And so I cannot return.
dialog_draft.subtext: I have already calculated this. I am not asking you to comfort me.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sits still. Both hands flat on his knees.

dialog_draft.line: And if he threatens to kill them — your mother, your wives, your son — what then?
dialog_draft.subtext: I need the answer for the report. But I am also asking because I want to know.
dialog_draft.action_before: Picks up the quill again. Does not yet write.

dialog_draft.line: Everything is in God's hands.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will not answer this question the way you want it answered.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lifts his eyes and both arms toward the ceiling. Holds them a moment. Lowers them.

dialog_draft.line: Save my family. Then give me a chance to serve. One month. If I bring you nothing — punish me as you see fit.
dialog_draft.subtext: Reading back his own words to him. The Russian turns them clinical; the Avar had spoken them as vow.
dialog_draft.action_before: Reads from the page. His voice is flat, accurate, almost kind.

dialog_draft.line: That is what I said.
dialog_draft.subtext: Yes. And you have reduced it to a sentence.
dialog_draft.action_before: Looks at the page as if confirming his own handwriting. A pause.

emotional_beats.beat: Loris-Melikov delivers the forecast — Shamil will not release the family
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Dread. The trap is closing from both sides.
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: a confirmation he has already accepted. Loris-Melikov: the discomfort of being the messenger of something he cannot fix.

emotional_beats.beat: HM raises hands to heaven — the gesture of total surrender to God
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Grief and awe. He is not broken. He has found a frame that holds the unbearable.
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: a practiced, genuine act of faith. Not performance.

emotional_beats.beat: Loris-Melikov reads HM's vow back to him from the page
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The gap between a warrior's oath and a bureaucrat's transcription — same words, different weight.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Loris-Melikov: faint unease at what he is doing. HM: a quiet, complete stillness.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The paralysis of a man whose courage is intact and whose hands are tied
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Ink-stained desk, afternoon light through narrow windows; HM's papakha rests on his knee — he has not been invited to set it aside

inner_monologue_converted.original: He is much concerned about the fate of his family and says that while they are in Shamil's hands he is paralysed
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: HM's hands lie flat and still on his knees throughout the scene — controlled immobility as paralysis made visible

inner_monologue_converted.original: He lifted his eyes and arms to heaven, and said that everything is in God's hands
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Direct translation — the gesture is scripted as the primary beat; dialogue stripped to minimum

inner_monologue_converted.original: He does not sleep at night, eats hardly anything, prays continually
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Shadows under his eyes, a stillness that reads as exhaustion held rigid — shown through posture and the quill-ink pause

on_the_nose_risk: Loris-Melikov's reading of the vow risks being too expository — mitigated by making it a formal act (reading from the record) rather than a summary
adapted_scenes.transition: Loris-Melikov sets the quill down; the scratch of pen on paper fades into the muezzin call rising from the city below — distant, precise, indifferent

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. LORIS-MELIKOV'S OFFICE - TIFLIS - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Loris-Melikov pauses over an unfinished report, the words Grozny and convoy visible in wet ink. Across the desk, Hadji Murad raises both hands toward the ceiling, then lowers them to the hilt of his dagger without drawing it. Loris turns the sand-glass beside the paper and waits. Hadji Murad leans over the map, places one finger near Chechnya, and keeps it there until Loris writes.

dialog_draft.line: Permit me one plain question. If Shamil sends such word, what answer do you give?
dialog_draft.subtext: I need to know whether you will break under family pressure.
dialog_draft.action_before: Loris-Melikov sands the last line, then sets the quill above a blank margin.

dialog_draft.line: Allah holds the day. I do not go back into Shamil's hand.
dialog_draft.subtext: Death is preferable to submission, but I will not say fear aloud.
dialog_draft.action_before: Hadji Murad lifts his eyes and open palms, then lets them fall slowly.

dialog_draft.line: The Prince will ask what protection satisfies both honor and prudence.
dialog_draft.subtext: Vorontsov will not trust you without a leash.
dialog_draft.action_before: Loris-Melikov dips the quill and avoids the dagger at Hadji Murad's belt.

dialog_draft.line: Twenty Cossacks. Thirty. Picked men. They guard me from enemies, and you from doubt.
dialog_draft.subtext: Give me enough freedom to reach my family, and call it your safeguard.
dialog_draft.action_before: Hadji Murad taps the map near Grozny with two hard fingers.

dialog_draft.line: Let news come from them. Let ransom move. Then my hand is useful to you.
dialog_draft.subtext: My loyalty depends on whether you help my family first.
dialog_draft.action_before: Hadji Murad slides Loris-Melikov's inkwell closer to the map mark.

emotional_beats.beat: Loris tests the limit of Hadji Murad's surrender.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The Russian hospitality tightening into examination.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Loris measures danger behind courtesy.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad refuses Shamil but asks for guarded movement toward Chechnya.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Hope mixed with the first shape of a trap.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad forces himself to bargain without pleading.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A captive ally bargaining for motion while his keepers call stillness prudence.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Wet ink, Caucasus map, sand-glass, dagger hilt, winter light through office glass.

inner_monologue_converted.original: He would never surrender to his foe and is interested in nothing but his family's ransom.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He raises his hands to God, keeps his hand near the dagger, and fixes the map at Grozny.

inner_monologue_converted.original: The Russians are perplexed by responsibility and fear that imprisonment would be unjust and impolitic.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Loris writes and hesitates over the report while framing the convoy as protection and guarantee.

adapted_scenes.transition: The wet ink of Grozny remains on the page as the sand runs out.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. WAR MINISTRY ANTEROOM - ST. PETERSBURG - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Chernyshov stands at the frost-rimmed window with Vorontsov's letter spread over a leather folder. His gloved finger moves down the page: no prison, a convoy of chosen Cossacks, Loris-Melikov attached, Tarkhanov trusted, Vozdvizhensk forbidden. He folds the sheet at the paragraph naming Hadji Murad's promise and sets it beside a clean cover note for the Emperor.

dialog_draft.line: Prince Vorontsov asks only that His Majesty see the necessity of the road already taken.
dialog_draft.subtext: He will frame risk as obedience before the Tsar can blame anyone.
dialog_draft.action_before: Chernyshov smooths the crease across Vorontsov's signature.

dialog_draft.line: And if the mountaineer rides beyond their permission?
dialog_draft.subtext: Someone must be named responsible before failure arrives.
dialog_draft.action_before: The officer stops sharpening his pencil and looks at the sealed dispatch.

dialog_draft.line: Then the paper will show every permission was also a boundary.
dialog_draft.subtext: The bureaucracy is already preparing its defense.
dialog_draft.action_before: Chernyshov places a blank margin over the word prisoner.

emotional_beats.beat: Vorontsov's courtesy is converted into imperial record.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The trap becomes colder because it looks polite.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Chernyshov feels protected by procedure.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad's freedom is listed as permissions and prohibitions.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Movement and captivity begin to look identical.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Chernyshov feels the matter can be managed from paper.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Elegant control disguising fear of escape
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Frosted glass, green desk baize, black wax, folded letter beside a clean imperial cover note.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Vorontsov fears being accused of a great mistake if Hadji Murad escapes.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Chernyshov folds and files the letter so every indulgence reads as controlled responsibility.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad is not to be treated as a prisoner, but must not act without consent.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: The letter's permissions are read aloud as a visible chain of named escorts and forbidden places.

adapted_scenes.transition: The folded dispatch rests over the covered word prisoner.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. WINTER PALACE CABINET - MORNING
adapted_scenes.action: Frost whitens the tall windows. CHERNYSHOV enters with Vorontsov's sealed report tucked under his arm, snow melting on his beaver collar. NICHOLAS stands at a desk without sitting; PRINCE DOLGORUKY waits near the door. Chernyshov opens the packet, lays a finger on Hadji Murad's name, and watches Nicholas's face harden before he reaches the accusation.

dialog_draft.line: Your Imperial Majesty, Prince Vorontsov again shows a most generous confidence in the mountain people.
dialog_draft.subtext: Vorontsov is endangering Russians by favoring natives.
dialog_draft.action_before: Chernyshov smooths the report flat with two gloved fingers.

dialog_draft.line: Read what is written.
dialog_draft.subtext: Do not guide my judgment.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas taps the desk once with a paper knife.

dialog_draft.line: It looks like Hadji Murad may be more useful inland, until his family is recovered.
dialog_draft.subtext: Remove Hadji Murad from Vorontsov and discredit both men.
dialog_draft.action_before: Chernyshov lowers his eyes and turns one page too carefully.

dialog_draft.line: No.
dialog_draft.subtext: Your plan fails because I choose the opposite.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas leaves the paper untouched.

dialog_draft.line: Shall I note that no change is ordered?
dialog_draft.subtext: The decision is final and must be recorded.
dialog_draft.action_before: Dolgoruky dips a pen and waits without looking at Chernyshov.

dialog_draft.line: The Caucasus remains as it is.
dialog_draft.subtext: Hadji Murad stays in Vorontsov's hands for now.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas turns toward the frozen window.

emotional_beats.beat: Chernyshov frames the report as a warning against Vorontsov.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The machinery of court intrigue tightening.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Chernyshov feels poised to wound a rival.

emotional_beats.beat: Nicholas rejects the suggestion before it can land.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Power working through mood rather than justice.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Nicholas feels irritated and absolute.

emotional_beats.beat: Dolgoruky records that Hadji Murad's position is unchanged.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: A distant life redirected by a cold room.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Chernyshov feels checked but must bow.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Petty malice broken against arbitrary power
adapted_scenes.visual_note: White frost, dark uniforms, blue seals, untouched report, Nicholas standing while others wait

inner_monologue_converted.original: Chernyshov intended to present Vorontsov's actions in an unfavorable light.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Chernyshov guides Nicholas's eye to Hadji Murad's name and phrases suspicion as deference.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Nicholas was in bad spirits and would not accept any suggestion from Chernyshov.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Nicholas interrupts, refuses to touch the paper, and gives clipped orders.

adapted_scenes.transition: The untouched report remains on the desk as palace cold carries back toward the Caucasus.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. WINTER PALACE WAITING-ROOM - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: CHERNYSHOV steps from the sledge, flicks snow from polished boots without overshoes, and climbs the carpeted stairs with spurs ringing louder than his old legs allow. In the mirror he removes his plumed hat, smooths the curled hair at his temples, straightens his cross and epaulets. Lackeys bow as he passes. DOLGORUKY checks a portfolio; the IMPERIAL AIDE-DE-CAMP glides from the cabinet door, glowing from proximity to power, and silently invites Chernyshov in.

dialog_draft.line: L'empereur?
dialog_draft.subtext: He needs the Emperor's mood before he risks the report.
dialog_draft.action_before: Chernyshov stops near the cabinet door and keeps one hand on his cross.

dialog_draft.line: Sa Majeste vient de rentrer.
dialog_draft.subtext: He displays court access as if it were rank.
dialog_draft.action_before: The aide-de-camp inclines his fresh, mustached face and lets the words linger.

emotional_beats.beat: Chernyshov performs youth and command before the palace servants.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The court's elegance feels brittle and staged.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Chernyshov feels exposed by age and cold.

emotional_beats.beat: The aide-de-camp returns from the cabinet and gestures Chernyshov forward.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Power sits behind a door, making courtiers theatrical.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Chernyshov gathers himself for obedience disguised as authority.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Vanity and fear polished into ceremony.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Silver beaver collar, plumed hat, mirror, carpeted stairs, gala livery, polished boots on red carpet, cabinet door.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Chernyshov thought over what he was about to report to the Emperor.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He paces near the cabinet door, flexing chilled feet and gripping his cross.

inner_monologue_converted.original: The aide-de-camp enjoyed the sound of his own voice and revered the cabinet.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He lets the French phrase linger, then walks toward the door with ritual softness.

adapted_scenes.transition: Chernyshov's spurs stop at the cabinet threshold.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. WINTER PALACE CABINET - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Dolgoruky checks the papers in his portfolio while Chernyshov works feeling back into his feet, pacing on the polished floor. The aide-de-camp opens the cabinet doors and bows them through. Nicholas sits behind an enormous writing table, black coat tight across his laced stomach, eyes fixed and dull beneath the portrait of Alexander I. Chernyshov stops short, smooths his face into obedience, and waits for the Emperor to choose the first word.

dialog_draft.line: His Imperial Majesty will receive you now.
dialog_draft.subtext: Enter carefully; the room belongs entirely to him.
dialog_draft.action_before: He holds the door open with one white-gloved hand.

dialog_draft.line: The papers.
dialog_draft.subtext: No ceremony is needed except obedience.
dialog_draft.action_before: He extends two fingers without rising.

dialog_draft.line: At once, Your Imperial Majesty.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will match your mood before I speak.
dialog_draft.action_before: He takes the top packet from Dolgoruky and bows over it.

emotional_beats.beat: The ministers cross from waiting room into imperial space.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The palace narrows around them.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Chernyshov measures every gesture.

emotional_beats.beat: Nicholas receives them without warmth.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Power appears exhausted and dangerous.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Nicholas demands control before content.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Cold obedience before a tired autocrat
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Lofty cabinet, four winter windows, Alexander I portrait, enormous desk, black uniform cloth, white gloves, polished floor.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Chernyshov thought over what he was about to report to the Emperor.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Chernyshov paces, stops at the door, smooths his face, and yields the first word to Nicholas.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Nicholas was in a dissatisfied and irate mood from fatigue and the masquerade.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Nicholas sits rigid and dull-eyed, coat strained, issuing clipped orders without greeting.

adapted_scenes.transition: The packet opens under Nicholas's hand; its paper rustle carries into the Caucasian report.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. THEATRE BOX / NICHOLAS'S BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: A masked young woman follows Nicholas through a masquerade corridor to a private box. Inside, an Uhlan officer freezes beside a fair young woman on a velvet sofa; she snaps her mask back over her face. Nicholas fills the doorway until the officer rises, bows almost double, and backs out with his partner. Later, Nicholas lies on a narrow bed under his cloak while the Swedish girl's dropped ribbon hangs from his fingers; Nelidova's miniature sits open on the table.

dialog_draft.line: Il y a quelqu'un!
dialog_draft.subtext: She knows the room is not theirs and fears exposure.
dialog_draft.action_before: She stops at the threshold and grips the doorframe.

dialog_draft.line: Well, friend. You are younger than I. You may give up your place.
dialog_draft.subtext: He turns terror into a courtly command.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas lets the silence stretch, then lowers one gloved hand toward the officer.

dialog_draft.line: I knew your face before I knew my own.
dialog_draft.subtext: She offers worship because it is the only power she has.
dialog_draft.action_before: She removes her mask and keeps her eyes on his portrait medallion.

dialog_draft.line: Then you have been well instructed.
dialog_draft.subtext: Her adoration pleases him because it confirms his image.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas studies her face as if inspecting a report.

emotional_beats.beat: The officer's panic clears the room for Nicholas.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Disgust at power disguised as manners.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Nicholas enjoys obedience.

emotional_beats.beat: The girl trades her mask for flattery.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Unease at her vulnerability.
emotional_beats.character_feels: The girl feels chosen.

emotional_beats.beat: The bed tableau places the ribbon beside Nelidova's portrait.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Cold recognition of appetite without remorse.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Nicholas remains satisfied and restless.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Autocratic appetite converts fear and adoration into private entitlement.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Velvet box, black domino masks, white gloves, hard military bed, cloak, ribbon, Nelidova miniature.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Nicholas is pleased by the terror he inspires.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He pauses in the doorway until the officer's body buckles into a bow.

inner_monologue_converted.original: He compares the young girl with Nelidova and feels no guilt.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: The girl's ribbon and Nelidova's miniature share the bedside table.

adapted_scenes.transition: The ribbon twists in Nicholas's hand; the same white fabric becomes paper in the next imperial dispatch.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. WINTER PALACE EMBANKMENT - MORNING
adapted_scenes.action: Nicholas steps from the small palace portico in a military cloak, his skin still reddened from ice. He passes a frost-edged icon in the doorway without looking back, then stops before a massive jurisprudence student frozen in salute. Nicholas studies the uniform, the elbow, the obedient height; his frown thins into approval, then hardens again when the student's answer fails him. As Nicholas returns toward the palace, Helena Pavlovna's carriage rolls up at the Saltykov entrance, its red-liveried footman bright against the snow.

dialog_draft.line: Your name.
dialog_draft.subtext: He tests whether the body before him belongs to imperial order.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas taps his gloved fingers once against his cloak clasp.

dialog_draft.line: Polosatov, Your Imperial Majesty.
dialog_draft.subtext: He offers perfect submission and hopes it is enough.
dialog_draft.action_before: Polosatov snaps his heels tighter and keeps his hand to his cap.

dialog_draft.line: Fine fellow. Do you wish to enter the army?
dialog_draft.subtext: He grants approval only if the student becomes useful to him.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas lets his eyes travel from the student's boots to his shoulders.

dialog_draft.line: Not at all, Your Imperial Majesty.
dialog_draft.subtext: He refuses without understanding the danger in refusal.
dialog_draft.action_before: Polosatov's salute trembles, but he does not lower it.

dialog_draft.line: Blockhead.
dialog_draft.subtext: A life outside command is stupidity to him.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas turns away before the word lands.

dialog_draft.line: What would Russia be without the Emperor?
dialog_draft.subtext: He buries private shame under ceremonial grandeur.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas walks alone, repeating a woman's name under his breath until the carriage wheels cut across the ice.

emotional_beats.beat: Nicholas performs piety and discipline before entering public space.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Cold distance from a man armored by ritual.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Restored control

emotional_beats.beat: A student's obedient body wins approval until his civilian choice provokes contempt.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The danger of casual power.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Irritated superiority

emotional_beats.beat: Helena's carriage arrives as Nicholas returns to the palace.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Court politics closing back around him.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Renewed vigilance

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A ruler crushes shame by turning every encounter into a judgment.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: White embankment, black military cloak, red footman's livery, rigid salute, palace stone and ice.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Unpleasant after-taste after private profligacy.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Nicholas mutters a woman's name, then stiffens into imperial posture.

inner_monologue_converted.original: The thought of his own greatness tranquilized him.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He asks what Russia would be without the Emperor while walking alone.

on_the_nose_risk: Final imperial line approaches direct self-mythologizing but matches Nicholas's ceremonial vanity.
adapted_scenes.transition: The red livery at the carriage door carries into the next court chamber.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. NICHOLAS'S CABINET - WINTER DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Nicholas enters from the corridor with snow-light on his boots, stops before a mirror, and smooths his whiskers, temple hair, wig, and mustaches until his face becomes ceremonial. In the cabinet, Chernyshov rises with a dispatch folder against his chest. Nicholas sits without offering warmth, strokes loose papers with his ringed white hand, and fixes his gaze on the tuft above Chernyshov's forehead as reports pass from theft to troop movements to Vorontsov's sealed account of Hadji Murad.

dialog_draft.line: The report. Read it.
dialog_draft.subtext: He wants obedience before information.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas flicks two fingers toward the chair without looking at it.

dialog_draft.line: The commissariat matter is proved, Your Imperial Majesty.
dialog_draft.subtext: He offers a punishable target to match the Tsar's mood.
dialog_draft.action_before: Chernyshov opens the folder to the safest paper first.

dialog_draft.line: Ranks.
dialog_draft.subtext: He turns corruption into ritual punishment.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas presses one palm flat over the list of names.

dialog_draft.line: Prince Vorontsov also sends word of Hadji Murad.
dialog_draft.subtext: He moves to the Caucasus only after measuring the room.
dialog_draft.action_before: Chernyshov slides a sealed Caucasus dispatch onto the desk.

dialog_draft.line: Proceed.
dialog_draft.subtext: The rebel matters because empire has noticed him.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas lifts his eyes from the seal to Chernyshov's face.

emotional_beats.beat: Nicholas polishes his appearance into office.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: A private man hardening into an institution.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Vexed discipline.

emotional_beats.beat: Chernyshov reads the room before the documents.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Court power as nervous choreography.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Calculated caution.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad's name enters the imperial cabinet as paperwork.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The distance between captive family and imperial abstraction.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Cold administrative interest.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Imperial authority reducing human crises to punishments and files.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Mirror, wax seals, ringed hand, frost-lit cabinet, red-liveried carriage glimpsed through a palace window.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Nicholas sees liberal court society as futile and feels sadness and vexation.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He watches Helena Pavlovna's carriage pass, frowns, whispers nonsense, then grooms himself into a mask.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Nicholas believes officials always steal but considers punishment his duty.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He silently flattens the embezzlement list and issues the single-word sentence.

adapted_scenes.transition: The wax seal on Vorontsov's dispatch carries into the scratch of Loris-Melikov's pen.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. WINTER PALACE CABINET - ST. PETERSBURG - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Nicholas sits at a polished table under high windows, moving petitions into small piles. He marks one document, lays it aside, then strikes names from a rewards list with a hard pencil stroke. Chernyshov opens Vorontsov's Caucasus dispatch; Nicholas stops trimming the list. As Chernyshov praises the forest-cutting campaign, Nicholas presses both palms flat on the map of the Caucasus.

dialog_draft.line: One honest man in Russia.
dialog_draft.subtext: He appoints himself judge over a corrupt empire.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas taps the accused official's file with one finger.

dialog_draft.line: It looks like it, your Imperial Majesty.
dialog_draft.subtext: He rewards vanity before policy is discussed.
dialog_draft.action_before: Chernyshov lowers his eyes and lets a careful smile appear.

dialog_draft.line: Leave it. The decision will be given.
dialog_draft.subtext: He enjoys delay as another form of command.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas places the file on the left side of the table.

dialog_draft.line: A courier from the Caucasus. Prince Vorontsov reports Hadji Murad's surrender.
dialog_draft.subtext: He presents the news as tribute to Nicholas.
dialog_draft.action_before: Chernyshov breaks the seal and smooths the dispatch.

dialog_draft.line: Good. A beginning.
dialog_draft.subtext: He hears a man's surrender as proof of his own design.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas lifts his chin without touching the dispatch.

dialog_draft.line: With the forests felled and their stores exhausted, they learn where endurance ends.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. WINTER PALACE CABINET - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Nicholas stands over the Caucasus map while Chernyshov holds the dispatch at chest height. Nicholas places two fingers on the mountain forests, then drags them across the paper as if scraping it clean. Chernyshov opens a second folder; Nicholas stops him with a glance, stamps a verdict on the first report, and only then lets the student case be read.

dialog_draft.line: How do you mean?
dialog_draft.subtext: He invites praise while pretending to test a military claim.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas keeps his fingers spread over the Caucasus map.

dialog_draft.line: Had your Majesty's plan been followed sooner, forests and stores alike would already be gone.
dialog_draft.subtext: He hands Nicholas ownership of another man's brutal strategy.
dialog_draft.action_before: Chernyshov smooths the dispatch paper with the side of his thumb.

dialog_draft.line: True.
dialog_draft.subtext: He accepts the flattery as fact and converts devastation into policy.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas presses the seal hard enough to bend the wax.

dialog_draft.line: The next case.
dialog_draft.subtext: Human lives are interchangeable files under his hand.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas pushes the Caucasus report aside without looking at Chernyshov.

dialog_draft.line: Bzhezovski. Of Polish descent, Roman Catholic, your Imperial Majesty.
dialog_draft.subtext: He supplies the prejudice Nicholas wants before judgment is spoken.
dialog_draft.action_before: Chernyshov checks the margin, then lowers his eyes.

emotional_beats.beat: Chernyshov credits Nicholas with the slow devastation plan.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The machinery of flattery tightening around policy.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Chernyshov feels protected by agreement.

emotional_beats.beat: Nicholas accepts contradiction without hesitation.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Dread at power detached from reality.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Nicholas feels confirmed.

emotional_beats.beat: The student case enters as another file to punish.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: A chill from war policy to domestic cruelty.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Nicholas feels ready to judge.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Autocratic vanity turning flattery into violence

inner_monologue_converted.original: Nicholas no longer saw his own inconsistencies or measured his actions by reality.

inner_monologue_converted.original: He had to feel certain that all Poles were villains.
