adapted_scenes.slug: INT. WINTER PALACE STUDY - ST. PETERSBURG - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Nicholas stands over two reports on a polished desk: a student case and Vorontsov's Caucasian dispatch. Chernyshov waits with his portfolio open, eyes lowered. Nicholas hears the Polish Catholic name, shuts his eyes, then writes a sentence across the margin in broad, misspelled strokes and signs it with an enormous flourish. Chernyshov reads, bows, and slides the Caucasus file into the same pool of ink.

dialog_draft.line: A Pole?
dialog_draft.subtext: He has already chosen the crime before hearing it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas taps the student's name with the blunt end of his pen.

dialog_draft.line: Of Polish descent, Your Imperial Majesty. A Roman Catholic.
dialog_draft.subtext: He supplies the prejudice the Emperor wants sanctioned.
dialog_draft.action_before: Chernyshov lowers his chin a fraction over the open report.

dialog_draft.line: Deserves death. We have no capital punishment. Twelve times through a thousand men.
dialog_draft.subtext: He calls torture mercy because the law flatters him.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas blots the margin, leaving the wet words still legible.

dialog_draft.line: All students will attend. It will do them good.
dialog_draft.subtext: He wants the punishment to educate by terror.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas pushes the paper across with two fingers.

dialog_draft.line: It shall be done. With respect to Prince Vorontsov's dispatch?
dialog_draft.subtext: He seals one cruelty and offers the next without blinking.
dialog_draft.action_before: Chernyshov bows, straightens the tuft on his forehead, and opens the second report.

dialog_draft.line: Keep to my system. Burn dwellings, strip food, raid without rest.
dialog_draft.subtext: He extends the same logic from one body to a whole people.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas presses his seal into the wax beside Vorontsov's crest.

emotional_beats.beat: Chernyshov names Bzhezovski's origin and faith.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The trap closes before any evidence matters.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Chernyshov feels secure in obedience.

emotional_beats.beat: Nicholas writes torture as a merciful legal sentence.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Revulsion at polished bureaucratic violence.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Nicholas feels confirmed by his own hand.

emotional_beats.beat: The Caucasus order follows without pause.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The private prejudice widens into imperial policy.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Chernyshov feels the room's danger and usefulness.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Cruelty laundering itself as order
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Frosted windows, green desk leather, wet black ink, imperial seal, two reports touching at the edges.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Nicholas justifies his hatred of Poles and enjoys ruthless cruelty.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He frowns at the identity, writes a fatal sentence as mercy, and adds public attendance.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Chernyshov knows the Emperor's decision ritual and admires it outwardly.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He waits through the closed-eyed pause, then bows over the finished margin note.

adapted_scenes.transition: The seal hardens beside Vorontsov's crest as the Caucasus file remains open.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. NICHOLAS'S CABINET - ST. PETERSBURG - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Nicholas stands over a green baize desk while Chernyshov holds Vorontsov's dispatch open beside the inkstand. The Emperor marks two lines with a blunt fingernail: burned dwellings, ruined stores, raids. Chernyshov lowers his eyes when Hadji Murad's name is read; Nicholas stops the pen in midair and fixes him in place. Bibikov waits by the door with another folder, then receives his own signed orders and bows under them.

dialog_draft.line: What answer shall be sent to Prince Vorontsov, Your Imperial Majesty?
dialog_draft.subtext: He invites Nicholas to own the harsh order.
dialog_draft.action_before: Chernyshov smooths the dispatch flat with two fingertips.

dialog_draft.line: His system is to be continued. Dwellings, stores, raids.
dialog_draft.subtext: He reduces destruction to administrative sequence.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas taps each marked line once.

dialog_draft.line: And Hadji Murad? Prince Vorontsov is confident.
dialog_draft.subtext: He tries to poison Vorontsov's proposal without opposing it openly.
dialog_draft.action_before: Chernyshov's gaze drops to the carpet.

dialog_draft.line: Confident. Good. The Emperor agrees with him.
dialog_draft.subtext: He rebukes Chernyshov and keeps Hadji Murad as a tool.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas closes the dispatch with his palm.

dialog_draft.line: It shall be carried out, Your Imperial Majesty.
dialog_draft.subtext: He chooses position over conscience.
dialog_draft.action_before: Bibikov receives the folder, sees the court-martial order, and bows.

dialog_draft.line: Necessary. It is not to be discussed.
dialog_draft.subtext: He turns cruelty into doctrine.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nicholas signs the last page and pushes it away.

emotional_beats.beat: Chernyshov tests whether Nicholas will overrule Vorontsov.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The room is dangerous even in whispers.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Chernyshov calculates his angle.

emotional_beats.beat: Nicholas approves scorched-earth policy and Hadji Murad's use.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Hadji Murad's cage tightens from far away.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Nicholas feels confirmed.

emotional_beats.beat: Bibikov accepts a cruel civil order without protest.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Empire appears as routine obedience.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Bibikov swallows dissent.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Bureaucratic cruelty made effortless by obedience
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Green baize desk, dispatch wax, ink-black signatures, frost at tall windows, officials standing below Nicholas's eye line

inner_monologue_converted.original: Bibikov saw the cruelty and injustice but could not express dissent.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Bibikov reads the order, pauses, bows, and accepts the folder.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Nicholas felt his duty had been fulfilled.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Nicholas signs, pushes the page away, and glances at the clock.

adapted_scenes.transition: The scratch of Nicholas's pen carries into Loris-Melikov's pen recording Hadji Murad.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. WINTER PALACE - CHURCH AND HALLS - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Nicholas fastens his orders over a tight uniform and steps into a hall packed with bowed officers and bare-shouldered ladies. He receives New Year greetings with a fixed glassy stare, then stands through Mass as priests and choir chant praise over him. During the final hymn, his eyes settle on Nelidova by a window before he turns away to a waiting minister. In the Hermitage passage, he signs a pension order and hands it off without slowing.

dialog_draft.line: From the special fund. Yearly. To the mother.
dialog_draft.subtext: He treats private damage as an administrative expense.
dialog_draft.action_before: He presses the paper flat with two jeweled fingers.

dialog_draft.line: As Your Imperial Majesty commands.
dialog_draft.subtext: He knows not to ask which mother or why.
dialog_draft.action_before: He lowers his eyes to the seal instead of Nicholas' face.

dialog_draft.line: The Emperor remembers service.
dialog_draft.subtext: He recasts appetite as benevolence.
dialog_draft.action_before: He glances once toward the chapel window where Nelidova stood.

dialog_draft.line: La Pologne...
dialog_draft.subtext: He tests whether Poland may be discussed before dinner.
dialog_draft.action_before: At the Pompeian Hall doors, he folds his napkin edge between his fingers.

dialog_draft.line: Not until Their Majesties are seated.
dialog_draft.subtext: Politics must wait for ritual; fear has etiquette.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lieven lets his gaze travel across the empty imperial chairs.

emotional_beats.beat: Court bodies arrange themselves around Nicholas' entrance.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The suffocation of worship made routine.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Nicholas feels confirmed by obedience.

emotional_beats.beat: Church praise and court praise become the same ritual.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The moral rot beneath ceremony.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Nicholas accepts reverence as obligation.

emotional_beats.beat: A pension order reduces yesterday's girl to paperwork.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Disgust at power laundering harm.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Nicholas feels efficient and untroubled.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Autocratic vanity disguising appetite as duty
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Gold braid, white candles, polished icons, low-necked gowns, sealed pension order, empty imperial dinner chairs.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Nicholas felt duty well fulfilled and accepted praise as the world's due.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He stiffens under greetings, stands through identical church praise, and signs relief paperwork without pause.

inner_monologue_converted.original: He compared Nelidova favorably with yesterday's girl.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: His eyes leave the hymn and settle on Nelidova before the pension order is issued.

adapted_scenes.transition: The folded pension order passes from hand to hand as dinner plates are set in the Pompeian Hall.

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: INT. POMPEIAN HALL, WINTER PALACE - ST. PETERSBURG - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action_description: A gilded dining hall. Candles blaze along a long table laid for a dozen. NICHOLAS I sits at the head, rigid and enormous in his general's tunic. CHERNYSHOV occupies a middle seat, eyes tracking the Emperor's mood. Foreign guests — the PRUSSIAN AMBASSADOR, aides-de-camp — maintain the careful stillness of men afraid to fidget. Nicholas swirls his glass, barely looking up, and announces the Caucasus is nearly finished — Hadji Murad has surrendered, the forests will be felled, the forts will strangle the mountaineers into submission. The PRUSSIAN AMBASSADOR exchanges the briefest glance with his aide: a glance they had rehearsed that morning. He then lavishes praise on the plan. Chernyshov nods. No one eats until Nicholas eats.

dialog_draft.line: The war ends when the trees end. No forest — no hiding place. Hadji Murad has understood this. The rest will follow.
dialog_draft.subtext: He needs to believe his strategy is genius, not attrition.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sets his fork down. Addresses no one in particular.

dialog_draft.line: Your Imperial Majesty's strategic instinct is — as always — the envy of every crowned head in Europe.
dialog_draft.subtext: He thinks you a fool, but a useful one.
dialog_draft.action_before: Touches the napkin at his lip; the faintest pause before speaking.

dialog_draft.line: Send word to Vorontsov. Hadji Murad's surrender changes nothing for the Chechens. Tighten the cordon. Double the raids.
dialog_draft.subtext: The mercy shown to one man is paid for by the suffering of thousands.
dialog_draft.action_before: Stands. Dinner is over. He does not look at Chernyshov — merely expects compliance.

dialog_draft.line: It will be done, Your Imperial Majesty.
dialog_draft.subtext: Already composing the courier dispatch in his head.
dialog_draft.action_before: Rises a half-second after Nicholas. Hands clasped.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Imperial indifference — a dinner-table decree condemns thousands while the Emperor moves on to ballet
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Cold candlelight; tall dark uniforms against white tablecloth; AMBASSADOR's glance to aide held one beat too long; Nicholas's back already turning

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. CHECHEN FOOTHILLS - DAWN TO MIDDAY
adapted_scenes.action: A Russian infantry column moves through open meadow, four battalions wide, guns jolting on frozen earth. BUTLER marches at the head of the 5th Company, black coat sharp against the grey line of soldiers. Bayonets catch the rising sun — blinding flashes of light. A gazelle bursts from a bramble patch, two soldiers break ranks laughing, chase it with bayonets raised. It escapes into the hills. Butler watches it go. He does not laugh. Ahead: dark forest-clad hills, snow peaks beyond. The column swallows the distance.

emotional_beats.beat: Butler watches the gazelle escape
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The gazelle's freedom contrasts with the column's mechanical advance
emotional_beats.character_feels: Unconscious longing — he cannot name what the animal's escape costs him

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The romance of war meeting the reality of the machine — beauty and violence, side by side, indistinguishable
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Bayonet-flash sunlight; grey column against white peaks; gazelle as white-and-grey streak vanishing uphill; Butler still while all move

inner_monologue_converted.original: He was filled with a buoyant sense of the joy of living, the danger of death, a wish for action
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Butler watches the fleeing gazelle in stillness while laughing soldiers chase it — his stillness is the subtext

adapted_scenes.transition: The column's rhythmic tramp carries into next scene — sound before image

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: EXT. STUBBLE MAIZE FIELD NEAR FOREST - DAY
adapted_scenes.action_description: A column of Russian infantry crosses a harvested maize field toward a tree-line. Butler walks beside a comrade, head high, almost smiling. A whistling shell tears the dirt twenty paces off the road; a baggage horse rears. Mounted Chechens spill from the forest under banners — among them a large green one. The OLD SERGEANT-MAJOR squints, points it out. The LITTLE GENERAL canters up on his ambler and stabs a gloved finger to the right. Butler salutes too crisply, wheels his company toward the ravine. Behind them, two Russian cannon cough; grey smoke unrolls down the valley. The horsemen check, swing back uphill. Butler raises his sabre. His soldiers fire. Powder smoke swallows the slope.

dialog_draft.line: It's beginning.
Green banner, your honour. That'll be Shamil himself.
Splendid. Splendid morning for it.
Your company — to the right. Meet them on the slope.
At once, sir.
Steady, my dear fellows. Steady.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: intoxicated joy of first real fire — empire as romance, not yet as cost
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Low autumn light, stubble gold, two greys of cannon smoke unrolling; green banner against pale sky; Butler's bright face among older, harder ones.

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: EXT. DESERTED AOUL ON RAVINE SLOPE - LATER
adapted_scenes.action_description: Butler's company runs into the village behind the Cossacks. Saklyas stand open. A clay oven is still warm; an unfinished loaf lies beside it. A child's wooden spoon in the dust. Soldiers move from house to house, find no one. An OFFICER relays the order. Men strike sparks into hay-stacks, set torches to corn-cribs and to the saklya thatch. Smoke columns rise. Butler stands in the lane, sabre still drawn, not fighting now. He watches a wall of flame swallow a doorway hung with a bright cloth. He coughs in the pungent smoke, lifts his sleeve to his mouth. He does not lower the sabre. He does not put it away.

dialog_draft.line: Burn the corn. Burn the hay. The saklyas too.
All of them?
All of them. Orders.
Of course. C'est la guerre.
They went up the ravine, your honour. Took the children.
Quite so. Quite so.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: the first crack in the imperial idyll — courtesy survives, conviction does not
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Warm oven, small spoon, bright doorway-cloth catching fire; smoke stacks black against blue; Butler lit orange, sabre still up.

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: EXT. CHECHEN AOUL — DAY
adapted_scenes.action_description: The aoul burns. Smoke billows from saklyas; soldiers drag out sacks, chase and shoot chickens. Officers sit beyond the smoke on a rise, eating honeycomb from a board, watching the village die. Butler leans back on one elbow, chewing, squinting at the haze. A corpse lies at the edge of his sightline — a mountaineer, hand upturned, dark stain spreading under his head. Butler's gaze slides past it without stopping. He reaches for more honeycomb. The sergeant-major refills cups. The major says something; Butler laughs.

dialog_draft.line: You see, my dear sir — it's not as it is with you in Petersburg.
dialog_draft.subtext: You think this is adventure. I have seen too many of these.
dialog_draft.action_before: The major gestures toward the smoke with his cup, unhurried.

dialog_draft.line: On the contrary. Petersburg is where one dies slowly. This at least has — clarity.
dialog_draft.subtext: He means: here he feels alive, chosen, clean. He does not see the corpse.
dialog_draft.action_before: Butler tears another comb from the board, does not look toward the body.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Willed blindness — the imperial soldier's self-protecting romance with war
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Amber honeycomb and officers' ease in foreground; burning saklyas and waxen upturned hand in soft focus background. Late afternoon haze. No eye contact with dead.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. MAJOR PETROV'S QUARTERS - FORT - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.scene_heading: INT. MAJOR PETROV'S QUARTERS - FORT - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: Tallow candles burn low over the cleared dinner table. Marya Dmitrievna stacks plates with quick, practiced hands. Major Petrov has slumped sideways, mouth open, snoring against the wall. Butler sits stiffly upright, uniform still buttoned, glass refilled. He watches Marya's freckled wrists move and looks away too fast. He sets the glass down with deliberate care, stands too quickly, smile a touch too bright. He bows from the waist, hand pressed flat to his tunic. Marya does not look up; she drags a corner of her apron across a wine ring on the wood. Butler walks out unsteadily. Off-screen, his boots find the boards, then silence. Marya covers Petrov with a shawl, blows out the candle.
adapted_scenes.action_description: Tallow candles burn low. Marya Dmitrievna stacks plates. Major Petrov slumps sideways snoring. Butler sits stiffly upright, uniform buttoned, glass refilled. He watches Marya's wrists, looks away. Sets glass down with care, stands too quickly. Bows from the waist, hand on tunic. Marya does not look up; drags her apron across a wine ring. Butler walks out unsteadily. Marya covers Petrov with a shawl, blows out the candle.

dialog_draft.line: Pour me another, mon cher — the chikhir's the only honest creditor I've got tonight.
dialog_draft.subtext: Dodging the ledger of debts and the burnt aoul his column left that morning.
dialog_draft.action_before: Pushes his glass forward without meeting Petrov's eye.

dialog_draft.line: Did you see them ride out today? Banners, the whole forest moving — c'est la guerre, and by God it's beautiful.
dialog_draft.subtext: Reassuring himself that leaving the Guards was not a ruinous mistake.
dialog_draft.action_before: Stands too quickly, smile a touch too bright.

dialog_draft.line: Marya Dmitrievna — forgive me — a splendid dinner. The major is fortunate. Truly fortunate.
dialog_draft.subtext: Pressing down a desire he will not name; protecting Petrov by overdoing the courtesy.
dialog_draft.action_before: Bows from the waist, hand pressed flat to his tunic.

dialog_draft.line: Well, then — batyushka, go to bed. Sleep is cheaper than wine.
dialog_draft.subtext: I see you. Go before you say what you cannot take back.
dialog_draft.action_before: Wipes the wine ring without lifting her eyes.

dialog_draft.line: It's beginning to be a long evening. I'll turn in — one needs a clear head for the morning, mon cher.
dialog_draft.subtext: Fleeing before the wine loosens what he keeps locked: the boy on the horse, the wailing woman.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sets the glass down with deliberate care.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Cheerful evasion collapsing into private shame; domestic order maintained over a moral hollow.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Low candle warmth, soldier's parade-coat against domestic clutter; freckled wrists, wine ring, untouched bread.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. RUINED AOUL - SADO'S SAKLYA - DAY
adapted_scenes.scene_heading: EXT. RUINED AOUL - SADO'S SAKLYA - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Smoke still drifts from blackened beams. The roof of the saklya has fallen in. The fountain runs murky; the mosque doors stand defiled. Sado's son lies on a horse covered with a barka, carried into the courtyard. The dead boy's mother stands over the body, smock torn at the breast, hair loose, fingernails dragging blood from her own face. She does not stop wailing. The grandfather sits against a scorched wall, whittling a stick, eyes fixed on nothing. The burnt apiary smolders behind him. Sado works a spade into hard ground beside the broken doorpost. Hadji Murad enters on foot, leading his horse. He sees the boy. He sees the mother. He stops. Eldar halts behind him, silent. Hadji Murad walks to Sado, takes the spade from his hands, and digs.
adapted_scenes.action_description: Smoke drifts from blackened beams. Roof of the saklya fallen in. Fountain runs murky; mosque doors defiled. Sado's son lies on a horse covered with a barka. The mother stands over the body, smock torn, hair loose, nails dragging blood from her face, wailing. Grandfather whittles a stick, eyes on nothing. Burnt apiary smolders. Sado works a spade into hard ground. Hadji Murad enters on foot leading his horse. Sees the boy. Sees the mother. Stops. Eldar halts behind him. Hadji Murad walks to Sado, takes the spade, and digs.

dialog_draft.line: Sado. Put down the spade. I will dig with you.
dialog_draft.subtext: The debt is mine. I brought the soldiers to your gate by coming here.
dialog_draft.action_before: Steps over the broken doorpost; sees the boy.

dialog_draft.line: The boy looked at me as if I were already a song. Now he is the silent one, and I am still talking.
dialog_draft.subtext: I should have died in his place; my survival is the obscenity here.
dialog_draft.action_before: Touches the barka covering the child's face.

dialog_draft.line: Tell the mother nothing from me. Words from my mouth would be another wound.
dialog_draft.subtext: My name on her tongue would taste like the bayonet.
dialog_draft.action_before: Glances toward the wailing woman, then away.

dialog_draft.line: My house was your house. What is left, Allah saw.
dialog_draft.subtext: I do not blame you. The vow stands. The price is mine to carry.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lays his hand flat on his chest, a long silence before the words.

dialog_draft.line: The bees also. Even the bees. Allah knows what oath I am keeping now.
dialog_draft.subtext: My bargain with the Russians has cost more than I priced; the ledger has turned against me.
dialog_draft.action_before: Draws his palms slowly down his beard.

dialog_draft.line: When the grave is closed, I ride at dawn. My family still waits in Vedeno.
dialog_draft.subtext: I cannot stop; if I stop to grieve I will see what I have become.
dialog_draft.action_before: Takes the spade from Sado's hands.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The protagonist confronts the price of his alliance — a child dead, a host's house ruined, a vow turned ledger.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Smoke, scorched apricot trees, defiled fountain; barka over a child's face; silent grandfather with stick; spade in hard earth.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. FORT OUTSKIRTS — DAWN
adapted_scenes.action: Butler steps out through the back porch into bright morning air. He squints against the whitewashed walls blazing in early sun, then turns left toward the dark ascending ridgelines and the distant snow peaks faint as clouds. He breathes deeply, hands loose at his sides — a man content. He tilts his head back. A faint smell of smoke from the direction of the hills. He does not look that way. He rolls his shoulders, straightens his jacket, and walks toward Petrov's quarters with the easy stride of a man who slept well.

emotional_beats.beat: Butler savors the mountain morning — beauty, clean air, the thrill of having survived
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Unease: the same hills that look beautiful to Butler are the hills they burned yesterday
emotional_beats.character_feels: Satisfied, alive, quietly proud

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Moral vacancy dressed as contentment — empire's violence absorbed into routine
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Smoke haze over distant ridge, barely visible. Fort walls white and loud. Butler's back to the hills as he walks away.

inner_monologue_converted.original: He was pleased that he had behaved so well in yesterday's affair; pleased to remember how Marya Dmitrievna had treated them at dinner on their return
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Butler's relaxed stride and backward glance at the mountains — he turns away from the smoke, toward breakfast

adapted_scenes.transition: Butler's easy walk toward the fort door cuts to: the Chechen aoul — polluted fountain, weeping women, children watching in silence

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. FORT STREET — DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Butler strolls the dusty street, loose-limbed after the raid, hat pushed back. He hears hoofbeats before he sees them — a column of Cossacks, then two riders parting the dust ahead. The first: a tall figure in a white Circassian coat, papakha, a chestnut horse with a near-silver mane moving in tight, controlled steps. Butler stops, reading the horse first. The officer beside the rider gestures toward the commandant's house. Hadji Murad turns his gaze on Butler — unhurried, measuring — and nudges his horse forward. He extends two fingers of his right hand, whip hanging from them, the gesture neither submission nor greeting but something in between.

dialog_draft.line: Kotkildy.
dialog_draft.subtext: I know who you are. I am not afraid of you.
dialog_draft.action_before: Butler straightens slightly, meets Hadji Murad's eyes.

dialog_draft.line: Saubul.
dialog_draft.subtext: Acknowledged. You are worth noting.
dialog_draft.action_before: Hadji Murad nods once — slow, deliberate — then takes Butler's hand, fingers closing briefly.

dialog_draft.line: Are you the chief?
dialog_draft.subtext: I deal with the man who decides, no one else.
dialog_draft.action_before: Eyes move past Butler toward the house.

dialog_draft.line: No. The chief is in there.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am small enough to be honest with you.
dialog_draft.action_before: Butler tilts his head toward the commandant's door.

emotional_beats.beat: Butler's admiring eye lands on the horse before the man — beauty recognized before danger
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Anticipation; the incongruity of this figure in this dusty fort
emotional_beats.character_feels: Butler: aesthetic pleasure shading into wariness

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad extends the whip-hung hand — neither supplication nor threat
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The ambiguity of the alliance: a warrior borrowing a lesser power's door
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad: controlled, purposeful, already calculating

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Arrival of an uncapturable force into a small world
adapted_scenes.visual_note: White coat against red dust; chestnut horse flanked by drab Cossack grey; Butler's hand meeting fingers weighted by a hanging whip

inner_monologue_converted.original: Butler's admiration of Hadji Murad's horse and mixed feelings about Marya Dmitrievna
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Butler's gaze tracking the chestnut horse before rising to the rider; posture relaxing from soldier to observer

inner_monologue_converted.original: Butler recognizing Hadji Murad's greatness and surprise at seeing him here
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Butler's stillness as the column approaches — he stops mid-stride, hat tilted, watching

adapted_scenes.transition: Hadji Murad's horse steps past Butler into shadow of the commandant's doorway — Butler watches him go, hand still warm from the grip

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT./INT. FORT QUARTERS — PETROV'S HOUSE — DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Hadji Murad sits his horse at the porch, Eldar flanking him. Both still. Butler pushes the front door — locked. He circles the building and finds Marya Dmitrievna in the kitchen, sleeves rolled, pressing pastry with floury hands. She stares at him a beat before her expression shifts. She pats her hair, smooths her apron. In the back room, Major Petrov sits up in bed, rolls a cigarette with practiced slowness, and calls for his orderly. The orderly brings a bottle. Petrov drinks, bites bread, stands.

dialog_draft.line: Hadji Murad. At your porch. Right now.
dialog_draft.subtext: I need you to register the absurdity of this.
dialog_draft.action_before: Butler leans into the kitchen doorframe, brushing flour off his sleeve.

dialog_draft.line: Invent something else.
dialog_draft.subtext: Men always bring chaos wrapped as news.
dialog_draft.action_before: She does not look up. Her hands keep pressing.

dialog_draft.line: He is at the door. On a horse.
dialog_draft.subtext: I need her to move.
dialog_draft.action_before: Butler steps into the kitchen fully, dropping the facade of urgency.

dialog_draft.line: Well, so much the better.
dialog_draft.subtext: This is not my problem to solve.
dialog_draft.action_before: She checks her hairpins, turns back to the dough.

dialog_draft.line: There is nothing so bad as mixing.
dialog_draft.subtext: The war can wait until I have dealt with my head.
dialog_draft.action_before: Petrov pulls on his coat, watches the orderly pour.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad waits in silence at the porch — the most dangerous man in the Caucasus, reduced to knocking on a locked door.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Uncomfortable irony — the asymmetry between his reputation and this domestic scene.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad: contained, watching. He has waited in worse places.

emotional_beats.beat: Marya Dmitrievna's non-reaction — she keeps pressing pastry while the mountain chief waits outside.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Wry recognition — domestic reality defeats imperial mythology.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Marya Dmitrievna: unfazed, faintly annoyed.

emotional_beats.beat: Petrov drinks before facing his guest — the empire's representative is a hungover major.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Deflating irony — this is the great Russian power Hadji Murad has defected to.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Petrov: grumbling, resigned.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Deflation — the empire's local face is a locked door, flour-dusted hands, and a vodka bottle.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Flour cloud in kitchen light. Hadji Murad motionless at porch, papakha high. Vodka bottle beside Petrov's bedside candle.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Marya Dmitrievna's domesticity — rolling pastry while a warlord waits
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Her hands never stop; she checks her hair only after she decides to help — priority is visible in the order of her actions.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Petrov grumbling about the authorities who sent 'that devil' to him
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Petrov drinks in silence before standing — complaint collapsed into ritual.

adapted_scenes.transition: Petrov buttons his coat and walks toward the parlor door — camera holds on the vodka bottle left on the table as his footsteps fade.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. MAJOR PETROV'S PARLOR — FRONTIER FORT — DAY
adapted_scenes.action: The major enters, still chewing rye bread, and reads the order without sitting down. He passes his eyes between the paper and Hadji Murad three times — measuring a horse, not greeting a man. Hadji Murad stands motionless, one hand resting on the hilt of his dagger, a faint contemptuous smile fixed at the middle distance. Marya Dmitrievna appears in the kitchen doorway, wiping her hands on a cloth. She looks at Hadji Murad; he looks back — a full second — and she turns away first. Butler steps aside to let the room settle.

dialog_draft.line: Yakshi, Bek. Yakshi. He stays. Butler — the office, or the storeroom?
dialog_draft.subtext: Treating Hadji Murad as a problem to be shelved, not a man to be addressed.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sets the order face-down on the table. Does not offer a chair.

dialog_draft.line: Keep him here. The guest chamber. Then at least we know where he is.
dialog_draft.subtext: She is already managing the major's incompetence; she wants the prisoner visible, not lost in some back office.
dialog_draft.action_before: Moves one step into the room — not invited, not asking.

dialog_draft.line: Women have no business here.
dialog_draft.subtext: He knows she is right. That is the problem.
dialog_draft.action_before: Waves her away without looking at her.

dialog_draft.line: It is the same to me. I need only what the Sirdar permitted — men who can carry word to the mountains.
dialog_draft.subtext: The lodging is beneath discussion. The only currency he recognises is access.
dialog_draft.action_before: Speaks to Butler, not the major. Does not turn his head.

emotional_beats.beat: Major reads order, treats Hadji Murad as cargo
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Quiet indignation — the measure of the man is already visible in how he is received
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad: contempt locked behind stillness

emotional_beats.beat: Marya Dmitrievna overrides the major, is dismissed
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Unexpected alliance — she and Hadji Murad share the same disregard for Petrov's bluster
emotional_beats.character_feels: Marya Dmitrievna: satisfaction; Hadji Murad: first flicker of attention toward her

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The hierarchy of dignity: who is measured, who measures, who refuses to be measured at all
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Cramped parlor, whitewashed walls, oil lamp unlit in daylight. Bread and vodka still on table. Hadji Murad's papakha the tallest object in the room.

inner_monologue_converted.original: From the first he was repelled by and contemptuous of the major
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Hadji Murad addresses Butler, not Petrov — visible social cut

inner_monologue_converted.original: Marya Dmitrievna pleased him particularly — he liked her simplicity
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Eye contact held one second, then she turns; he does not

adapted_scenes.transition: Marya Dmitrievna disappears back into the kitchen; the sound of a ladle on iron carries into the silence of the parlor

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. MAJOR PETROV'S QUARTERS - FORT - EVENING
adapted_scenes.action: Marya Dmitrievna sets a covered dish before Hadji Murad, freckles flushed. He keeps his eyes on the bread, breaking it deliberately. Butler leans in, half-eager, half-shy, refilling Hadji Murad's cup with kvass instead of wine. Major Petrov, already drinking, watches from across the table with thickening attention. A spy in a sheepskin coat slips in from the corridor, bows, whispers in Avar at Hadji Murad's ear. Hadji Murad's hands stop. He draws his palms slowly down his beard. Marya catches the change and stills, dish in hand. Butler glances between them, pretending not to see.

dialog_draft.line: Eat, batyushka. It is still warm.
dialog_draft.subtext: I see your news is bad — stay in this room with me a little longer.
dialog_draft.action_before: Pushes the dish closer, not looking at Petrov.

dialog_draft.line: Mon cher — your spy travels faster than our couriers. Saubul.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am awed and ashamed of being awed.
dialog_draft.action_before: Self-deprecating laugh, taps the rim of his glass.

dialog_draft.line: Butler. They are still in Vedeno. The imam has returned.
dialog_draft.subtext: The wound is Yusuf; I will not name him here.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lowers the bread, looks past Butler at the wall.

dialog_draft.line: Brothers — pour. Vedeno is far. Tonight we eat.
dialog_draft.subtext: Do not let me notice what this evening is becoming.
dialog_draft.action_before: Drains his glass, signals for another.

dialog_draft.line: Allah knows. It is so.
dialog_draft.subtext: The topic is closed; the calculation begins now.
dialog_draft.action_before: Palms drawn slowly down beard.

dialog_draft.line: God see them. Eat, all of you.
dialog_draft.subtext: I have run out of arguments — I have started to take his side.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sets the dish down hard, face red, will not meet Petrov's eye.

emotional_beats.beat: Spy delivers news of family in Vedeno
emotional_beats.audience_feels: dread tightening
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad: the trap closing

emotional_beats.beat: Marya silently chooses Hadji Murad over her own table
emotional_beats.audience_feels: moral pivot
emotional_beats.character_feels: Marya: doubt about her side

emotional_beats.beat: Petrov drowns the room in liquor
emotional_beats.audience_feels: decay of empire's edge
emotional_beats.character_feels: Petrov: receding dignity

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A man hears his family is still hostage; a Russian woman, watching, quietly defects in her heart.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Lamp-lit table, kvass and vodka side by side, sheepskin coat by the door, samovar steam, Marya's red freckled flush against pale linen.

inner_monologue_converted.original: He liked her simplicity and especially the foreign type of her beauty
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Hadji Murad keeps his eyes on the bread; his hands stop when she stills.

inner_monologue_converted.original: The news he received through the spies was not good
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Spy whispers; Hadji Murad's palms slowly down his beard.

adapted_scenes.transition: Spy's whisper of 'Vedeno' carries across the cut into rifle-fire of Shamil's caracoling murids.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. VEDENO - SHAMIL'S RESIDENCE - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Riflefire and pistols crack into a winter sky. Shamil rides at the centre of caracoling murids who circle him firing into the air, chanting La ilaha illa Allah. Villagers crowd rooftops, answering with their own volleys. Two empty saddles ride beside him — the dead murids' horses, led on long reins. Shamil sits motionless, upright, beard ungloved, eyes fixed on the gate of his compound. He does not return the salute. As he dismounts, a MURID kneels and presents a sealed list of prisoners. Shamil takes it without looking. From a low pit nearby, a hand reaches up between iron bars — Yusuf, unseen by Shamil, listening to the hooves above.

dialog_draft.line: Imam — victory. The Russians fled before us.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am rehearsing the version of the battle that you require.
dialog_draft.action_before: Kneels, eyes lowered, presents the list with both hands.

dialog_draft.line: It is written. Allah has decided.
dialog_draft.subtext: The defeat will be called victory because I say it is.
dialog_draft.action_before: Strokes beard once, slowly, takes the list without breaking gaze on the gate.

dialog_draft.line: The traitor's son. He waits still in the pit?
dialog_draft.subtext: I am preparing his sentence; respect for the father is the most efficient form of erasure.
dialog_draft.action_before: Pauses one long beat at the threshold.

dialog_draft.line: He waits, Imam. As you commanded.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will not say his name; I know what is coming.
dialog_draft.action_before: Rises, eyes still down.

dialog_draft.line: (below, to himself) My father — Inshallah. Not afraid. Not afraid.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is asking the unseen listener — God, his father — to stay a little longer.
dialog_draft.action_before: Grips an iron bar, knuckles white, stares up at hoof-shadows on the grating.

dialog_draft.line: So.
dialog_draft.subtext: Verdict pronounced; the matter is closed.
dialog_draft.action_before: Turns from the courier, enters the compound; the gate closes.

emotional_beats.beat: Defeat dressed as triumph
emotional_beats.audience_feels: religious power as performance
emotional_beats.character_feels: Shamil: a verdict already in him

emotional_beats.beat: Shamil names Yusuf only as 'the traitor's son'
emotional_beats.audience_feels: cold descent toward sentence
emotional_beats.character_feels: Shamil: closing the door

emotional_beats.beat: Yusuf hears hooves but cannot see his fate
emotional_beats.audience_feels: claustrophobic dread
emotional_beats.character_feels: Yusuf: a boy underneath listening

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Authority pronouncing itself as God; a son in a pit hearing the verdict he cannot yet name.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Hard winter light, gunpowder smoke pluming over flat roofs, two riderless horses, iron grating over earth pit, Shamil's stillness at the centre of motion.

inner_monologue_converted.original: according to the Russians he had been vanquished... according to him and all the murids he had been victorious
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Caracoling murids fire into the sky; two empty saddles ride beside Shamil.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Yusuf...put in prison — that is, into a pit more than seven feet deep
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: A hand reaches up between iron bars; hoof-shadows fall across his face.

adapted_scenes.transition: Iron-grate shadow over Yusuf's face dissolves to lamp-grate shadow on Hadji Murad's hands at the fort.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. VEDENO AOUL — DAY / INT. SAKLYA / INT. PIT — CONTINUOUS
adapted_scenes.scene_heading: EXT. VEDENO AOUL — DAY / INT. SAKLYA / INT. PIT — CONTINUOUS
adapted_scenes.action: Villagers crowd rooftops and lanes. Rifle shots crack the air. SHAMIL rides a white Arab on a red leather bridle, plain brown cloak, white turban, immovable face — eyes fixed past the crowd. MURIDS in gold and silver caracole around him, chanting. INSIDE the saklya, two of HADJI MURAD'S WIVES press to the lattice, watching. PATIMAT sits on the earth floor, grey hair loose, arms locked around her knees, staring into dying embers. She does not turn. BELOW GROUND, in the fetid pit, YUSUF presses his face to a slit of light. The chanting reaches him muffled. Other prisoners stir in the dark. He grips the wood until his knuckles whiten. Outside, Shamil dismounts without looking at anyone. The cheering swells.
adapted_scenes.action_description: Villagers crowd rooftops. Rifle shots. SHAMIL rides a white Arab, plain brown cloak, white turban, face immovable. MURIDS in gold and silver caracole around him, chanting. Inside the saklya, two of HADJI MURAD'S WIVES watch through the lattice. PATIMAT sits on the floor, grey hair loose, arms around her knees, staring into the embers. She does not turn. Below ground, in the pit, YUSUF presses his face to a slit of light, the chanting reaching him muffled. He grips the wood until his knuckles whiten. Shamil dismounts without looking at anyone.

dialog_draft.line: Lya illyah il Allah!
dialog_draft.subtext: We are God's army; the Imam is His proof.
dialog_draft.action_before: Firing pistols skyward as they circle Shamil

dialog_draft.line: He looks at no one.
dialog_draft.subtext: He has already decided about us.
dialog_draft.action_before: Touches the lattice, does not lean closer

dialog_draft.line: Let the embers go out.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will not warm a house that hosts him.
dialog_draft.action_before: Tightens her arms around her knees, eyes on the ash

dialog_draft.line: Sit down, boy. They are not singing for you.
dialog_draft.subtext: Hope here is the cruelest thing.
dialog_draft.action_before: Spits, turns his back to the slit of light

dialog_draft.line: My father — my father rides better than him.
dialog_draft.subtext: If I say it loud enough I will not weep.
dialog_draft.action_before: Wipes his face with the back of his hand, knuckles white on the wood

dialog_draft.line: It is so. Allah has decided.
dialog_draft.subtext: Hadji Murad's death is now a matter of timing.
dialog_draft.action_before: Strokes his beard once, slowly, before stepping down from the saddle

emotional_beats.beat: Shamil enters as God's instrument; the aoul converts his pageantry into authority
emotional_beats.audience_feels: dread of consolidated power
emotional_beats.character_feels: cold certainty

emotional_beats.beat: Patimat's refusal — silent dissent at the hearth
emotional_beats.audience_feels: grief
emotional_beats.character_feels: unyielding hatred

emotional_beats.beat: Yusuf's bravado collapsing into a boy at the slit
emotional_beats.audience_feels: pity
emotional_beats.character_feels: envy and despair

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Triumph as verdict — Shamil's arrival seals Hadji Murad's family inside a closing fist.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: White horse / red bridle as sole color against drab cloaks; dying embers in saklya; single shaft of dust-light into the pit; intercut on chanting.

inner_monologue_converted.original: she had always hated Shamil, and now she hated him more than ever and had no wish to see him
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Patimat refuses to rise; stares into dying embers; will not turn toward the window

inner_monologue_converted.original: endured tortures such as can only be felt by the young who are full of vitality and deprived of freedom
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Yusuf's white-knuckled grip on the wooden slit; bravado breaks mid-sentence about his father

adapted_scenes.transition: The chanting outside fades into the steady scratch of a quill — match-cut to the next scene's writing implement.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT./INT. SHAMIL'S COMPOUND — VEDENO — MIDDAY
adapted_scenes.action: Shamil rides through the outer gate. Petitioners and armed Lesghians press back against the walls; some drop to their knees. He passes without a glance, his face a stone mask. In the inner court he dismounts at the penthouse. Through a gap in the women's fence — barely visible — the dark eyes of AMINAL watch him. He does not look at the fence. A MURID approaches with a prayer mat, places it on the ground. Shamil stares at it a long moment, then kneels. His hands press together, but his eyes are hollow.

dialog_draft.line: The petitioners have waited since dawn, Imam.
dialog_draft.subtext: You cannot disappear into the women's quarters — they all see you.
dialog_draft.action_before: Murid sets down the prayer mat and steps back.

dialog_draft.line: They will wait until God is satisfied.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am performing this for them, not for God — and I know it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Shamil kneels onto the mat without looking at the murid.

emotional_beats.beat: Shamil rides through his kneeling court — outwardly triumphant
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Something is wrong beneath the ceremony
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hollowed out — the display is a lie he must maintain

emotional_beats.beat: Aminal's eyes through the fence — he does not turn
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The cost of the mask: even private longing must be suppressed in public
emotional_beats.character_feels: Longing and anger — he cannot be a man, only the Imam

emotional_beats.beat: He kneels to pray without devotion
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Tragic irony — the sacred leader performing sacredness as performance
emotional_beats.character_feels: Emptiness where faith once lived

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Sacred authority as prison
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Dusty courtyard, harsh noon light; kneeling figures cast short shadows; women's fence — a single dark eye visible in a crack; prayer mat on bare earth

inner_monologue_converted.original: He only desired one thing: rest and the delights of family life, and the caresses of his favorite wife
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: His eyes move once toward the women's fence, then deliberately away — he kneels to pray

inner_monologue_converted.original: He knew very well that his campaign had been unsuccessful
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: His face is stone as people kneel — no acknowledgment, no warmth; the mask is effort, not ease

adapted_scenes.transition: The sound of Shamil's murmured prayer carries under a cut to a different world — Russian voices, lantern light

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: INT. SHAMIL'S GUEST CHAMBER - VEDENO - DAY
adapted_scenes.action_description: Six old council members in tall caps and Circassian coats rise as SHAMIL enters, towering a head above them. Hands lift palms upward; eyes close; a murmured prayer; palms drawn down beards in unison. They sit. Shamil takes the larger cushion. JEMAL EDDIN, white-bearded, leans toward him, speaks low. A scribe records. On a low tray: stale tea, a folded list of cases. Shamil hears each verdict without looking up: a hand for theft, a head for murder, three pardons — settled with single nods. Then Jemal Eddin sets down a second paper. The room stills. ZEIDAT lingers in the doorway with a covered dish, pretending not to listen. Shamil's hand goes to his beard. One slow stroke. He does not stroke it again.

dialog_draft.line: Hadji Murad sent men for his family. They were taken before they reached the Russian line. Mother, wives, the boy Yusuf — all at Vedeno now, under guard.
Three days the elders have waited?
Three days, Imam.
It is written: a man is known by what he reaches for when he thinks no one watches. Hadji Murad reached for the infidel.
And the boy, Imam? He is twelve.
Allah has decided. The mother and the wives — held. The boy — Shamil will see him. So.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Cold judicial calm masking a verdict already formed; a child's life weighed on a tray of stale tea.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Low cushions, tall papakhas, daggers on belts; afternoon light through narrow window; covered dish steaming faintly in doorway; stilled hand on white beard.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. SHAMIL'S COUNCIL CHAMBER - VEDENO - DAY
adapted_scenes.scene_heading: INT. SHAMIL'S COUNCIL CHAMBER - VEDENO - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Low cushions on a carpeted floor. SHAMIL sits on the largest, his councilors arrayed before him. JEMAL EDDIN reads aloud from a freshly inked proclamation; a SCRIBE waits with sealing wax. Shamil listens without expression, then nods once. The scribe presses the seal. A MURID carries the parchment out. The councilors lean in. One whispers Hadji Murad's name. Shamil's hand stills on his beard. He closes his eyes. The room falls completely silent. Outside, wind in the courtyard. A child's voice — distant, from the harem — laughs and is hushed. Shamil's eyes open. He looks past his councilors at nothing. He strokes his beard once, slowly.
adapted_scenes.action_description: Low cushions on a carpeted floor. Shamil sits on the largest, his councilors arrayed before him. Jemal Eddin reads a proclamation; the scribe seals it. A murid carries it out. The councilors turn to the next matter. One whispers Hadji Murad's name. Shamil's hand stills on his beard. He closes his eyes. The room falls silent. Outside: wind. From the harem, a distant child laughs and is hushed. Shamil's eyes open. He strokes his beard once, slowly.

dialog_draft.line: "...endure. It is better to die in enmity with the Russians than to live with the Unbelievers."
dialog_draft.subtext: Reading Shamil's will as God's will.
dialog_draft.action_before: Holds the parchment at arm's length, voice carrying.

dialog_draft.line: So. Send it to every aul.
dialog_draft.subtext: Approval is dispatch — no debate invited.
dialog_draft.action_before: A single nod toward the scribe.

dialog_draft.line: Imam — and Hadji Murad?
dialog_draft.subtext: Naming the wound everyone has been circling.
dialog_draft.action_before: Leans forward, voice low.

dialog_draft.line: Hadji Murad loves his son.
dialog_draft.subtext: Yusuf is the lever; the decision is already made.
dialog_draft.action_before: His hand stops mid-stroke on his beard.

dialog_draft.line: Bring him back, Imam?
dialog_draft.subtext: Asking for the verdict the Imam will not say plainly.
dialog_draft.action_before: Glances at the others, then at Shamil.

dialog_draft.line: Allah has decided. The boy will write to his father. Enough.
dialog_draft.subtext: Yusuf will be the bait; HM dies on return. The matter is closed.
dialog_draft.action_before: Closes his eyes; opens them; looks at no one.

emotional_beats.beat: Proclamation sealed and dispatched.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The machinery of holy war is bureaucratic.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Shamil — administrative satisfaction.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad's name enters the room.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Dread.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Shamil — a private, unadmitted ache.

emotional_beats.beat: Shamil names Yusuf without naming the killing.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Cold recognition: the son is the weapon.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Shamil — resolve disguised as prophecy.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Sacred authority transmuting personal need into a death sentence — a father's love weaponized against another father.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Low afternoon light through narrow windows. Carpets, cushions, sealing wax, parchment. Shamil's stillness as the scene's center of gravity. A single distant child's laugh as the only warmth.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Shamil knew Hadji Murad's son was loved passionately and could be the lever.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: A distant child's laugh from the harem; Shamil's hand stilling on his beard at the mention of Yusuf.

inner_monologue_converted.original: The councilors knew his closed eyes meant the Prophet was speaking to him.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Total silence in the chamber; no one moves while his eyes are closed.

adapted_scenes.transition: Wind in the chamber carries to wind across a Tiflis courtyard — Shamil's silence into Hadji Murad's.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. SHAMIL'S GUEST CHAMBER - VEDENO - DAY
adapted_scenes.scene_heading: INT. SHAMIL'S GUEST CHAMBER - VEDENO - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Shamil sits cross-legged on a low cushion, eyes closed. The councilors wait in silence. After a long pause, Shamil opens his eyes — narrowed to slits — and strokes his beard once, slowly. Jemal Eddin gestures toward the doorway. YUSUF enters: thin, pale, ragged, the smell of the pit on him. Black eyes burning. He stops, meets Shamil's half-closed gaze, then crosses the carpet and kisses the Imam's long-fingered hand. A scribe sets paper, reed pen, and an inkpot before the boy on a low writing-board. Yusuf kneels at the board. Shamil watches him without moving. Outside, the courtyard fountain ticks water onto stone.
adapted_scenes.action_description: Shamil pronounces in cold cadence; Yusuf takes dictation, the pen scratching each verdict onto paper.

dialog_draft.line: Thou art Hadji Murad's son.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am summoning him through you — you are no longer a boy, you are a message.
dialog_draft.action_before: Strokes beard once, slowly. Eyes barely open.

dialog_draft.line: I am, Imam.
dialog_draft.subtext: I have rehearsed obedience for months. Watch how well I do it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Bows his head, hands flat on the writing-board.

dialog_draft.line: Canst thou write?
dialog_draft.subtext: Allah has decided. The pen is already in your hand.
dialog_draft.action_before: Nods once toward the scribe's reed.

dialog_draft.line: I was preparing myself to be a Mullah —
dialog_draft.subtext: Please remember that. Please.
dialog_draft.action_before: Picks up the reed. The tip trembles before it touches paper.

dialog_draft.line: Write to thy father. If he returns before Bairam, all shall be as it was. If not — thy grandmother, thy mother, and the rest go to the aouls. And thee — I will behead. Allah has decided.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am preparing the audience for his execution; the offer of mercy is the most efficient form of erasure.
dialog_draft.action_before: Frowns. The beard-stroke ceases. Stillness.

dialog_draft.line: Add this. I have had pity on thee, and will not kill thee — only put out thine eyes, as I do to all traitors. So. Go.
dialog_draft.subtext: Mercy and mutilation in one breath — let Hadji Murad read both and choose.
dialog_draft.action_before: Closes his eyes again. The verdict is sealed.

emotional_beats.beat: Shamil's silence becomes a verdict before any word is spoken
emotional_beats.audience_feels: dread, ceremony
emotional_beats.character_feels: (Yusuf) tremulous veneration curdling into recognition

emotional_beats.beat: Yusuf takes dictation of the threat against his own body
emotional_beats.audience_feels: horror at the boy's stillness
emotional_beats.character_feels: (Yusuf) the hostage's discipline — not a muscle stirs

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A son made into the instrument of his father's death sentence — sacred ritual as political execution
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Low light through narrow window. Reed pen, inkpot, paper on a wooden board. Shamil's still hands; Yusuf's trembling one. Carpet, cushions, beads.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Yusuf's tremulous veneration for the Imam, his admiration out of defiance to his father
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He kisses Shamil's hand without prompting, kneels at the writing-board before he is told

inner_monologue_converted.original: Councilors knew Shamil was listening to the Prophet
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Sustained silence; nobody moves until Shamil opens his eyes

adapted_scenes.transition: The reed pen scratches the final word; the sound carries into the next scene as another quill, in another office, scratches across Russian dispatch paper.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. SHAMIL'S GUEST CHAMBER / OUTSIDE PIT - VEDENO - DUSK
adapted_scenes.action: Shamil sits motionless on his cushions, gaze fixed on YUSUF — bound, shivering, smaller than the room. A SCRIBE waits, quill poised. Shamil strokes his beard once, slowly. He speaks without raising his voice. The SCRIBE's pen scratches. A MURID lifts Yusuf to his feet. Outside the chamber, in a stone passage, Yusuf twists free, snatches the attendant's dagger and drives it toward his own throat. Two MURIDS catch his arm, wrench the blade away, force him down. They drag him across torchlit ground back to the pit's iron grate, lower him into the dark. CUT TO Shamil's quarters: he stands in a white fur cloak in his wife AMINAL's empty room. He waits. From across the courtyard Aminal watches him through Zeidat's doorway, smiling softly. He waits longer. He leaves.

dialog_draft.line: Write that I have had pity. Write that I will not kill him.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am erasing him with mercy.
dialog_draft.action_before: Strokes his beard once, slowly.

dialog_draft.line: His eyes — as with all traitors. Write it. Go.
dialog_draft.subtext: His father will read this and break.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lifts one finger from his knee.

dialog_draft.line: My father — my father will —
dialog_draft.subtext: Please come for me.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lunges, dagger flashing toward his own throat.

dialog_draft.line: Hold him. Hold him.
dialog_draft.subtext: Don't let him cheat the verdict.
dialog_draft.action_before: Clamps both hands on Yusuf's wrist.

emotional_beats.beat: Shamil pronounces blinding as mercy.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: cold dread
emotional_beats.character_feels: Shamil: certainty; Yusuf: collapse

emotional_beats.beat: Yusuf's suicide attempt fails.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: pity, helplessness
emotional_beats.character_feels: Yusuf: shame, terror

emotional_beats.beat: Shamil's white figure waits in vain for Aminal.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: uneasy intimacy
emotional_beats.character_feels: Shamil: small, human, unseen

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Sacred cruelty as bureaucracy; the prophet alone in his white cloak.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Low oil lamps; white fur cloak against dark stone; iron pit-grate; quill on parchment; torch shadows on cell wall.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Yusuf appeared calm but rushed to stab himself
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Composure inside the chamber; violent self-attempt the moment the door closes

inner_monologue_converted.original: Aminal angry over silk given to Zeidat
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: She watches him search her room and silently refuses to appear

adapted_scenes.transition: Shamil's solitary white figure dissolves into morning light at the Russian fort.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. MAJOR'S HOUSE - FORT - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: A week later. The Russian fort. MARYA DMITRIEVNA kneads dough at a low table, sleeves pushed up, a flush across her freckles. KHANEFI stands in her kitchen doorway holding a copper bowl, jaw tight. She points him out without looking up. He does not move. She steps toward him; he sets a hand on the dagger at his belt. A breath. She points again — out. He goes. ELDAR enters from the corridor carrying Hadji Murad's tray, smiles shyly, takes it through to the inner room. BUTLER appears at the kitchen door, freshly shaved, uniform brushed. Marya Dmitrievna does not turn. From the inner room HADJI MURAD's low voice can be heard greeting Eldar in Avar. Butler hesitates at the threshold. Marya wipes her hands on her apron, fills a small glass of tea, sets it on the table for Butler without a…

dialog_draft.line: Out of my kitchen, batyushka. Out.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will not be afraid of you in my own house.
dialog_draft.action_before: Slams flour from her palms, points to the door.

dialog_draft.line: Bismillah.
dialog_draft.subtext: I yield, but only to God, not to you.
dialog_draft.action_before: Hand falls slowly from his dagger.

dialog_draft.line: Splendid morning. Is he — receiving?
dialog_draft.subtext: I came to see her. I tell myself I came to see him.
dialog_draft.action_before: Self-deprecating half-smile in the doorway.

dialog_draft.line: He is. Sit. Drink your tea, Butler.
dialog_draft.subtext: I see exactly why you came.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sets the glass down without meeting his eye.

dialog_draft.line: Any word? About the boy?
dialog_draft.subtext: I have started to grieve for your son with you.
dialog_draft.action_before: Stands at Hadji Murad's threshold, lowers her voice.

dialog_draft.line: Allah knows.
dialog_draft.subtext: Do not ask again today.
dialog_draft.action_before: Hand to chest; the smallest shake of the head.

emotional_beats.beat: Marya stands her ground against Khanefi.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: respect, tension easing
emotional_beats.character_feels: Marya: defiant, alive

emotional_beats.beat: Butler arrives shaved and shy.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: tender amusement and unease
emotional_beats.character_feels: Butler: longing, shame

emotional_beats.beat: Marya carries tea to Hadji Murad herself.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: an unsanctioned bond crossing sides
emotional_beats.character_feels: Marya: sympathy; HM: dignified gratitude

emotional_beats.beat: Butler's tea cools, untouched.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: quiet displacement
emotional_beats.character_feels: Butler: rivalry he will not name

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: An enemy guest becomes a household; allegiance begins to slip in tea-glasses and silences.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Flour-dust in slanted window light; copper bowl; glass tea in podstakannik; Avar dagger at belt; brushed Russian uniform; doorway thresholds as fault lines.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Marya quarrelled with Khanefi, who nearly killed her
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Standoff in the kitchen, hand on dagger, she points him out

inner_monologue_converted.original: She felt particular respect and sympathy for Hadji Murad
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: She carries the tea herself, lingers at his threshold

inner_monologue_converted.original: Butler had become quite friendly with Hadji Murad
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Butler shaved, brushed, hovering at the kitchen door

inner_monologue_converted.original: HM had taken a fancy to Butler, gathered from Eldar's smile
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Eldar's shy welcoming smile carries the friendship across the language barrier

adapted_scenes.transition: Butler's untouched tea cools — match-cut to a cold glass on a different table at nightfall.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. MAJOR'S QUARTERS - FORT - EVENING
adapted_scenes.action: Khanefi sits cross-legged on a mat, eyes half-closed, and sings. His high tenor fills the low-ceilinged room. Butler listens from a chair, leaning forward, hands loose between his knees. Hadji Murad stands apart, arms folded, eyes shut. The song ends on a long dying note. Silence holds. Then Hadji Murad opens his eyes and says his two words. Butler looks at the interpreter. Marya Dmitrievna moves quietly at the far table, setting out cups.

dialog_draft.line: (singing) But thou, my Brother the elder, wilt never forget — with vengeance denied me.
dialog_draft.subtext: The song is his and HM's shared history of blood — sung freely here because they are far from that history
dialog_draft.action_before: Khanefi closes his eyes, voice lifting on the last phrase

dialog_draft.line: Good song. Wise song.
dialog_draft.subtext: The song is about his own death. He knows it. He accepts it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Opens his eyes slowly; does not look at Khanefi — looks at nothing

dialog_draft.line: What does it mean — the part about the brother?
dialog_draft.subtext: He senses something dark but cannot name it
dialog_draft.action_before: Leans toward the INTERPRETER sitting beside him

dialog_draft.line: Blood feud. Between him and Khanefi. Before.
dialog_draft.subtext: Past tense — but the obligation never fully disappears
dialog_draft.action_before: Gestures briefly toward both men

dialog_draft.line: And now?
dialog_draft.subtext: He is beginning to understand that men here carry their histories in their bodies, not just their minds
dialog_draft.action_before: Does not look away from Hadji Murad

dialog_draft.line: (without turning) Now we ride.
dialog_draft.subtext: The answer to every question about the past is the same: forward motion
dialog_draft.action_before: Picks up his papakha from the bench; taps it once against his palm

emotional_beats.beat: Khanefi's song names death and blood-debt openly
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Unease at the beauty of something so violent
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad: recognition; Khanefi: release; Butler: wonder and incomprehension

emotional_beats.beat: HM's two-word verdict — 'Good song. Wise song.'
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The weight of a man who has accepted his ending
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: clarity, not grief; Butler: the first real glimpse of HM's interior

emotional_beats.beat: Butler asks; HM answers with departure
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The intimacy is real but the man is already gone
emotional_beats.character_feels: Butler: something irretrievable passing; HM: the fort was always temporary

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: intimacy at the threshold of departure — a song about death sung by the man who may die
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Low lamplight; Khanefi's face upturned while singing; HM's closed eyes; Butler's leaning posture; Marya Dmitrievna as distant domestic witness

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad always listened to this song with closed eyes
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: HM stands apart, arms folded, eyes shut — then opens them only when it ends

inner_monologue_converted.original: When it ended he always remarked: 'Good song! Wise song!'
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Verbal ritual rendered as behavioral reflex — two words, no explanation

adapted_scenes.transition: HM picks up his papakha; the room shifts from song to preparation — cut to the departure gathering already assembled

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. MAJOR PETROV'S QUARTERS — FORT — DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Officers gather around two tables — tea at one, vodka at the other. Hadji Murad enters in travel dress, limping but unhurried. He shakes hands down the line without ceremony, takes a chair by the window rather than the offered divan. A brief silence. Someone pours tea; he accepts the tumbler and sets it down untouched. Butler touches his knee in farewell. Then Eldar appears in the doorway, white burka folded over one shoulder, sword in hand. Hadji Murad rises, takes the burka, and crosses to Marya Dmitrievna — holds it out.

dialog_draft.line: Did you like Tiflis?
dialog_draft.subtext: Filling silence; he knows nothing of this man
dialog_draft.action_before: Petrovsky leans forward, wine in hand, obligatory smile.

dialog_draft.line: The dog gave meat to the ass — the ass gave hay to the dog. Both went hungry.
dialog_draft.subtext: The Russian ball was absurd to me; your customs are not mine
dialog_draft.action_before: A brief, dry smile. He glances at Marya Dmitrievna before he speaks.

dialog_draft.line: When shall we meet again?
dialog_draft.subtext: He does not expect an answer; this is already goodbye
dialog_draft.action_before: Butler sets down his glass and touches Hadji Murad's knee.

dialog_draft.line: Kunak bulug. Strong kunak. Time — ayda — go.
dialog_draft.subtext: I call you friend; that is the only word worth saying now
dialog_draft.action_before: He stands, takes the burka from Eldar, and pauses at Butler before turning.

dialog_draft.line: Oh, why?
dialog_draft.subtext: She is moved and does not know what to do with it
dialog_draft.action_before: He holds the burka out to her. She flushes and does not take it immediately.

dialog_draft.line: You praised it. It is necessary.
dialog_draft.subtext: Honor requires this; it is not a gift, it is an obligation fulfilled
dialog_draft.action_before: He waits, still holding it, patient as stone.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad enters and sits apart — the officers cannot reach him
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The distance between two worlds made physical
emotional_beats.character_feels: Composed, already elsewhere in his mind

emotional_beats.beat: The dog-and-ass proverb — laughter breaks the tension, then dies
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Brief warmth, then the reminder of incomprehension
emotional_beats.character_feels: Diplomatic, faintly amused, undeceived

emotional_beats.beat: Butler's farewell touch — the only real human contact in the scene
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Unexpected tenderness; these two actually liked each other
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM registers it; his 'kunak' is not a formula

emotional_beats.beat: The burka gift to Marya Dmitrievna — a warrior's code applied to a soldier's woman
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Dignity crossing the divide; something genuine
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: duty discharged with grace. Marya: seen, for once, by someone outside the fort's rules

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Departure as ceremony — dignity held on both sides, understanding held by neither
adapted_scenes.visual_note: White burka bright against dark officer tunics. Samovar steam. Window light behind HM — he is already half outside.

inner_monologue_converted.original: The silence that followed his entrance did not at all abash him
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: HM takes the window chair instead of the offered divan; sits with the stillness of a man who owns the room without claiming it

inner_monologue_converted.original: He fixed an indifferent gaze on the tea-table
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He accepts the tea tumbler and sets it down untouched — no gesture of refusal, no gesture of acceptance

adapted_scenes.transition: The white burka draped over Marya Dmitrievna's arms as HM walks out — cut to: open road, the burka now a receding memory

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. MAJOR PETROV'S HOUSE - PARLOR - DAY
adapted_scenes.scene_heading: INT. MAJOR PETROV'S HOUSE - PARLOR - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Hadji Murad rises. He lifts his black burka from the bench, folds it once across his arm, and steps to Marya Dmitrievna. Eldar stands at his shoulder, eyes lowered. Hadji Murad murmurs to the interpreter without looking at her, then offers the burka with both hands. Marya Dmitrievna's freckled cheeks flush. She wipes her hands on her apron before she takes it. Hadji Murad turns to the Major, slides the sheathed sword from Eldar's grip, and lays it across Petrov's palms. The Major fumbles, ashamed of his empty hands. Hadji Murad waves once before his own face — refusal — then touches the mountains beyond the window, then his heart. He walks out. Butler follows. Behind them, the officers crowd around the sword, drawing the blade an inch to read the Gurda mark.
adapted_scenes.action_description: Hadji Murad gives his burka to Marya Dmitrievna and his sword to the Major; refuses the offered horse; touches the mountains, then his heart, and walks out.

dialog_draft.line: He says: you praised the burka. Take it.
dialog_draft.subtext: A debt of hospitality being paid in cloth.
dialog_draft.action_before: Steps half a pace forward, hands behind his back.

dialog_draft.line: Oh — why?
dialog_draft.subtext: She knows why. She is buying a moment.
dialog_draft.action_before: Freckles flush; she wipes her palms on her apron.

dialog_draft.line: It is necessary. Like Adam.
dialog_draft.subtext: What is given covers what cannot be said.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lays the folded burka across her forearm with both hands.

dialog_draft.line: Well, then — thank you. God grant you rescue your son. Ulan yakshi.
dialog_draft.subtext: She has stopped being the Major's woman; she is speaking as a mother.
dialog_draft.action_before: Holds the burka against her chest, looks him in the face for the first time.

dialog_draft.line: Tell him to take my chestnut gelding. I have nothing else.
dialog_draft.subtext: Trying to match a gift he cannot match.
dialog_draft.action_before: Receives the sword across both palms, eyes wet.

dialog_draft.line: Allah knows. It is well.
dialog_draft.subtext: The matter is closed; the mountains owe him nothing.
dialog_draft.action_before: Waves his hand once before his face. Touches the window glass toward the peaks. Touches his heart.

emotional_beats.beat: Burka passes from warrior to woman.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: tender unease — a ceremony of farewell that is also a will.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad: clearing the ledger. Marya: claimed by something she cannot name.

emotional_beats.beat: Refusal of the horse.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: respect for a man who will not be paid.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad: contempt-without-anger for transaction; Petrov: shamed.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A leave-taking that is secretly a last will — gifts as the only honest language left.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Black wool burka. Gurda blade catching window light. Freckles flushing. Hand on glass. Hand on heart. Snow-blue mountains framed in the casement.
adapted_scenes.transition: His hand leaves his heart and reaches for the door — sound of horses shifting outside cuts to the porch.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. MAJOR PETROV'S HOUSE - PORCH - CONTINUOUS
adapted_scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MAJOR PETROV'S HOUSE - PORCH - CONTINUOUS
adapted_scenes.action: Bright cold light. Three horses stand bridled below the wooden porch, breath steaming. Hadji Murad steps out, Butler at his shoulder. Eldar moves first to the horses, hand resting on his dagger. Hooves clatter on hard ground. Arslan Khan rides up at a fast walk, reins one-handed. He sees Hadji Murad. His face does not change. His other hand is already at his belt. The pistol comes up. Butler freezes. Eldar's hand closes on his dagger but he is two paces too far. Hadji Murad — who limps — is suddenly down the porch steps in a single uncoiled rush, low and sideways, like a cat dropped from a wall. The pistol tracks him and loses him. Arslan Khan's horse shies. Russian soldiers run in from the yard, rifles half-raised, shouting. Hands seize Arslan Khan's bridle. The pistol stays raised, then…
adapted_scenes.action_description: On the porch Arslan Khan draws a pistol on Hadji Murad. HM, despite his lameness, drops sideways off the porch like a cat. Russian soldiers seize the bridle. The blood-feud has just walked into the open.

dialog_draft.line: Khan — for God's sake —
dialog_draft.subtext: He is not protesting the law; he is protesting that it has arrived here, now, on his porch.
dialog_draft.action_before: Half-step forward, palms open, parade reflex.

dialog_draft.line: Hadji Murad.
dialog_draft.subtext: Naming him is the trigger; the pistol only finishes the sentence.
dialog_draft.action_before: Reins the horse, pistol already rising.

dialog_draft.line: Agha —
dialog_draft.subtext: Vow, not warning: I am between you and the bullet.
dialog_draft.action_before: Hand on dagger, two paces too far away.

dialog_draft.line: Hold him! Hold the bridle!
dialog_draft.subtext: Imperial order trying to outrun a private feud — and barely making it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Running in, rifle butt up, grabs the cheekstrap.

dialog_draft.line: The debt.
dialog_draft.subtext: I have no self outside this killing. The ground is only borrowed.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lowers the pistol an inch, does not holster it.

dialog_draft.line: Allah knows.
dialog_draft.subtext: The door closes. The next meeting will not have Russian hands on the bridle.
dialog_draft.action_before: Mounted now, palms drawn slowly down his beard, eyes on Arslan Khan, not blinking.

emotional_beats.beat: Arslan Khan's pistol rises.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: the floor of the film drops — the safe house was never safe.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Arslan: cold settling. HM: clarity. Butler: helpless witness.

emotional_beats.beat: HM uncoils off the porch.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: awe — the lame man is suddenly the fastest thing on screen.
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: the body remembering what it is.

emotional_beats.beat: Russian hands on the bridle.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: the empire saving him for itself, not for him.
emotional_beats.character_feels: HM: noted; the feud will outlast the soldiers.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The feud arrives at the porch — and the man who has been a guest is, in one second, a hunted naib again.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Steaming horse breath. Black pistol against pale sky. Wood porch, scuffed boots. HM low and sideways like a thrown shadow. Bridle in a Russian fist. No blood — yet.
adapted_scenes.transition: Hadji Murad's unblinking gaze on Arslan Khan holds — cut on the click of a hammer being thumbed back down.
