adapted_scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MAJOR PETROV'S HOUSE - FORT NUKHA - DAY
adapted_scenes.action_description: Hadji Murad limps down the porch steps toward three saddled horses. ARSLAN KHAN reins his mount up at the gate, sees him, rips a pistol from his belt and fires. The shot cracks; the ball splits a porch post. Hadji Murad does not flinch. Lame leg dragging, he closes the distance like a cat, seizes Arslan Khan's bridle with one hand, draws his dagger with the other and presses the point under the rider's jaw. He says something low in Avar. Butler and Eldar burst from opposite sides and lock the two men's arms. MAJOR PETROV stomps out, sleeves rolled, half-shaved. Marya Dmitrievna freezes in the doorway, a folded blanket pressed to her chest. Arslan Khan dismounts, pale, trembling, eyes never leaving Hadji Murad's face. The Major shoves him toward the house. Hadji Murad lowers the dagger, breath steady, almost smiling, and walks back to the horses as if nothing has happened.

dialog_draft.line: Not on my porch, Arslan. Not on my porch.
Why, man? Why him?
He says it is their law. Blood for blood. A relation.
And on the road? If he catches you on the road?
Then it is so. Allah knows.
It is well.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A man already dead walking past his own murderer with steady hands.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Low winter sun on packed earth; pistol-smoke hangs; dagger-tip at throat held a beat too long; Marya frozen in doorway with folded blanket.

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MAJOR PETROV'S HOUSE - YARD - MOMENTS LATER
adapted_scenes.action_description: Hadji Murad fits his palm to the horse's withers and swings up — short leg barely brushing the stirrup — landing high in the saddle as if poured into it. He settles his dagger, adjusts the sword. Khanefi and Eldar mount in silence behind him. He does not look at the house. Then his eyes find Marya Dmitrievna where she stands on the step, blanket still in her arms. He nods once. She steps forward, stops, hands tightening on the cloth. Butler hangs back at the gatepost, one hand half-raised. Hadji Murad turns his horse a quarter-circle so he faces her squarely.

dialog_draft.line: Good-bye, my lass. I thank you.
God help you. God help you bring them home.
Don't forget your kunak.
Tell him: Hadji Murad does not forget.
Agha. Ready.
It is well.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A farewell that everyone present understands as final, and no one will name.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Three riders silhouetted against pale sky; Marya's blanket lowered slowly; Butler's raised hand held, then dropped; dust rising in the road.

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: INT. MAJOR'S HOUSE — VOZDVIZHENSK FORT — DAY
adapted_scenes.action_description: The room still holds the warmth of guests. MARYA DMITRIEVNA clears the table with deliberate force, stacking dishes louder than necessary. PETROVSKY watches from a chair. BUTLER stands near the window, looking out at the road where Hadji Murad has just disappeared. The MAJOR enters, reading the room.

dialog_draft.line: He'll be up to tricks. Terrible rogue, that one.
dialog_draft.subtext: Unsettled by HM's composure — diminishing what he can't control.
dialog_draft.action_before: Petrovsky leans back, arms folded.

dialog_draft.line: It's a pity there aren't more Russian rogues of such a kind. A week under our roof and nothing but good from him. Courteous. Wise. Just.
dialog_draft.subtext: Her loyalty is a rebuke — to the officers, to the empire, to the casual cruelty around her.
dialog_draft.action_before: She sets a dish down hard. Turns to face Petrovsky.

dialog_draft.line: She's quite smitten, and that's a fact.
dialog_draft.subtext: Deflecting her moral clarity with domestication.
dialog_draft.action_before: Major enters, glances at his wife, almost smiling.

dialog_draft.line: Well, and if I am? He's a Tartar and still a good man. What does that say about the rest of you?
dialog_draft.subtext: The accusation lands. She is done excusing the men around her.
dialog_draft.action_before: She meets the Major's eyes without flinching.

dialog_draft.line: You're quite right to take his part, Marya Dmitrievna.
dialog_draft.subtext: Butler means it — and hears his own complicity in the silence he's kept until now.
dialog_draft.action_before: Butler turns from the window. Quiet. Almost ashamed.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A domestic woman's lone moral testimony — the only honest verdict on Hadji Murad in this room.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Late morning light through small fort windows; cleared table, stacked dishes; Butler's back to room before turning; road visible and empty outside.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. MILITARY CAMP — TENT ROW — DAY
adapted_scenes.action: A long row of campaign tents dressed with white linen and regimental silver — absurd formality in the mud. BUTLER and POLTORATSKY sit side by side at the officers' table, champagne poured, the roast half-eaten. At the head, SIMON VORONTSOV leans close to MARYA VASILEVNA VORONTSOVA, murmuring something that makes her smile. Around them, uniformed men perform the ritual of a Petersburg dinner party. Butler watches Vorontsov with the faint attention of someone cataloguing a world he half-belongs to.

dialog_draft.line: Our man at the center will disgrace himself when the toasts begin. He has never been able to say the obvious thing without making it sound like a confession.
dialog_draft.subtext: Light mockery as social bonding — Poltoratsky performing wit to feel at home here.
dialog_draft.action_before: Leans toward Butler, keeping his voice below the table noise.

dialog_draft.line: My dear fellow, I have sat through worse in Petersburg. At least the champagne travels well.
dialog_draft.subtext: Butler is comfortable here — this world is his native register, not a place he is visiting.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lifts his glass, watching Vorontsov across the table.

dialog_draft.line: You know why the men pulled night watch all week? To keep the shells from troubling the Prince's table.
dialog_draft.subtext: The cost of aristocratic comfort — said lightly, but the fact lands.
dialog_draft.action_before: Cuts his meat without looking up.

dialog_draft.line: And did it work?
dialog_draft.subtext: He already knows the answer. He is testing whether Poltoratsky will say the rest.
dialog_draft.action_before: A pause; he sets down his fork.

emotional_beats.beat: Butler observes the imperial dinner ritual in a Caucasian camp
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The absurd distance between Petersburg manners and the war surrounding the tent
emotional_beats.character_feels: Butler is at ease but something snagged — Poltoratsky's remark about the night watch sits wrong

emotional_beats.beat: The cost of Marya Vasilevna's presence is named sideways
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Faint unease — beautiful people insulated by others' danger
emotional_beats.character_feels: Poltoratsky notes it with a joke, which is the only way he knows how

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Imperial comfort built on invisible labor — the empire at dinner
adapted_scenes.visual_note: White linen, regimental silver against camp mud; Marya Vasilevna as the only woman at the table; champagne catching afternoon light

inner_monologue_converted.original: Soldiers resented being posted on night watch to protect Marya Vasilevna from shells
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Poltoratsky's dry remark about the night watch — the resentment delivered as camp gossip rather than authorial commentary

adapted_scenes.transition: The clink of champagne glasses fades into the distant crack of a rifle from the hills

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: INT. POLTORATSKY'S TENT — FORT — NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action_description: Butler sits hunched at the card table, both elbows planted, chalk dust on his sleeves. The adjutant lays down his cards and tallies the column of figures with a clean, deliberate hand. Around the table, officers find reasons to look elsewhere — at the lamp, at the tent wall. Butler stares at the numbers. He begins to speak. No one meets his eye. Poltoratsky refills his glass without being asked.

dialog_draft.line: I'll send it from home. Within the fortnight.
As you wish. I'll note it.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Shame — the collapse of the officer-gentleman performance Butler has maintained all season
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Chalk tallies on green baize; lamplight; officers' turned shoulders; Butler's soiled cuff against the table

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: INT. VORONTSOV'S RECEPTION ROOM — TIFLIS — DAY
adapted_scenes.action_description: A gilded anteroom. HADJI MURAD sits on a straight-backed chair, papakha in his lap, watching an aide cross to an inner door. He has been here before — the furniture, the portraits, the carpets are familiar. The aide returns with a polite nod and a raised hand: not today. Hadji Murad stands, replaces his papakha, and walks out through tall doors into the Tiflis sun. He pauses on the steps, looking south toward the mountains he cannot reach. LORIS-MELIKOV catches up beside him, folding a document.

dialog_draft.line: The Prince asks you to be patient. Argutinski arrives next week. Then he can speak with more authority.
dialog_draft.subtext: The bureaucracy will outlast you — I am sorry.
dialog_draft.action_before: Catches Hadji Murad on the steps, lowers his voice

dialog_draft.line: Next week. He said next week before.
dialog_draft.subtext: I understand I am being managed, not helped.
dialog_draft.action_before: Does not turn; keeps his eyes on the mountains

dialog_draft.line: There is another matter. Nukha — a town to the east. The Prince grants permission to go there. A mosque. A quieter place to wait.
dialog_draft.subtext: We are moving you farther from the mountains and calling it a favor.
dialog_draft.action_before: Opens the folded document, holds it toward him

dialog_draft.line: And my family?
dialog_draft.subtext: All of it means nothing without this answer.
dialog_draft.action_before: Finally turns, looks at the document without taking it

dialog_draft.line: He will write to Petersburg. As I said.
dialog_draft.subtext: I have no better answer.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lets the silence hold a beat before speaking

dialog_draft.line: Then I will go to Nukha. Allah knows.
dialog_draft.subtext: I have no choice. I will wait. But the waiting is its own kind of captivity.
dialog_draft.action_before: Takes the document, folds it into his coat without reading it

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Enforced waiting as a form of imprisonment — the cage has no bars, only paperwork
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Pale Tiflis midday glare on stone steps; HM's papakha against the pale sky; document passing hand to hand; mountains barely visible in haze

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. HADJI MURAD'S HOUSE - NUKHA - DAY
adapted_scenes.scene_heading: INT. HADJI MURAD'S HOUSE - NUKHA - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: A bare reception room near the mosque. Hadji Murad enters in riding dress, dust on his boots, prayer still cooling on his lips. Eldar shadows the doorway. Councillor KIRILLOV waits with a leather satchel on his knees, the Russian officer in charge standing stiffly behind him. Hadji Murad does not sit. He sets a folded paper — a spy's report — on the low table between them, then draws his palms slowly down his beard. Kirillov rises, offers a sealed dispatch. Hadji Murad does not take it. He raises both hands, then four fingers, holds them in the air until Kirillov looks away.
adapted_scenes.action_description: A bare reception room near the mosque. Hadji Murad enters in riding dress, dust on his boots, prayer still cooling on his lips. Eldar shadows the doorway. Councillor KIRILLOV waits with a leather satchel on his knees, the Russian officer in charge standing stiffly behind him. Hadji Murad does not sit. He sets a folded paper — a spy's report — on the low table between them, then draws his palms slowly down his beard. Kirillov rises, offers a sealed dispatch. Hadji Murad does not take it. He raises both hands, then four fingers, holds them in the air until Kirillov looks away.

dialog_draft.line: His Excellency the Sardar requests your presence in Tiflis on the twelfth, to meet General Argutinski.
dialog_draft.subtext: Vorontsov is buying time he does not intend to repay.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lifts the satchel an inch, as if it weighed the answer.

dialog_draft.line: Yakshi. Have you brought money?
dialog_draft.subtext: I will measure your master's word in coin, not in courtesies.
dialog_draft.action_before: Holds up both hands, then four fingers, lets them hang in the air.

dialog_draft.line: I have.
dialog_draft.subtext: And nothing more.
dialog_draft.action_before: Touches the satchel.

dialog_draft.line: For two weeks now. Give here.
dialog_draft.subtext: Every day my son is closer to the knife.
dialog_draft.action_before: Steps forward; his hand opens, palm up, motionless.

dialog_draft.line: Three thousand rubles to my friends in Daghestan. Tell the Sardar — Hadji Murad has said it.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will buy back my family with or without his permission.
dialog_draft.action_before: Turns to Eldar, taps the folded spy's paper twice.

dialog_draft.line: Allah knows.
dialog_draft.subtext: The matter of Tiflis is closed; I will not come.
dialog_draft.action_before: Draws his palms slowly down his beard, gaze flat on Kirillov.

emotional_beats.beat: HM realizes the audience in Tiflis is a deferral, not a rescue.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: the cage closing
emotional_beats.character_feels: cold clarity

emotional_beats.beat: He commits, in one gesture, to a plan the Russians have not authorized.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: dread
emotional_beats.character_feels: resolve

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A trapped man recognizes that the patron is the cage; the rescue must now be his own act.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Bare whitewashed walls; low table; satchel of coin; folded spy's paper; dust on riding boots; afternoon light angled through narrow window; muted ochres, leather, steel.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad's longing to know what message the official had brought him
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He prays first, refuses the dispatch, lets the silence outweigh the courier.

inner_monologue_converted.original: He had thought rescuing his family would be easy; it had proved harder.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: The spy's paper on the table; the held-up fingers counting wasted weeks.

adapted_scenes.transition: His four raised fingers hold a beat too long, rhyming forward into a courier's hand counting coin on a saddle blanket.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. HADJI MURAD'S QUARTERS - NUKHA - DAY
adapted_scenes.scene_heading: INT. HADJI MURAD'S QUARTERS - NUKHA - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Councillor KIRILLOV, fat, unarmed, in civilian dress, fumbles a leather purse open on a low table. The OFFICER IN CHARGE hovers near the door. The INTERPRETER stands between them. Hadji Murad watches without rising. Kirillov counts seven piles of ten gold coins, pushes them across. Hadji Murad sits, sweeps the gold into the sleeve of his Circassian coat in one motion, stands. He steps to Kirillov and slaps the bald pate with the flat of his hand — a single dry sound. Kirillov staggers, scarlet. Hadji Murad walks out without looking back. The officer's hand drifts to his sword hilt and stops there.

dialog_draft.line: His Excellency the Viceroy desires your presence in Tiflis on the twelfth. To meet General Argutinski.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am the Viceroy's voice; you will obey.
dialog_draft.action_before: Straightens his coat, addresses the interpreter, not Hadji Murad.

dialog_draft.line: Yakshi. Have you brought the money?
dialog_draft.subtext: Your message is nothing. The gold is everything.
dialog_draft.action_before: Does not rise. Eyes fixed on the purse, not the man.

dialog_draft.line: What does he want with so much money?
dialog_draft.subtext: He cannot understand me — I may speak plainly.
dialog_draft.action_before: Turns half away, lowers voice to the officer in Russian.

dialog_draft.line: Two weeks. Give here.
dialog_draft.subtext: I heard your Russian. I am counting your insolence too.
dialog_draft.action_before: Holds up both hands, then four fingers — slow, deliberate.

dialog_draft.line: Tell him Hadji Murad does not speak with him. Let him give the money.
dialog_draft.subtext: You are a clerk. I will not be inventoried by a clerk.
dialog_draft.action_before: Glances once at Kirillov's unarmed belt, then away.

dialog_draft.line: He holds a rank equal to colonel — you cannot —
dialog_draft.subtext: Please do not make me draw on you.
dialog_draft.action_before: Half-step forward as Hadji Murad passes; voice cracks.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Contained fury hardening into contempt — the captive measures his cage and finds it shabby.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Seven gold piles on dark wood. Circassian coat sleeve swallowing the gold. Bald pate flushing red. Officer's hand frozen on hilt. Cold daylight, no fire.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. HADJI MURAD'S QUARTERS - NUKHA - DUSK
adapted_scenes.scene_heading: INT. HADJI MURAD'S QUARTERS - NUKHA - DUSK
adapted_scenes.action: The light in the room has gone grey-blue. Hadji Murad sits on the floor cushion, the gold from his sleeve poured out before him in a small bright heap he does not touch. Two SPIES enter behind the officer, hoods drawn to their eyes. The first — TAVLINIAN SPY, fleshy, swarthy. The second — OLD SPY, thin, white-bearded. The officer withdraws. Eldar takes a position at the door, back turned. The spies do not sit. The Tavlinian speaks first, low, in Avar. The old man only nods. Hadji Murad's hands rest on his knees and do not move while he listens. When the news lands, he draws his palms slowly down his beard, once. He does not speak for a long count. Outside, dogs bark and stop.

dialog_draft.line: Your friends will not ride for them. The imam has sworn — whoever moves the woman or the boy, his house is opened, his sons under the knife.
dialog_draft.subtext: No man in the mountains will burn for you now.
dialog_draft.action_before: Glances at the heap of gold; looks away as if it shames him.

dialog_draft.line: Yusuf is moved. Deeper. Vedeno, then beyond. The pit is dug already.
dialog_draft.subtext: Your son is on the edge already.
dialog_draft.action_before: Does not raise his eyes; speaks to the floor.

dialog_draft.line: And my mother? My wives?
dialog_draft.subtext: Name them so I know what is left of me.
dialog_draft.action_before: Voice lower than before. The hands on his knees do not move.

dialog_draft.line: Held. For now. Until the imam decides.
dialog_draft.subtext: Until he tires of waiting for you to break.
dialog_draft.action_before: Shifts weight; the hood slips, he pulls it back.

dialog_draft.line: Allah knows.
dialog_draft.subtext: The Russian road is closed. Only one road left, and it is mine alone.
dialog_draft.action_before: Draws his palms slowly down his beard. Once.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The last door closing — grief refusing to become a sound, hardening into resolve.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Untouched gold heap glinting in last daylight. Two hooded silhouettes against unlit wall. Hadji Murad's hands motionless on knees. Beard-stroke as final gesture. Dogs barking, then silence.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. HADJI MURAD'S QUARTERS - NUKHA - DUSK
adapted_scenes.scene_heading: INT. HADJI MURAD'S QUARTERS - NUKHA - DUSK
adapted_scenes.action: Two messengers stand at the doorway, road dust on their cherkesskas. The fleshy one shifts weight; the thin old man holds his papakha in both hands. Hadji Murad sits cross-legged on a worn carpet, elbows on knees, turbaned head bowed. A low brass tray holds untouched tea. He does not look up as they speak. He listens. The tea cools. Outside a horse stamps once. Hadji Murad raises his head. He reaches into a pouch at his belt and lays two gold pieces on the carpet, sliding one toward each man with two fingers. He draws his palms slowly down his beard.

Hadji Murad sits cross-legged on a worn carpet, elbows on knees, turbaned head bowed. A low brass tray holds untouched tea. He does not look up as they speak. He listens. The tea cools. Outside a horse stamps once. Hadji Murad raises his head. He reaches into a pouch at his belt and lays two gold pieces on the carpet, sliding one toward each man with two fingers. He draws his palms slowly down his beard.

dialog_draft.line: Your friends will not move. Shamil has promised the knife to any hand that lifts for your house.
The imam has set watchers on the road to Vedeno. Even your cousins keep their doors shut.
Go.
What answer shall we carry?
The answer will be as Allah pleases. Go.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A man hears the last door close on the path he hoped would open.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Low oil-lamp glow, two gold coins catching light on dark carpet, untouched tea, dust on messengers' boots, turban shadow over closed eyes.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. HADJI MURAD'S QUARTERS - NUKHA - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.scene_heading: INT. HADJI MURAD'S QUARTERS - NUKHA - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: The lamp has burned low. Hadji Murad sits exactly as before, elbows on crossed knees. Hours have passed; only the wick has changed. A moth circles the flame. Eldar stands silent by the wall, kinjal across his lap, watching his master. From an inner room: the soft breathing of a child not present here — only imagined. Hadji Murad's hand finds a small leather thong at his throat — a token, his son's. He closes his fist around it. He rises in one motion. He walks to the wall and lifts down a saddle blanket. He folds it once, sets it beside his sword. Eldar stands without being told. Hadji Murad meets his eyes. He says nothing. He draws his palms down his beard, slowly, finally. The lamp gutters.
adapted_scenes.action_description: The lamp has burned low. Hadji Murad sits exactly as before, elbows on crossed knees. Hours have passed; only the wick has changed. A moth circles the flame. Eldar stands silent by the wall, kinjal across his lap, watching his master. From an inner room: the soft breathing of a child not present here — only imagined. Hadji Murad's hand finds a small leather thong at his throat — a token, his son's. He closes his fist around it. He rises in one motion. He walks to the wall and lifts down a saddle blanket. He folds it once, sets it beside his sword. Eldar stands without being told. Hadji Murad meets his eyes. He says nothing. He draws his palms down his beard, slowly, finally. The lamp gutters.

dialog_draft.line: Sardar?
Saddle the bay. And the second horse.
Tonight?
Before the next prayer.
And the Russians?
Allah knows. It is so.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The falcon chooses the mountains, knowing the bells will betray him.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Guttering oil lamp, moth at flame, leather thong in fist, sword and folded blanket on floor, kinjal across Eldar's lap, deep blue pre-dawn at the window.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. HENCHMEN'S ROOM - HADJI MURAD'S QUARTERS - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: Moonlight spills through the window onto four sleeping bodies on burkas. The outer door creaks. Hadji Murad enters carrying a black wadded beshmet, drops it on the table — a dull metallic thud betrays gold sewn into the lining. Gamzalo rises, recognizes him, settles back. Eldar springs up, pulling on his own beshmet. Khan Mahoma and Bata sleep on. Hadji Murad hands Eldar a fistful of gold pieces. Eldar carries them into the moonlight at the window, draws a small knife from under his dagger, begins unstitching the lining with practiced fingers. Gamzalo sits cross-legged, eye fixed on his master. Outside, nightingales trill in the garden. Hadji Murad does not lower his voice. He speaks as if naming weather.
adapted_scenes.action_description: Moonlight spills through the window onto four sleeping bodies on burkas. The outer door creaks. Hadji Murad enters carrying a black wadded beshmet, drops it on the table — a dull metallic thud betrays gold sewn into the lining. Eldar springs up, pulling on his beshmet. Khan Mahoma and Bata sleep on. Hadji Murad hands Eldar gold pieces. Eldar takes them to the window, draws a knife, begins unstitching the lining. Gamzalo sits up cross-legged, eye fixed on his master. Nightingales trill outside.

dialog_draft.line: Sew these in too.
dialog_draft.subtext: The decision is made. We ride for the mountains.
dialog_draft.action_before: Drops the gold into Eldar's open palm without ceremony.

dialog_draft.line: Gamzalo. The rifles. The pistols. The powder. Tomorrow we go far.
dialog_draft.subtext: We break out. Be ready to die.
dialog_draft.action_before: Turns to Gamzalo, draws his palm slowly down his beard.

dialog_draft.line: We have bullets and powder. It is enough.
dialog_draft.subtext: I have waited for this order. I am ready now.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lays a hand flat on the rifle beside his blanket.

dialog_draft.line: Hadji Agha — Allah wills it.
dialog_draft.subtext: Reaffirms the oath; binds himself to whatever follows.
dialog_draft.action_before: Mutters something low and incomprehensible, half prayer, half growl.

dialog_draft.line: Ready, Agha.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will absorb the bullet meant for you.
dialog_draft.action_before: Pauses the knife mid-stitch, meets HM's eyes once, then bends back to the lining.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Quiet, irrevocable commitment — the moment loyalty becomes operational
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Moonlight on dagger and gold; black beshmet on table; sleeping bodies as still landscape; nightingale song against weapon-metal silence.

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: INT. SAFE-HOUSE — MURIDS' ROOM / HALL — PRE-DAWN
adapted_scenes.action_description: A single oil lamp. Gamzalo, Khanefi, Bata and Eldar sit among rifles, pistols, daggers on a felt cloth. Khanefi rasps a blade along a whetstone in slow strokes. Bata pinches fresh powder onto a flintlock pan. Eldar wraps bullets in oiled rags. The screech of iron marks every breath. Outside, nightingales. Hadji Murad steps into the hall barefoot, a clay jug in hand. He stops at the murids' threshold. Khanefi, not lifting his eyes from the blade, begins to sing — low, clear. The men do not pause their work. Hadji Murad's hand tilts; water spills from the jug onto his foot. He does not move. Bata's voice cracks over the song's last line with a fierce shout. Silence returns — only the whetstone, the birds. Hadji Murad rights the jug, draws his palms slowly down his beard, walks back.

dialog_draft.line: (singing, low) Fly on, ye winged ones — fly to our homes. Tell our mothers, tell our sisters, tell the white maidens that fighting we died.
(singing) Our bodies will never lie in a tomb. Wolves will tear them. Ravens will pluck out our eyes.
Lya-illaha-illa-Allah!
It is enough. Sleep one hour. We ride at first light.
Ready, Agha.
It is well. Allah knows.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A man hearing his own elegy and accepting it without speaking it
adapted_scenes.visual_note: One oil lamp, cold blue pre-dawn at the window; oiled steel, paper cartridges, felt cloth; spilled water on bare foot; nightingales outside; no music score — only whetstone and song

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: INT. HADJI MURAD'S QUARTERS, NUKHA HOUSE - PRE-DAWN
adapted_scenes.action_description: A clay jug tilts in Hadji Murad's hand. Water spills across the floorboards before he notices. He rights the jug, sets it down, presses a wet palm to his shaven scalp. Outside, KHANEFI's high tenor carries through the wall — an Avar lullaby. Hadji Murad stops moving. The song reaches him like a hand laid on his chest. He crosses to his weapons laid out on a folded cloak: shashka, pistol, kinjal. He runs his thumb along the dagger's edge, sets it down. Sits on the edge of the bed. From a saddlebag he draws a small folded square of cloth — a child's embroidered cap, the stitching uneven. He turns it once in his fingers. His hands still. The song outside lifts, then breaks. Hadji Murad's jaw tightens; his palms slide slowly down his beard. He folds the cap, returns it to the saddlebag, and rises — too quickly, favouring the bad leg. He limps to the door, throws it open. Cold pre-dawn air. The sentry post, dim. He calls into the courtyard, voice low and dry.

dialog_draft.line: Eldar.
Agha.
The horses. Saddled. Before the officer wakes.
To ride where?
Out. Only out.
Yusuf is waiting. Allah knows how long.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A father's grief crystallising into the decision to act — the still moment before flight.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Hold on the spilled water pooling between floorboards as Khanefi's song bleeds in. The embroidered cap is the only object that matters in the frame — Yusuf, present in absence.

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: INT./EXT. FORT VOZDVIZHENSKY — OFFICERS' MESS / STABLE YARD — NIGHT TO DAWN
adapted_scenes.action_description: INT: Officers crammed around a long table. Regimental singers perform. Major Petrov, ash-pale and past drunk, straddles a chair and hacks the air with a drawn sword — laughing, then swearing. Butler watches from the wall, a cup in hand, face carefully empty. Around him: noise, vodka, the theatre of men who have nothing left to fear or hope for. EXT INTERCUT: Across the yard, in darkness, Hadji Murad stands at his doorway. He stares toward the ridge. Calls Eldar's name — quiet, controlled. Eldar appears. HM speaks two words. Eldar disappears toward the horses.

dialog_draft.line: Brothers — to the campaign! To Baryatinsky! To whoever dies first!
dialog_draft.subtext: A man performing courage he no longer feels, drinking to bury what the frontier has cost him.
dialog_draft.action_before: Swings sword at the air. Officers cheer or look away.

dialog_draft.line: Here we go.
dialog_draft.subtext: He recognizes the spectacle for what it is — decay — and does not leave.
dialog_draft.action_before: Refills his cup without looking at it.

dialog_draft.line: Saddle the horses.
dialog_draft.subtext: Motion is the only answer to the image of Yusuf's eyes in the dark.
dialog_draft.action_before: Steps outside. Does not look back into the room.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Two kinds of ruin: the Russian officer dissolving into drink and performance; the Avar warrior holding himself together by force of will — both trapped, both moving toward the edge.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Mess hall: yellow lamp-smoke, scattered vodka cups, sword flash. Yard cut: pre-dawn grey, HM a still shape against the dark stable.

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: EXT. FORT COURTYARD - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action_description: Butler walks alone along the fort's earthen wall. Marya Vasilevna Vorontsova crosses the yard toward him, shawl pulled tight. They fall into step without greeting — a practiced ease. She stops at the well, rests a hand on the stone rim. Kamenev emerges from a doorway, reads them both, smirks. Butler straightens. Marya Vasilevna does not move from the well.

dialog_draft.line: Still walking, Butler? The major'll hear about it.
The major's asleep.
Is he.
Good night, Kamenev.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Unspoken desire, watched from the outside
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Torchlight on well-stones, cold courtyard, three figures at angles — proximity without touch, Kamenev backlit in doorframe

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. RIDGE ABOVE NUKHA - DAWN
adapted_scenes.scene_heading: EXT. RIDGE ABOVE NUKHA - DAWN
adapted_scenes.action_description: First grey light. Five horses stand saddled in a hollow of frost-burned scrub. HADJI MURAD tightens the girth on a lean bay, eyes the ridge above. ELDAR holds the bridle of a second horse, watchful. The other murids check primings. Far below, the Russian fort is still dark; cocks crow somewhere. Hadji Murad lays a hand on Eldar's shoulder, then draws his palms slowly down his beard. He looks east, toward the Avar mountains. Drawn dagger in hand, he steadies his breath. Hooves sound from the ridge above — Cossacks coming. Eldar turns, raises his rifle. Hadji Murad shakes his head once: stay. He swings into the saddle. The bay snorts steam.
adapted_scenes.action: First grey light. Five horses stand saddled in a hollow of frost-burned scrub. HADJI MURAD tightens the girth on a lean bay, eyes the ridge. ELDAR holds the bridle of a second horse. The other murids check primings. Far below, the Russian fort is still dark. Hadji Murad lays a hand on Eldar's shoulder, then draws his palms slowly down his beard. He looks east. Drawn dagger in hand, he steadies his breath. Hooves sound from the ridge — Cossacks coming. Eldar raises his rifle. Hadji Murad shakes his head once: stay. He swings into the saddle.

dialog_draft.line: The horse is good. The road is open. A man rides while he can.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am leaving now; whoever stands in my way will fall.
dialog_draft.action_before: tightens the girth, eyes the ridge

dialog_draft.line: Yusuf is in Vedeno. The sardar gives me carpets. Allah knows what a father is worth in carpets.
dialog_draft.subtext: The Russians have failed me; my son's life is the only currency now.
dialog_draft.action_before: draws palms slowly down his beard

dialog_draft.line: Tell them Hadji Murad rode out at dawn. Not as a guest. Not as a prisoner.
dialog_draft.subtext: If I die, let the manner of it be mine; I refuse the cage.
dialog_draft.action_before: lays his hand on Eldar's shoulder

dialog_draft.line: My mother told me: do not be Khan, do not be killed. Forgive me, mother.
dialog_draft.subtext: I have chosen the second; the family must live even if I do not.
dialog_draft.action_before: falls silent, looks east

dialog_draft.line: It is well. Allah is great.
dialog_draft.subtext: The matter is closed; I am ready.
dialog_draft.action_before: draws the dagger, steadies his breath

emotional_beats.beat: He chooses the death he can author over the cage he cannot.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: awe and dread
emotional_beats.character_feels: clarity, grief, readiness

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A man chooses his own death rather than another man's leash.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: First grey light, frost on scrub, breath of horses; lone bay against pale sky; drawn dagger glints; eastward horizon line.
adapted_scenes.transition: the bay's hooves on frozen ground carry into Cossack hooves arriving at the major's house

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. MAJOR PETROV'S HOUSE - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MAJOR PETROV'S HOUSE - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action_description: Moonlight on the snow-packed yard. Sounds of tulumbas and drunk singing from inside. KAMENEV swings down from his horse. CHIKHIREV, a Don Cossack, dismounts and unbuckles a saddle-bag. BUTLER and MARYA DMITRIEVNA stand on the steps, returning from a walk. Kamenev gestures Chikhirev forward. The Cossack lifts out a heavy linen sack. Kamenev takes it, reaches inside. Marya Dmitrievna sets her hand on her hip; her freckles go hot. Kamenev hauls out a man's head by the shaven skull and holds it up in the moonlight — black short-cut beard, one eye half-open, blue lips, a kindly childlike expression on the wounds. Butler's jaw locks. Marya Dmitrievna goes still. Then she turns her back on the sack and steps toward the door. Butler cannot move. Inside, the singing rolls on. Kamenev grins, waiting for the response that does not come.
adapted_scenes.action: Moonlight on the snow-packed yard. Tulumbas and drunk singing from inside. KAMENEV dismounts. CHIKHIREV unbuckles a saddle-bag, lifts out a heavy linen sack. BUTLER and MARYA DMITRIEVNA stand on the steps. Kamenev reaches inside the sack. Marya sets her hand on her hip; her freckles go hot. Kamenev hauls out a man's head by the shaven skull and holds it up in the moonlight — black beard, one eye half-open, blue lips, a kindly childlike expression on the wounds. Butler's jaw locks. Marya goes still. She turns her back on the sack. Butler cannot move. Inside, the singing rolls on.

dialog_draft.line: Shall I show you a novelty? You won't be frightened, Marya Dmitrievna?
dialog_draft.subtext: Watch me make a woman flinch; it will warm me against what I have done.
dialog_draft.action_before: loosens the sack with both hands, half-smiling

dialog_draft.line: Why should I be frightened, Peter Nikolaevich? I have washed worse than what is in your sack.
dialog_draft.subtext: Refusing to give him the flinch he wants from a woman.
dialog_draft.action_before: hand on hip, freckles already going hot

dialog_draft.line: What does this mean? Who has killed him?
dialog_draft.subtext: If I name what I see, I will have to feel it.
dialog_draft.action_before: cannot tear his eyes from the head

dialog_draft.line: Cover it, Kamenev. For God's sake — cover it. The moonlight is indecent on a face like that.
dialog_draft.subtext: Cover the part of the campaign I have been pretending not to see.
dialog_draft.action_before: takes a half step back, jaw tightening

dialog_draft.line: Cut-throats. Drunk cut-throats, the lot of you. Take that thing off my step.
dialog_draft.subtext: The line has been crossed; she will not pretend otherwise.
dialog_draft.action_before: sees the face in the moonlight, goes still

dialog_draft.line: God see them. God see all of you. I am going inside.
dialog_draft.subtext: Out of arguments; refusing to keep arguing; grief held flat so it does not break her.
dialog_draft.action_before: turns her back on the Cossack and the sack

emotional_beats.beat: Marya breaks her domestic role and names the campaign for what it is.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: shame on behalf of empire
emotional_beats.character_feels: moral nausea, grief

emotional_beats.beat: Butler registers, without language, that this man was more whole than any Russian at the table.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: quiet horror
emotional_beats.character_feels: shame, awe, the beginning of doubt

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Witness — the moment empire's price is held up in the moonlight and cannot be unseen.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Cold moonlight on packed snow; warm orange spill from window with drunken song; severed head as still icon; Marya's red freckles vs blue lips.
adapted_scenes.transition: Marya's door closing rhymes against the drunken song still rolling inside; Butler alone with the air

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. MAJOR PETROV'S QUARTERS - FORT - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: Lamplight on a stained tablecloth. The MAJOR and TWO OFFICERS, flushed with drink, lurch up as KAMENEV enters with a COSSACK carrying a heavy canvas sack. The Cossack lowers the sack onto the table; Kamenev unties it. He lifts out a severed head by the hair — Hadji Murad's. Butler stands rigid in the doorway, color draining from his face. The major sways forward, eyes wet and stupid, and presses his mouth to the dead forehead. The officers grin. The Cossack, expressionless, slides the head back into the sack and lays it down with strange care, as if not to bruise it against the floor.

dialog_draft.line: What does this mean. Who has killed him?
dialog_draft.subtext: He cannot yet make the face on the table belong to the man who taught him the word saubul.
dialog_draft.action_before: Stops in the doorway, one hand still on the frame.

dialog_draft.line: Wanted to give us the slip. He died like a hero — I take it round to all the forts.
dialog_draft.subtext: He has rehearsed this speech a dozen times today and is proud of it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sets the head down, wipes his palm on his coat.

dialog_draft.line: No — no, let me kiss him. He gave me a sword. Only last week.
dialog_draft.subtext: Drunken sentiment is the only register left to a man who has nothing honest to say.
dialog_draft.action_before: Pushes past Butler, swaying, arms wide.

dialog_draft.line: All the same — a valiant head. A fine fellow.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is congratulating himself on having known a great man, briefly, by accident.
dialog_draft.action_before: Raises his glass over the table.

dialog_draft.line: Saubul, he taught me. That was the word. Saubul. And I — well. Here we are.
dialog_draft.subtext: Tasting the dead man's word like a debt he cannot pay; the soldier's idyll has finally come due.
dialog_draft.action_before: Looks past them at the dark window, jaw tightening.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: obscene farewell — empire kisses what it killed
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Single oil lamp, candle-yellow on a soiled cloth; canvas sack dark against white linen; Butler in shadow at the doorframe; drunken faces close to the head.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. PORCH OF MAJOR'S HOUSE - FORT - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: MARYA DMITRIEVNA sits on the second step, shawl pulled tight, the sounds of laughter and a voice reciting Kamenev's speech leaking out the door behind her. BUTLER comes out into the porch, hand half-raised. She glances at him once and turns her face away. Inside, the major shouts: Let me kiss him! Marya Dmitrievna's freckled face goes red, then very still. She rises. Butler steps down toward her; she does not look at him. She gathers her skirt, descends the last step, and walks toward the back door. Butler is left standing on the porch with the noise of the room behind him and the cold yard in front.

dialog_draft.line: Marya Dmitrievna — please. It might happen to anyone out here. That's war, isn't it.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is begging her not to make him see what he just saw — and pretending the phrase still means something to him.
dialog_draft.action_before: Steps down into the porch, hand half-raised.

dialog_draft.line: You're all cut-throats. Cut-throats, and drunk on top of it.
dialog_draft.subtext: I have stopped pretending this is war; I am calling it what it is, even at the cost of my place here.
dialog_draft.action_before: Stands up from the step, will not look at Butler.

dialog_draft.line: A dead man's head belongs in the earth, batyushka, not bumping about in a sack from fort to fort.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am still trying to cover for you by calling you batyushka, but do not mistake the softness for permission.
dialog_draft.action_before: Freckles flushing red, hand flat against her hip.

dialog_draft.line: He looked at me like I was a person. Your major in there has not done that since Easter.
dialog_draft.subtext: I have begun to doubt our side of this, and I do not yet know where to put the doubt.
dialog_draft.action_before: Voice goes flat, almost toneless.

dialog_draft.line: They're drunk in there, Marya Dmitrievna. They don't — they don't mean the grinning. Truly.
dialog_draft.subtext: He knows perfectly well they do mean it, and that he belongs to them, not to her.
dialog_draft.action_before: Glances back at the door, lowers his voice.

dialog_draft.line: Well, then — God see them, the lot of you. I have soup on the stove and no stomach for any of it.
dialog_draft.subtext: I have run out of arguments and I refuse to keep arguing; I will go back to the work that keeps me a person.
dialog_draft.action_before: Turns toward the back door, skirt gathered tight.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: the woman of the fort steps for one moment out of her role and names the killing
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Cold porch, breath visible; warm yellow light leaking from the door behind Butler; Marya's freckled face going red then flat; her shawl, the wooden step, the dark yard.

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: EXT. ROAD OUTSIDE NUKHA FORT - MORNING
adapted_scenes.action_description: Mist lifts off the river. Spring grass, sprouting corn, glitter of water. HADJI MURAD swings up onto his white Kabarda horse in the fort yard. Behind him, all FIVE HENCHMEN — GAMZALO, ELDAR, and three others — mount in silence, rifles slung. The COMMANDER steps forward, frowning. NAZAROV, young, brown-haired, the Cross of St. George on his chest, tightens his roan's girth. Four Cossacks behind him: FERAPONTOV, IGNATOV, MISHKIN, PETRAKOV. The Commander speaks. Hadji Murad does not turn his head. He touches his heel to the horse and rides through the gate. The Commander's mouth closes around the unfinished order. On the road beyond the fort the party walks past peasant women with baskets, a creaking buffalo wagon, soldiers driving carts. After a mile and a half Hadji Murad lifts his reins. The Kabarda breaks into a smooth amble. The Cossacks have to push their horses to a quick trot to keep up. Gamzalo's eyes find Eldar's. A small nod. Nazarov rises in his stirrups, glances back at the receding fort, then forward at the Avar's straight back.

dialog_draft.line: You are not to take them all. Those were the orders.
Nazarov. Keep close to him. Close, you understand.
All right, your honor.
Ah. He's got a fine horse under him.
Hadji Agha.
It is well.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A door closing without a sound — the moment a captive man stops being a guest.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Wet spring light, mist burning off the river. White Kabarda horse against dark Cossack mounts. Fort gate framing the departure; road opening into a wide valley.

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: EXT. ROAD TO THE MOUNTAINS - NUKHA OUTSKIRTS - DAY
adapted_scenes.action_description: A bright Caucasian morning. Hadji Murad rides the white Kabarda at the head of a small Cossack escort — Nazarov, Ignatov, Ferapontov, Petrakov — and four mounted mountaineer henchmen. The road curves toward the blue ridgeline. Hadji Murad's gloved heel touches the Kabarda; the horse breaks from amble to quick trot. The Cossacks scramble to keep pace. Ferapontov slaps his saddle, eyes the white horse with a grin. Nazarov leans forward, urging his big roan gelding alongside. Hadji Murad does not turn his head. His right hand drifts beneath the burka, finds the pistol grip. The henchmen exchange one glance — settled long ago. The Kabarda climbs to a canter, then a gallop. Nazarov's whip cracks; the roan surges. Hooves drum the packed road. Ignatov twists in the saddle, hand going to his carbine. Nazarov reaches for Hadji Murad's bridle — fingers an inch from the leather — when the pistol comes up under the burka. Muzzle flash. Nazarov clutches his chest, reels forward onto the saddle-bow. The henchmen draw curved blades and pistols in one motion. Petrakov twists toward his rifle too late.

dialog_draft.line: Three hundred rubles for that horse in Tiflis. Pity he is on our side.
Hey, kunak — steady. You mustn't do that.
Mind, they are up to something, the devils.
The road bends toward the mountains. Allah knows the rest.
Humbug. You won't get away.
At them, lads —

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A man choosing his death over another man's roof; the escort's casual morning becomes a slaughter in three heartbeats.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: White Kabarda against blue ridges; burka concealing pistol; sun-bleached dust; roan gelding closing; flash of curved Avar blades; thistle-color sky.

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: EXT. RAVINE EDGE - SAME ROAD - MOMENTS LATER
adapted_scenes.action_description: Smoke from spent powder drifts in the still air. Cossack horses scatter, one fallen across Ignatov's crushed leg. Two henchmen wheel back, blades wet, scanning the road for riders from Nukha. Hadji Murad reins the Kabarda hard at the lip of the ravine, listens — distant bugle, faint. The mountains stand close enough to count the snow-veins. He swings down, palms a fistful of road dust, lets it fall. Right hand moves slowly down his beard. Eldar holds the bridles, eyes wide, waiting for the order. Hadji Murad does not give one. He looks east, where his son is held. Then south, where the Russian columns will come. He thumbs back the hammer of the second pistol. The wind lifts the edge of his burka. He does not run. He waits in the open road, where they can see him, where the mountain will see him.

dialog_draft.line: Sardar — the column will be on the road within the hour.
Yusuf. A black horse waits for you. Your father is coming.
Sardar, you kept your carpets. I keep my word.
Mother — forgive. I could not be Khan. I will not be a guest.
It is well. Hadji Murad rides home.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The fugitive becomes a martyr in slow motion; grief, prayer, and resolve compressed into one stilled body waiting for the column.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Powder smoke; dust drifting from open palm; second pistol cocked; burka snapping in mountain wind; lone figure framed against bare ridge; no music — only wind and distant bugle.

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: EXT. HIGH ROAD ABOVE NUKHA - DAY
adapted_scenes.action_description: Four Cossack bodies sprawl in the dust beside lathered horses. GAMZALO wipes his blade once on a dead Cossack's sleeve, his good eye already on the road behind. BATA stoops, slaps the cartridge-pouches free of the slain, slings them across his saddle. KHANEFI grabs the bridle of NAZAROV's loose bay; HADJI MURAD reins in his grey, sees it, snaps a flat hand. Khanefi releases the horse without a word. Far below, a single rider — MISHKIN — is already a black dot pounding toward the fortress walls of Nukha. A heartbeat later, from the watchtower, a single muzzle-flash. The crack of the alarm shot rolls up the valley. The grey horse under Hadji Murad has turned from white to grey with sweat. He draws his palms slowly down his beard.

dialog_draft.line: Hadji Agha. Four down. One rode for the tower.
Bismillah. Mishkin is gone, agha. The horse was faster than my arm.
Khanefi. Leave the horse. A man who runs with a dead Cossack's mount runs slow.
Gamzalo. How many cartridges?
Pouches full. Powder dry. Enough for a short quarrel — not a long one.
Allah is great. It is enough, Hadji Agha.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The point of no return — the killing is done, the alarm is up, and four men measure how many breaths remain between them and Yusuf.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Wet road, Cossack blood blackening the dust, lathered grey horse, distant white watchtower trailing smoke; low afternoon light, mountains to the right, rice-fields glinting to the left.

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: EXT. FORK ABOVE THE RICE-FIELDS — BENERDZHIK BELOW - DAY
adapted_scenes.action_description: The four reach a fork. To the right: the road climbing toward the saklyas and minarets of Benerdzhik and, beyond, the mountains. To the left: low rice-fields, then the dull gleam of the Alazan. Khanefi halts, looking left, his jaw tight. Bata reins the spent mare alongside Hadji Murad, eyes back on the road they have just cut. Gamzalo tilts his good eye toward the low fields. Hadji Murad sits motionless on his grey, breath even, the mountains pulling at him from the right like a rope. Behind them, far down the valley, the watchtower fires a second alarm shot. Hadji Murad lifts a flat hand and points — left, toward the wet fields. He turns the grey off the high road. The others follow without a sound. The mountains stand untouched on their right shoulder as they ride away from them.

dialog_draft.line: Two smokes, maybe less, and that tower will spit horsemen at us like seeds.
Agha. Right is the mountains. Left is —
Rice-fields, then the river. Not the right road. They will look right.
Bata. The river, not the mountains. They will look for us where our hearts go.
It is well. We ride until the horses fall, then we ride on foot. Yusuf is waiting.
Allah knows the rest.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A man turning his back on the mountains he loves in order to outwit the men hunting him — discipline mastering longing at the cost of every instinct.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Forked track, minarets of Benerdzhik on the right ridge, flooded rice-fields stretching left to the Alazan; horses' hooves breaking the water's mirror; mountains receding behind the riders.

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: EXT. FLOODED RICE-FIELD NEAR BENERDZHIK - DUSK
adapted_scenes.action_description: Hadji Murad reins his horse at a fork. To the right, the saklyas and minarets of Benerdzhik. To the left, drowned fields and the Alazan beyond. He turns left. Eldar follows without question. The horses sink to their pasterns. Each hoof tears free with a wet pop, like corks drawn from bottles. The men ride zig-zag, hunting drier ground. They find none. Light bleeds from the sky. Hadji Murad spots a patch of higher ground choked with shrubs. He rides for it. They dismount among the bushes. Hadji Murad spreads bread and cheese on a folded burka. The horses tear at thin grass, flanks heaving.

dialog_draft.line: Eldar. The road to the right is theirs. We go left. The Alazan will hide our tracks.
The field is water. The horses sink. We rest in the shrubs until the moon goes behind the hill.
Eat. Let them graze. A tired horse is a dead rider.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A hunted man calculating distance against the life of his son.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Wet ochre mud, last grey light, two horsemen as silhouettes in flooded field; shrubs as black islands. Burka spread like a small dark altar.

adapted_scenes.scene_heading: EXT. SHRUB COVER ABOVE THE RICE-FIELD - NIGHT
adapted_scenes.action_description: Moonlight thins, then fails behind the hill. Darkness. Two nightingales in the shrubs answer one another. Hadji Murad sits motionless, listening. His hand rests on the folded burka. He draws his palms slowly down his beard. Eldar watches him, says nothing. Hadji Murad rises, refolds the burka for ablution, faces the dark hill, washes his hands and face from a small flask Eldar pours. He kneels. He prays. He has barely touched his forehead to the burka when a sound reaches them through the dark — many horses, splashing, closing. Eldar's hand goes to his pistol. Hadji Murad lifts one finger: wait. He finishes the prayer. Only then does he stand and check his powder.

dialog_draft.line: The nightingales sing again. Last night I heard the song of Hamzad. Bring me water — I will pray.
Allah knows the road. Hadji Murad has given his word. It is well.
Sardar — horses. Many.
I hear them. Finish the prayer first.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A warrior preparing his soul for death while the enemy closes in.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Pitch dark with a faint silver wash; nightingale song layered over distant water-splash; burka as prayer mat; pistol hammer catching no light.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. CLUMP OF SHRUBS / OLD DITCH - RICE-FIELD BOG - PRE-DAWN TO FIRST LIGHT
adapted_scenes.action: Six men crouch in a thicket above a rice-field bog. Hooves splash through wet ground on both flanks. BATA peers through branches at moving black shadows; KHANEFI sees the same shapes on the opposite side. HADJI MURAD spreads his burka, performs ablutions in silence, then rises. Without a word he points to an old ditch among the shrubs. The men begin cutting branches with their daggers, throwing earth onto a low bank. Hadji Murad works beside them, his hands in the mud. The hobbled horses stand tied in the clearing. Light grows. KARGANOV (off, in militia voice) shouts the surrender call from beyond the embankment. HADJI AGA stands at his shoulder, dagger hilt under his palm, refusing to face the ditch. A rifle reports from the bank. A bullet hits the militiaman's horse — it staggers…

dialog_draft.line: Brother — bad ditch. Shallow. The wolf does not sleep where the hunter sees his ear.
dialog_draft.subtext: I'm terrified; the position is a death-trap and I want Hadji Murad to hear it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Wipes mud from rifle, eyes the embankment.

dialog_draft.line: Karganov. Militia. Both sides of the clump.
dialog_draft.subtext: I have counted them. We are surrounded; no point softening the fact.
dialog_draft.action_before: Returns from the edge, eye on the ditch.

dialog_draft.line: Khanefi, Gamzalo, Hadji Aga — dig deeper. A handful of earth is worth a brother today.
dialog_draft.subtext: Affection delivered as command; he is already counting which of them will not see the evening.
dialog_draft.action_before: Cuts a branch with his dagger, lays it on the bank.

dialog_draft.line: Hadji Murad. Listen — come out. The Russians offer the road back. Refuse, and I cannot hold them.
dialog_draft.subtext: Come out so I do not have to remember what we were. Make this easy for the man I am pretending to be.
dialog_draft.action_before: Touches dagger hilt, will not face the ditch.

dialog_draft.line: Karganov. You are many. We are five. Come closer and count again.
dialog_draft.subtext: Acknowledges the trap by name; refuses to plead; measures the officer's nerve before the first shot.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sets rifle on the embankment, does not rise.

dialog_draft.line: Allah knows. It is well.
dialog_draft.subtext: The door closes. He has chosen the manner of his death and is at peace with it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Draws both palms slowly down his beard, then sights the rifle.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad performs ablutions as the encirclement closes — the warrior settles his soul before his body fights.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Reverence and dread; we know this is preparation for death.
emotional_beats.character_feels: A clear, almost weightless calm — Hamzad's pattern recognized, accepted.

emotional_beats.beat: The decision to entrench transforms a hunted band into a small fortress; affection is delivered as command.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Tightening of the chest; admiration braided with grief.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Quiet love for his men, already counting losses.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Aga's surrender call comes from the wrong mouth — the kunak who became the executioner — and Hadji Murad answers with a rifle, not a word to him.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Cold recognition of betrayal sealed; no melodrama, only a horse falling.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Closure. The matter is decided in his head and no further discussion is welcome.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: The acceptance of a death already chosen — the warrior settling into the manner of his end.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Pre-dawn cold blue dissolving into wet first light. Mud, broken branches, breath in the air. Loaded objects: spread burka, dagger blades used as shovels, the touched dagger hilt of Hadji Aga…

inner_monologue_converted.original: He fancied that it would be so, and suddenly his soul became serious. He spread out his burka and performed his ablutions.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Silent ablution as the hooves close in — body and soul ordered before the fight; no line spoken.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Well, then, we shall fight like Hamzad, thought Hadji Murad.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: The closing line 'Allah knows. It is well.' delivered after palms drawn down beard — the door shut, the manner of death chosen.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Having understood that he was surrounded ... decided to entrench himself in it and to resist as long as strength and ammunition lasted.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Wordless point at the old ditch; daggers used as shovels; Hadji Murad working in the mud beside his men.

adapted_scenes.transition: The crack of rifles and the thud of Gamzalo's horse hitting the earth carry into the next scene — sound bridge of gunfire across the bog.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. SHRUB-ENTRENCHMENT ABOVE THE RIVER - MORNING
adapted_scenes.action: Spring shrubs crowd a shallow ditch on the slope. Five men kneel behind earth and roots: Hadji Murad sighting his rifle, Gamzalo spitting tobacco, Khan Mahoma humming, Eldar half-rising in tremor, Khanefi seating fresh powder. In the bushes a black mare thrashes, blood blackening the new grass. Distant militiamen advance shrub to shrub, fire badly, fall back. A horseman crests the ridge with two hundred riders behind him. Hadji Aga reins in twenty paces from the ditch and does not dismount; Akhmet Khan settles at his shoulder. Hadji Aga raises his hand, calls across. Hadji Murad lowers the rifle one finger's width and looks up.

dialog_draft.line: Hadji Murad. Come out. The road back is open one hour - no longer.
dialog_draft.subtext: Step into the open so I do not have to climb in after you and see your face.
dialog_draft.action_before: Reins in, does not dismount; thumb finds the dagger hilt.

dialog_draft.line: Hadji Aga. Once you ate from my dish. Today you bring two hundred guns to my ditch.
dialog_draft.subtext: Adat remembers what you have chosen to forget.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lowers rifle a finger's width, looks across.

dialog_draft.line: Eh - Hadji Aga brings two hundred guests. Khanefi, load faster, brother - the wedding starts.
dialog_draft.subtext: We are outnumbered and likely to die; I will not say it.
dialog_draft.action_before: Snaps the breech shut, grins sideways at Khanefi.

dialog_draft.line: Listen - you fired first. By Allah, every man here heard it. The blood from this hour is on your hand, not mine.
dialog_draft.subtext: I need a witness God cannot cross-examine, because my own men remember what we were.
dialog_draft.action_before: Half-turns to Akhmet Khan without looking at the ditch.

dialog_draft.line: Bismillah. Loaded, agha.
dialog_draft.subtext: My hands will not fail you; that is all I have to give.
dialog_draft.action_before: Slaps powder into the pan, passes the gun up.

dialog_draft.line: Swords out, my men! Through the shrubs - do not stop at the first shot. Akhmet, the right. Close the ring, close it!
dialog_draft.subtext: If I stop talking I will have to think whose throat is at the end of this order.
dialog_draft.action_before: Touches dagger hilt; spurs forward two lengths, then checks the horse.

emotional_beats.beat: A former kunak appears at the head of two hundred guns and demands surrender.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Dread layered with moral nausea - a friendship is being executed in public.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad: cold recognition. Hadji Aga: the shame he has converted into haste.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Aga manufactures a pretext ("you fired first") and orders the swords drawn.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The line is crossed; what was negotiation is now slaughter.
emotional_beats.character_feels: The murids settle into the calm of men who have stopped hoping.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Betrayed kinship hardening into the law of the gun.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Spring greens, black blood on grass, sheepskin papakhas dark against shrubs; sunlight half up the trees; the dying mare a constant motion at frame edge.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Aga had once been Hadji Murad's kunak.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: HM's line naming the shared dish; Hadji Aga's refusal to look at the ditch.

inner_monologue_converted.original: He shrieked with joy every time his bullet hit.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Gamzalo spitting, sighting, not turning his head.

adapted_scenes.transition: The crackle of the first volley carries forward; smoke drifts off the shrubs into the next scene's still air.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. SHRUB-ENTRENCHMENT - DITCH AND RIVER LINE - LATER MORNING
adapted_scenes.action: Smoke streaks the shrubs. Three militiamen lie among the new grass; others crawl shrub to shrub. Inside the ditch, Khanefi rams a wrapped bullet home and passes the gun up. Gamzalo glances once at his bleeding mare, settles a pace closer to Hadji Murad. Eldar half-rises with the dagger; Hadji Murad's hand on his shoulder presses him down without a glance. Khan Mahoma's grin is gone; he flattens his voice. Hadji Murad marks one shrub, then the next, with his eyes, then looks up at the sun lifting behind a branch. Khanefi sets the last powder flask in his reach and kneels back to the wall. Hadji Murad draws his palms slowly down his beard.

dialog_draft.line: Hadji Agha. Two hundred. Hadji Aga leads them. Akhmet Khan rides at his shoulder.
dialog_draft.subtext: Old kunak turned dog, and your enemy's son beside him - this is the death they chose for you, not a soldier's death.
dialog_draft.action_before: Spits, sights down the rifle, does not turn his head.

dialog_draft.line: Khanefi - the long gun. Load the short one after. Eldar, sit. A dagger is for the last step, not the first.
dialog_draft.subtext: Buying Eldar's life by holding him still; counting which of his men will not see the river.
dialog_draft.action_before: Voice no louder than the reloading; hand presses Eldar down.

dialog_draft.line: No. We stay. Where you stay, Agha.
dialog_draft.subtext: Do not send me back without you. I will not go.
dialog_draft.action_before: Plants knee against the earth wall.

dialog_draft.line: Akhmet Khan in the bushes, agha. The son. He wants your head more than the Russian coin.
dialog_draft.subtext: This is the man who will not let us out alive.
dialog_draft.action_before: Goes still, peers through the leaves, voice flattens.

dialog_draft.line: Wherever you fall, Hadji Agha. One step before. As I swore.
dialog_draft.subtext: The imam is nothing now, the Russian paper is nothing - only you, only the oath.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lays a hand briefly on Hadji Murad's shoulder, then back to the rifle.

dialog_draft.line: Allah knows the road. Yusuf is in Vedeno. Hadji Murad is here. It is well.
dialog_draft.subtext: Closing the door on his son; accepting that he will not reach him; naming his own death without the word.
dialog_draft.action_before: Draws his palms slowly down his beard; eyes on the sun above the branch.

emotional_beats.beat: The murids close ranks around Hadji Murad and rename their loyalty as oath, not cause.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: A solemn intimacy inside the noise - men choosing the man over the banner.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Eldar, Gamzalo, Khan Mahoma, Khanefi: a fierce tenderness pretending to be discipline.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad lets his son go and accepts that this ditch is the end of the road.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Tragic stillness - a father privately burying a future he will not reach.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad: grief converted into command; the warrior closing the door on the father.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: An oath outlasting its cause; a father's grief swallowed into a soldier's calm.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Tight ditch, powder smoke, blood-streaked grass, sun rising past a single branch; faces grimed; the dying mare at the edge of frame; powder flask passed hand to hand.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad was already thinking of leaping on his horse and trying to make his way to the river.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He marks the shrubs with his eyes, looks up at the sun above the branch - the order given without being given.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Eldar's whole body trembled with impatience to rush dagger in hand at the enemy.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Eldar half-rising; HM's hand pressing him back down without a glance.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Khanefi did the duty of a servant even here.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Khanefi ramming wrapped bullets home, setting the last flask in HM's reach, kneeling back to the wall.

adapted_scenes.transition: The sun clears the branch; HM's drawn-down palms become the cue for the break to the river in the next scene.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. WOODED GULLY NEAR NUKHA - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Powder smoke drifts low over a shallow ditch ringed by leafless shrubs. HADJI MURAD lies wedged behind an earth bank, beshmet torn at the shoulder, cotton wool blackened in the wound. KHANEFI rams a wrapped bullet, hands the rifle across. KHAN MAHOMA sings under his breath, fires, reloads. BATA staggers back from the tethered horses, throat ragged, sits hard in the dirt and spits red. ELDAR rises to his knees, dagger half-drawn, leans out past the bank — a bullet takes him; he reels backwards onto Hadji Murad's leg. Hadji Murad meets the boy's open eyes once, then draws his leg slowly free. Khanefi bends over the body, fingers in the bloodied coat, pulling cartridges. A second bullet strikes Hadji Murad in the side. He folds down into the ditch, plugs the new wound, lifts the rifle again.…

dialog_draft.line: The swords, Agha. Now. Inshallah.
dialog_draft.subtext: I have made my peace. Do not make me outlive you.
dialog_draft.action_before: Resettles his hand on the dagger, eyes fixed on Hadji Murad.

dialog_draft.line: Khanefi. The powder. Keep it dry.
dialog_draft.subtext: I am still your naib; do not look at my wound.
dialog_draft.action_before: Plugs cotton wool into the shoulder, hands the rifle back.

dialog_draft.line: Hadji Agha — your shoulder leaks. Plug it tighter. A bridegroom does not bleed on his own wedding, no?
dialog_draft.subtext: Do not die yet, master — I have nothing left to be if you go.
dialog_draft.action_before: Tears a strip from his own beshmet, throws it across.

dialog_draft.line: Tssh — neck. Just the neck. Bad road today.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is hit and dying and refuses to name it; the road is him.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sits down hard, spits red into the dirt.

dialog_draft.line: Eldar. Rest now. You ran ahead of us all.
dialog_draft.subtext: The boy is dead and I cannot bury him.
dialog_draft.action_before: Draws his leg slowly from beneath the body.

dialog_draft.line: Allah is great. Hadji Murad has said it.
dialog_draft.subtext: The door is closed; I will die standing in my own name.
dialog_draft.action_before: Draws both palms slowly down his beard.

emotional_beats.beat: Eldar's vow becomes his death; his body falls across his master.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Grief held in by the discipline of the fight.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad: the first irreversible loss of the day.

emotional_beats.beat: Bata sits down to die without naming the wound.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The mountains' code made flesh — silence as honor.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Bata: craft to the last; refusal of self-pity.

emotional_beats.beat: Second bullet finds Hadji Murad's side; he plugs it and goes on firing.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The hero is dying and will not show it.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Hadji Murad: closing the door on hope, opening it on his own name.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A dying band keeps faith with itself by working — loading, singing, naming the dead in the same low register as orders.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Low winter sun cutting through powder smoke; bare shrubs as cover; bloodied cotton wool, brass cartridges in a torn coat; horses tethered uphill; mud, frost, papakhas dark with sweat.

inner_monologue_converted.original: Memories and pictures succeeded one another with extraordinary rapidity in his imagination — Abu Nutsal, Vorontsov, Yusuf, Sofiat, Shamil — without evoking any feeling.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Cut entirely from this scene; refused as voice-over per NF-04. The flicker of memory becomes the slow palm-down-the-beard gesture and the closing line in his own name.

inner_monologue_converted.original: This wound in the side was fatal and he felt that he was dying.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: He folds down into the ditch, plugs the wound without looking at it, lifts the rifle one more time.

adapted_scenes.transition: The last shot from the ditch fades into the silence of a cooling teacup — the report has already travelled.

adapted_scenes.slug: INT. VORONTSOV'S STUDY - TIFLIS / EXT. SHAMIL'S COURT - VEDENO / INT. PIT - VEDENO - INTERCUT - EVENING
adapted_scenes.action: TIFLIS: VORONTSOV sets a teacup down without sound. An aide places a folded telegram beside it. He reads, folds it once along its existing crease, lifts a pen, tests the nib against his thumb. — VEDENO: SHAMIL sits on a low cushion, Quran open across his knees. A COURIER kneels, a cloth bundle on the carpet between them. Shamil does not look up. Strokes his beard once. Turns a single page of the Book. — PIT: YUSUF, ribs wrapped in his own arms, presses his ear to the cold stone wall, listening upward into the dark. A bowl of food sits untouched. He turns his face to the wall. Above him a lantern crosses, casts moving bars across the pit floor.

dialog_draft.line: Read it again, mon cher. Slowly. I should like to be quite certain the dispatch says "killed" and not merely "taken."
dialog_draft.subtext: He already heard. He needs the second reading to compose his face before anyone sees it move.
dialog_draft.action_before: Sets the teacup down without sound.

dialog_draft.line: We shall write that he fell bravely, surrounded, refusing surrender. That much, at least, is true — and it costs the Empire nothing to say so.
dialog_draft.subtext: Begins drafting the official lie before the body is cold; civility as the polite face of erasure.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lifts the pen, tests the nib against his thumb.

dialog_draft.line: So. The wolf is meat. Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un.
dialog_draft.subtext: The threat to my Imamate has erased itself, and Allah signs the receipt.
dialog_draft.action_before: Strokes his beard once, slowly.

dialog_draft.line: His mother remains. His wife remains. His sons remain. Treat them as guests of the Imam. So.
dialog_draft.subtext: They live as my warning to the next ambitious naib; mercy here is a longer blade.
dialog_draft.action_before: Turns a single page of the Book.

dialog_draft.line: My father — he is coming. The stones will know his horse before the dogs above know their own names.
dialog_draft.subtext: He is begging the dark to keep the promise his father has not yet kept.
dialog_draft.action_before: Presses ear to the pit wall, listening upward.

dialog_draft.line: Mother — if she asks — say I stood. Say it even if —
dialog_draft.subtext: He has just understood, for one second, that he may not see her again.
dialog_draft.action_before: Turns his face to the wall so the guard cannot see.

emotional_beats.beat: Vorontsov mourns a lost trophy and drafts the official version in the same breath.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The chill of empire's politeness — affection and erasure in one gesture.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Vorontsov: a real, small wound he will not name; relief he will not name either.

emotional_beats.beat: Shamil receives the news and converts it instantly into doctrine, then into hostage policy.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: The two empires mirror each other in their composure.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Shamil: cold satisfaction sealed by a turned page.

emotional_beats.beat: Yusuf, still waiting, speaks the eulogy he does not yet know is needed.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Tragic irony — the son's hope outliving the father by a beat.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Yusuf: a child's faith the film will not let him keep.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: Three rooms close around a body none of them will see — the same death received three different ways, each a verdict on the world that produced it.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Tiflis: lamp-lit study, folded telegram, French wallpaper. Vedeno: low cushion, Quran, cloth bundle on carpet, no jewelry. Pit: stone walls slick with damp, lantern bars, untouched bowl. Three…

inner_monologue_converted.original: Hadji Murad's dying montage of remembered faces (Abu Nutsal, Vorontsov, Yusuf, Sofiat, Shamil) without feeling.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: The montage is externalized: the three surviving witnesses (Vorontsov, Shamil, Yusuf) each receive the death in their own room. The flat affect of the source becomes the flat affect of empire and doctrine.

adapted_scenes.transition: Yusuf's turned face on stone rhymes with Vorontsov's folded telegram and Shamil's turned page — three closures in the same image.

adapted_scenes.slug: EXT. RAVINE / THORN-BRUSH SLOPE - CAUCASUS - DAY
adapted_scenes.action: Powder-smoke drifts over a shallow ditch. Hadji Murad, head shaven and bleeding, drags himself from behind the bank. He fires his pistol; a militiaman running toward him drops. Hadji Murad climbs out of the ditch and limps forward, dagger in hand, straight at the foe. Several rifles crack. He reels, falls. Militiamen rush in with triumphant shrieks, then stop short: the body moves. The shaven head lifts, hands find the trunk of a young tree, the torso pushes up against the bark. Gamzalo, wrists bound at the bank's edge, jerks against his ropes. Khanefi, blood on his sleeve, sets his back to Gamzalo's. A shudder passes through Hadji Murad. He staggers from the tree and falls full-length on his face. He does not move again.

dialog_draft.line: Cut my hands loose. One rifle. It is enough.
dialog_draft.subtext: Do not let me die kneeling. Let me die owing nothing.
dialog_draft.action_before: Tests the rope at his wrists with a slow pull.

dialog_draft.line: Bismillah. Agha — rise. The bay still stands.
dialog_draft.subtext: I will not let you die alone; if you mount, I die at your stirrup.
dialog_draft.action_before: Wipes blood from his palm onto his sleeve.

dialog_draft.line: Yusuf...
dialog_draft.subtext: The deepest wound named once; the brevity is the grief and the farewell he could never speak aloud.
dialog_draft.action_before: Bleeding head lifts; eyes find no horizon.

dialog_draft.line: A horse. A black one.
dialog_draft.subtext: The promised gift to his son becomes the only inheritance left; love delivered in concrete things, never in words.
dialog_draft.action_before: Lips move against the bark.

dialog_draft.line: Hadji Murad has stood.
dialog_draft.subtext: Third-person oath-register: the name still belongs to him, the body does not.
dialog_draft.action_before: Body shudders; pushes once from the trunk.

dialog_draft.line: Allah knows.
dialog_draft.subtext: The closing door. The matter ends with this sentence.
dialog_draft.action_before: Palms try to draw down the beard; fall.

emotional_beats.beat: Hadji Murad rises from the ditch and walks at the foe with a dagger.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Terror tipping into awe — a man choosing the manner of his end.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Past pity, past anger; only the body finishing what the will began.

emotional_beats.beat: Shot down, he rises again at the tree; the militiamen halt.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: Dread that the body will not consent to die.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Honour outlasting flesh; the name still his, the body no longer.

emotional_beats.beat: He falls full-length, mown like a thistle. Stillness.
emotional_beats.audience_feels: A grief without consolation.
emotional_beats.character_feels: Last consciousness; nothing more.

adapted_scenes.emotional_core: A man dies on his own terms while the empire watches.
adapted_scenes.visual_note: Trampled thorn-brush; smoke ribboning low; shaven crown above bloodied collar; young tree-trunk as last upright; dagger gripped white-knuckled.

inner_monologue_converted.original: All these images passed through his mind without evoking any feeling — everything seemed insignificant in comparison with what was beginning within him.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Tempo barely changes between living and dying; he limps forward at the foe with the same measured weight he carried into Russian salons.

inner_monologue_converted.original: He did not move, but still he felt.
inner_monologue_converted.converted_to: Body fallen, hand still curled around the dagger hilt; one breath visible in the dust before the militiamen close in.

adapted_scenes.transition: The shudder of the falling body carries into the heavy footfall of Hadji Aga stepping over him.
